title: The Old Man and the Pigeons
text_zh: 每天早上六点半，公园里有一个老人。他总是带一个白色的袋子，里面是面包。他把面包放在地上，然后坐在椅子上看报纸。鸽子慢慢地飞过来，越来越多。老人从来不看它们，但是他知道每一只鸽子什么时候来。有一天早上下雨了，老人还是来了。鸽子也来了。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn bàn, gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yí gè lǎorén. Tā zǒngshì dài yí gè báisè de dàizi, lǐmiàn shì miànbāo. Tā bǎ miànbāo fàng zài dìshang, ránhòu zuò zài yǐzi shàng kàn bàozhǐ. Gēzi mànmàn de fēi guòlái, yuè lái yuè duō. Lǎorén cónglái bú kàn tāmen, dànshì tā zhīdào měi yì zhī gēzi shénme shíhòu lái. Yǒu yì tiān zǎoshang xià yǔ le, lǎorén háishi lái le. Gēzi yě lái le.
text_en: Every morning at six-thirty, there is an old man in the park. He always brings a white bag with bread inside. He puts the bread on the ground, then sits on a chair reading the newspaper. The pigeons slowly fly over, more and more of them. The old man never looks at them, but he knows when each pigeon arrives. One morning it rained, and the old man still came. The pigeons came too.

questions.q_zh: 这段话说明了什么道理？
questions.q_en: What principle does this passage illustrate?

options.text: 简单的生活也能很快乐
options.pinyin: jiǎndān de shēnghuó yě néng hěn kuàilè
options.text_en: a simple life can also be happy

options.pinyin: yí gè rén bù néng kuàilè

questions.explanation: The old man's daily routine of feeding pigeons, reading his paper, and coming even in the rain shows contentment in simple, repeated acts — no wealth or complexity needed.

questions.q_zh: 读完这段话，你觉得作者想说什么？
questions.q_en: After reading this, what do you think the author wants to say?

options.text: 平凡的日子也值得珍惜
options.pinyin: píngfán de rìzi yě zhíde zhēnxī

options.text_en: should move to a big city

questions.explanation: The old man repeats the same quiet ritual every morning, rain or shine, and even knows each pigeon's schedule — this deep attention to an ordinary routine shows ordinary days carry real value.

title: Rain on the Window
text_zh: 下雨的时候，我喜欢坐在窗户旁边。雨水从上面慢慢地流下来，有的快，有的慢。我发现大的雨滴总是比小的快。有时候两个雨滴会在中间碰到一起，变成一个更大的。外面的街上，人们走得很快，但是有一个女人站在那里，没有伞，看着天空笑。她可能觉得雨很舒服。
text_pinyin: Xià yǔ de shíhòu, wǒ xǐhuān zuò zài chuānghù pángbiān. Yǔshuǐ cóng shàngmiàn mànmàn de liú xiàlái, yǒude kuài, yǒude màn. Wǒ fāxiàn dà de yǔdī zǒngshì bǐ xiǎo de kuài. Yǒu shíhòu liǎng gè yǔdī huì zài zhōngjiān pèng dào yìqǐ, biànchéng yí gè gèng dà de. Wàimiàn de jiē shàng, rénmen zǒu de hěn kuài, dànshì yǒu yí gè nǚrén zhàn zài nàlǐ, méiyǒu sǎn, kànzhe tiānkōng xiào. Tā kěnéng juéde yǔ hěn shūfú.
text_en: When it rains, I like to sit by the window. The rainwater slowly flows down from above, some fast, some slow. I discovered that big raindrops are always faster than small ones. Sometimes two raindrops meet in the middle and become one bigger one. On the street outside, people walk quickly, but there is a woman standing there, without an umbrella, looking at the sky and smiling. She probably thinks the rain feels nice.

questions.q_zh: 作者发现了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the author discover?

options.pinyin: xiǎo yǔdī bǐ dà yǔdī kuài
options.text_en: small raindrops are faster than big ones

options.text: 大雨滴总是比小雨滴快
options.pinyin: dà yǔdī zǒng shì bǐ xiǎo yǔdī kuài
options.text_en: big raindrops are always faster than small ones
all raindrops are the same speed

options.pinyin: yǔdī bú huì pèng dào yīqǐ

questions.explanation: 我发现大的雨滴总是比小的快 directly states the observation — big raindrops always move faster than small ones.

questions.q_zh: 那个女人为什么没有伞？
questions.q_en: Why does the woman have no umbrella?

options.pinyin: tā kěnéng juéde yǔ hěn shūfu
options.text_en: she probably finds the rain comfortable
she just came out of a shop

questions.explanation: She stands in the rain without an umbrella, 看着天空笑 (looking at the sky and smiling) — her enjoyment suggests she finds the rain 很舒服 (comfortable).

title: The Locksmith's Hands
text_zh: 我家楼下有一个修锁的老师傅。他的手很大，上面有很多小的伤。他工作的时候非常仔细，眼睛一直看着锁里面。有人问他：这个锁能修好吗？他总是先不说话，用手摸一摸，然后才回答。他说每个锁都不一样，要先听它的声音。我觉得他不只是在修锁，他是在和锁说话。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā lóu xià yǒu yí gè xiū suǒ de lǎo shīfù. Tā de shǒu hěn dà, shàngmiàn yǒu hěn duō xiǎo de shāng. Tā gōngzuò de shíhòu fēicháng zǐxì, yǎnjīng yìzhí kànzhe suǒ lǐmiàn. Yǒu rén wèn tā: zhège suǒ néng xiū hǎo ma? Tā zǒngshì xiān bù shuōhuà, yòng shǒu mō yi mō, ránhòu cái huídá. Tā shuō měi gè suǒ dōu bù yíyàng, yào xiān tīng tā de shēngyīn. Wǒ juéde tā bù zhǐshì zài xiū suǒ, tā shì zài hé suǒ shuōhuà.
text_en: Downstairs from my apartment there is an old master locksmith. His hands are big, with many small scars on them. When he works he is extremely careful, his eyes always looking inside the lock. When someone asks him: can this lock be fixed? He always says nothing first, feels it with his hands, and only then answers. He says every lock is different — you have to listen to its sound first. I think he is not just fixing locks; he is talking to them.

questions.q_zh: 老师傅工作的时候怎么样？
questions.q_en: How is the old master when he works?
questions.explanation: 他工作的时候非常仔细，眼睛一直看着锁里面 — 'extremely careful, eyes always on the lock' directly describes his working style.

questions.q_zh: 故事里最重要的不是发生了什么，而是什么？
questions.q_en: The most important thing in this story is not what happened, but what?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「人物的心情和想法」, indicating the character's mood and thoughts.

title: Autumn Leaves Know When
text_zh: 我每天经过同一条路去上班。九月的时候，路两边的树还是绿色的。十月，有几棵树开始变黄了，但不是所有的。我发现靠近路灯的树总是最后变颜色。可能因为晚上也有光，它们觉得夏天还没有结束。我跟同事说了这个想法，他说我想得太多了。但是第二年，我又看了一次，还是一样的。
text_pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān jīngguò tóng yì tiáo lù qù shàngbān. Jiǔ yuè de shíhòu, lù liǎng biān de shù háishi lǜsè de. Shí yuè, yǒu jǐ kē shù kāishǐ biàn huáng le, dàn bú shì suǒyǒu de. Wǒ fāxiàn kào jìn lùdēng de shù zǒngshì zuìhòu biàn yánsè. Kěnéng yīnwèi wǎnshàng yě yǒu guāng, tāmen juéde xiàtiān hái méiyǒu jiéshù. Wǒ gēn tóngshì shuō le zhège xiǎngfǎ, tā shuō wǒ xiǎng de tài duō le. Dànshì dì èr nián, wǒ yòu kàn le yí cì, háishi yíyàng de.
text_en: I pass the same road every day going to work. In September, the trees on both sides of the road are still green. In October, a few trees start turning yellow, but not all of them. I discovered that the trees near streetlights are always the last to change color. Maybe because there is light at night too, they think summer hasn't ended yet. I told my colleague this idea, and he said I think too much. But the next year, I checked again — it was still the same.

questions.q_zh: 读完这段话，你觉得作者想说什么？
questions.q_en: After reading this, what do you think the author wants to say?

options.text: 平凡的日子也值得珍惜
options.pinyin: píngfán de rìzi yě zhíde zhēnxī

options.text_en: should move to a big city

questions.explanation: The narrator notices the same road daily, tracks which trees change color first, tests a theory across two years — this sustained attention to mundane details shows ordinary days are worth close observation and cherishing.

questions.q_zh: 同事怎么看他的想法？
questions.q_en: What did the colleague think of his idea?

options.pinyin: juéde tā xiǎng de tài duō le
yě qù kàn le nàxiē shù
options.text_en: also went to look at those trees

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「觉得他想得太多了」, indicating thought he was overthinking.

questions.q_zh: 第二年他发现了什么？
questions.q_en: What did he find the second year?

options.pinyin: suǒyǒu shù yīqǐ biàn sè le
options.text_en: all the trees changed color together

options.pinyin: lùdēng de shù xiān biàn le
options.text_en: the trees near streetlights changed first

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「跟去年一样」, indicating same as last year.

title: The Sound of the Neighborhood Waking Up
text_zh: 我住的小区每天早上都有一样的声音。五点半，第一个声音是楼下老太太开门的声音。然后是她的拖鞋在楼梯上的声音。六点，有人开始做早饭，能闻到油的味道。六点半，孩子们的声音从楼上传下来。七点，所有的车开始动了。我不用看钟，听声音就知道几点了。周末的时候，这些声音都晚一个小时。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù de xiǎoqū měi tiān zǎoshang dōu yǒu yíyàng de shēngyīn. Wǔ diǎn bàn, dì yí gè shēngyīn shì lóu xià lǎo tàitài kāi mén de shēngyīn. Ránhòu shì tā de tuōxié zài lóutī shàng de shēngyīn. Liù diǎn, yǒu rén kāishǐ zuò zǎofàn, néng wén dào yóu de wèidào. Liù diǎn bàn, háizimen de shēngyīn cóng lóu shàng chuán xiàlái. Qī diǎn, suǒyǒu de chē kāishǐ dòng le. Wǒ bú yòng kàn zhōng, tīng shēngyīn jiù zhīdào jǐ diǎn le. Zhōumò de shíhòu, zhèxiē shēngyīn dōu wǎn yí gè xiǎoshí.
text_en: The neighborhood where I live has the same sounds every morning. At five-thirty, the first sound is the old lady downstairs opening her door. Then it's the sound of her slippers on the stairs. At six, someone starts making breakfast — you can smell the oil. At six-thirty, children's voices come down from upstairs. At seven, all the cars start moving. I don't need to look at the clock; I can tell the time by listening. On weekends, all these sounds come one hour later.

questions.q_zh: 故事里最重要的不是发生了什么，而是什么？
questions.q_en: The most important thing in this story is not what happened, but what?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「人物的心情和想法」, indicating the character's mood and thoughts.

questions.q_zh: 周末有什么不同？
questions.q_en: What's different on weekends?

options.pinyin: shēngyīn dōu wǎn yī ge xiǎoshí
options.text_en: all sounds are an hour late

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「声音都晚一个小时」, indicating all sounds are an hour late.

title: The Cat at the Fish Shop
text_zh: 菜市场旁边有一家卖鱼的小店。店门口总是坐着一只胖猫。很多人觉得这只猫一定经常吃鱼。其实不是。我仔细看过很多次，老板从来不给猫鱼吃。猫也从来不去拿鱼。它只是喜欢坐在那里，因为那个地方冬天暖和，夏天有风。猫选择地方住，比我们聪明。
text_pinyin: Cài shìchǎng pángbiān yǒu yì jiā mài yú de xiǎo diàn. Diàn ménkǒu zǒngshì zuòzhe yì zhī pàng māo. Hěn duō rén juéde zhè zhī māo yídìng jīngcháng chī yú. Qíshí bú shì. Wǒ zǐxì kàn guò hěn duō cì, lǎobǎn cónglái bù gěi māo yú chī. Māo yě cónglái bú qù ná yú. Tā zhǐshì xǐhuān zuò zài nàlǐ, yīnwèi nàge dìfāng dōngtiān nuǎnhuo, xiàtiān yǒu fēng. Māo xuǎnzé dìfāng zhù, bǐ wǒmen cōngmíng.
text_en: Next to the wet market there is a small fish shop. A fat cat always sits at the shop entrance. Many people think this cat must eat fish all the time. No. I've watched carefully many times — the boss never gives the cat fish. The cat never goes to take fish either. It just likes sitting there because that spot is warm in winter and breezy in summer. Cats are smarter than us at choosing where to live.

questions.q_zh: 猫为什么坐在鱼店门口？
questions.q_en: Why does the cat sit at the fish shop entrance?

options.text: 因为那个地方冬暖夏凉
options.pinyin: yīnwèi nàge dìfāng dōng nuǎn xià liáng
options.text_en: because that spot is warm in winter and cool in summer
because the cat is waiting for someone

questions.explanation: 因为 (because) directly states the reason: because that spot is warm in winter and cool in summer.

questions.q_zh: 老板给猫鱼吃吗？
questions.q_en: Does the boss give the cat fish?
questions.explanation: Look for 从来不 (never) in the text — it tells you this has never happened.

title: The Woman Who Waters at Dusk
text_zh: 每天傍晚六点，对面楼上有一个女人出来浇花。她的阳台上有很多花，红的、白的、紫色的。她浇花的时候很慢，一盆一盆地浇。有时候她停下来，看一看远处的天空。我觉得她不只是在浇花，她是在等天黑。浇完花以后，她会在阳台上坐几分钟，然后才进去。那几分钟，整个城市都是安静的。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān bàngwǎn liù diǎn, duìmiàn lóu shàng yǒu yí gè nǚrén chūlái jiāo huā. Tā de yángtái shàng yǒu hěn duō huā, hóng de, bái de, zǐsè de. Tā jiāo huā de shíhòu hěn màn, yì pén yì pén de jiāo. Yǒu shíhòu tā tíng xiàlái, kàn yi kàn yuǎnchù de tiānkōng. Wǒ juéde tā bù zhǐshì zài jiāo huā, tā shì zài děng tiān hēi. Jiāo wán huā yǐhòu, tā huì zài yángtái shàng zuò jǐ fēnzhōng, ránhòu cái jìnqù. Nà jǐ fēnzhōng, zhěnggè chéngshì dōu shì ānjìng de.
text_en: Every evening at six, a woman on the building across comes out to water her flowers. Her balcony has many flowers — red, white, purple. She waters them slowly, pot by pot. Sometimes she pauses and looks at the distant sky. I think she's not just watering flowers; she's waiting for nightfall. After finishing, she sits on the balcony for a few minutes before going inside. In those few minutes, the whole city is quiet.

questions.q_zh: 这段话用了什么方式让读者理解人物？
questions.q_en: How does the passage help the reader understand the character?

options.pinyin: tōngguò miáoxiě tā zuò de shì

options.text: 直接说他/她是什么样的人
options.pinyin: zhíjiē shuō tā shì shénme yàng de rén
options.text_en: by directly stating what kind of person they are

questions.explanation: The passage describes every evening at six, a woman on the building across comes out to water her flowers, showing how.

questions.q_zh: 读完这段话，你觉得作者想说什么？
questions.q_en: After reading this, what do you think the author wants to say?

options.text: 平凡的日子也值得珍惜
options.pinyin: píngfán de rìzi yě zhíde zhēnxī

options.text_en: should move to a big city

questions.explanation: The woman waters each pot slowly, pauses to watch the sky, and sits quietly after finishing — these unhurried daily rituals show that ordinary moments, repeated with attention, hold real value.

title: Morning Tai Chi
text_zh: 公园里每天早上有一群人打太极。他们动作很慢，好像时间也变慢了。最前面的那个老人，穿白色的衣服，他的动作最好看。我站在旁边看了很久。我发现他们的脚从来不离开地面，只是慢慢地移动。有一个年轻人动作太快了，旁边的阿姨小声跟他说：慢一点，像水一样。年轻人的脸红了，但是他慢下来了。
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ měi tiān zǎoshang yǒu yì qún rén dǎ tàijí. Tāmen dòngzuò hěn màn, hǎoxiàng shíjiān yě biàn màn le. Zuì qiánmiàn de nàge lǎorén, chuān báisè de yīfú, tā de dòngzuò zuì hǎokàn. Wǒ zhàn zài pángbiān kàn le hěn jiǔ. Wǒ fāxiàn tāmen de jiǎo cónglái bù líkāi dìmiàn, zhǐshì mànmàn de yídòng. Yǒu yí gè niánqīng rén dòngzuò tài kuài le, pángbiān de āyí xiǎoshēng gēn tā shuō: màn yìdiǎn, xiàng shuǐ yíyàng. Niánqīng rén de liǎn hóng le, dànshì tā màn xiàlái le.
text_en: Every morning in the park, a group of people do tai chi. Their movements are slow, as if time has slowed down too. The old man at the front, wearing white clothes, has the most beautiful movements. I stood nearby watching for a long time. I noticed their feet never leave the ground — they just move slowly. One young man moved too fast, and the auntie beside him whispered: slower, like water. The young man blushed, but he slowed down.

questions.q_zh: 老人为什么喜欢这个地方？
questions.q_en: Why might the old man like this place?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi zhèlǐ yǒu hěn duō rén
options.text_en: because there are many people here

questions.explanation: 因为 (because) directly states the reason: because it's quiet here.

questions.q_zh: 阿姨跟年轻人说什么？
questions.q_en: What did the auntie say to the young man?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「慢一点，像水一样」, indicating a little slower, like water.

title: The Bench Nobody Sits On
text_zh: 公园里有很多椅子，但是有一把椅子从来没有人坐。它在两棵大树中间，看起来很好。我问旁边卖水的阿姨，她说：那把椅子下午两点以后太阳会晒到，大家都知道。我下午两点去看了一下，真的，只有那一把椅子在太阳下面。所有的事情都有原因，只是你可能还不知道。
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu hěn duō yǐzi, dànshì yǒu yì bǎ yǐzi cónglái méiyǒu rén zuò. Tā zài liǎng kē dà shù zhōngjiān, kàn qǐlái hěn hǎo. Wǒ wèn pángbiān mài shuǐ de āyí, tā shuō: nà bǎ yǐzi xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn yǐhòu tàiyáng huì shài dào, dàjiā dōu zhīdào. Wǒ xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn qù kàn le yíxià, zhēn de, zhǐyǒu nà yì bǎ yǐzi zài tàiyáng xiàmiàn. Suǒyǒu de shìqíng dōu yǒu yuányīn, zhǐshì nǐ kěnéng hái bù zhīdào.
text_en: The park has many benches, but one bench is never occupied. It's between two big trees and looks perfectly fine. I asked the auntie selling water nearby, and she said: that bench gets sun after two in the afternoon — everyone knows. I went to check at two in the afternoon, and sure enough, only that one bench was in the sun. Everything has a reason; you just might not know it yet.

questions.q_zh: 为什么没有人坐那把椅子？
questions.q_en: Why does nobody sit on that bench?

options.text_en: it gets sun in the afternoon

questions.explanation: 卖水的阿姨 explains that 那把椅子下午两点以后太阳会晒到 — the bench gets direct sunlight after 2 PM, so everyone avoids it.

questions.q_zh: 谁告诉作者原因的？
questions.q_en: Who told the author the reason?
questions.explanation: 「卖水的阿姨」 tells you who — the water-selling auntie nearby provided the explanation.

title: The Last Light
text_zh: 我的办公室在二十楼。每天下班的时候，我会在窗户前站一会儿。太阳下山的时候，最后的光总是先照到最高的楼。然后慢慢地，从上到下，所有的楼都变暗了。最后变暗的是河边那座老教堂的顶。我数过，从第一座楼变暗到最后，大概要十二分钟。每天都差不多，但是冬天比夏天快一点。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de bàngōngshì zài èrshí lóu. Měi tiān xiàbān de shíhòu, wǒ huì zài chuānghù qián zhàn yíhuìr. Tàiyáng xià shān de shíhòu, zuìhòu de guāng zǒngshì xiān zhào dào zuì gāo de lóu. Ránhòu mànmàn de, cóng shàng dào xià, suǒyǒu de lóu dōu biàn àn le. Zuìhòu biàn àn de shì hé biān nà zuò lǎo jiàotáng de dǐng. Wǒ shǔ guò, cóng dì yí zuò lóu biàn àn dào zuìhòu, dàgài yào shí'èr fēnzhōng. Měi tiān dōu chàbuduō, dànshì dōngtiān bǐ xiàtiān kuài yìdiǎn.
text_en: My office is on the twentieth floor. Every day when I leave work, I stand by the window for a while. When the sun sets, the last light always hits the tallest buildings first. Then slowly, from top to bottom, all the buildings go dark. The last to go dark is the top of the old church by the river. I've counted — from the first building going dark to the last takes about twelve minutes. It's about the same every day, but a little faster in winter than summer.

questions.q_zh: 最后变暗的是什么？
questions.q_en: What goes dark last?

options.pinyin: hé biān lǎo jiàotáng de dǐng
options.text_en: the top of the old church by the river

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「河边老教堂的顶」, indicating the top of the old church by the river.

questions.q_zh: 从第一座楼变暗到最后要多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long from the first building going dark to the last?
questions.explanation: 「大概十二分钟」 describes the manner — about twelve minutes.

questions.q_zh: 冬天和夏天有什么不同？
questions.q_en: What's different between winter and summer?

options.text_en: a bit faster in winter than summer

questions.explanation: 差不多 (about the same) tells you things are similar — a bit faster in winter than summer.

title: The Street Sweeper's Route
text_zh: 我家门前的清洁工是一个五十多岁的男人。他每天早上四点就开始扫地。我发现他总是从东边开始，往西边扫。我问他为什么，他说：因为早上的风从东边来，这样扫的时候风帮我。他做这个工作已经做了十五年了。十五年里他发现了很多别人不知道的事情，比如哪棵树秋天掉叶子最多，哪个垃圾桶总是最先满。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā mén qián de qīngjiégōng shì yí gè wǔshí duō suì de nánrén. Tā měi tiān zǎoshang sì diǎn jiù kāishǐ sǎo dì. Wǒ fāxiàn tā zǒngshì cóng dōngbiān kāishǐ, wǎng xībiān sǎo. Wǒ wèn tā wèishénme, tā shuō: yīnwèi zǎoshang de fēng cóng dōngbiān lái, zhèyàng sǎo de shíhòu fēng bāng wǒ. Tā zuò zhège gōngzuò yǐjīng zuò le shíwǔ nián le. Shíwǔ nián lǐ tā fāxiàn le hěn duō biérén bù zhīdào de shìqíng, bǐrú nǎ kē shù qiūtiān diào yèzi zuì duō, nǎge lājītǒng zǒngshì zuì xiān mǎn.
text_en: The street sweeper outside my home is a man in his fifties. He starts sweeping at four every morning. I noticed he always starts from the east and sweeps westward. I asked him why, and he said: because the morning wind comes from the east, so the wind helps me when I sweep this way. He has been doing this job for fifteen years. In fifteen years he has discovered many things others don't know — like which tree drops the most leaves in autumn, and which trash can always fills up first.

questions.q_zh: 清洁工为什么从东边开始扫？
questions.q_en: Why does the sweeper start from the east?

options.text_en: because the east side is dirtier

options.text: 因为风从东边来，可以帮他
options.pinyin: yīnwèi fēng cóng dōngbiān lái, kěyǐ bāng tā
options.text_en: because the wind comes from the east and helps him
because he lives on the east side

questions.explanation: He explains 因为早上的风从东边来 — the morning wind blows from the east, so sweeping eastward means the wind pushes debris in his direction of travel.

questions.q_zh: 他做这个工作多长时间了？
questions.q_en: How long has he been doing this job?
questions.explanation: 他做这个工作已经做了十五年了 states the duration directly — fifteen years of sweeping the same streets.

title: Two Cups of Tea
text_zh: 楼下的茶馆里，每天下午有两个老人坐在同一张桌子旁边。一个喝绿茶，一个喝红茶。他们很少说话，偶尔看一看对方，点一下头。有一天只有喝绿茶的老人来了。茶馆的服务员还是放了两杯茶在桌上。我问服务员为什么，她说：他们已经这样喝了八年了，少一杯茶不对。第二天，两个人都来了。
text_pinyin: Lóu xià de cháguǎn lǐ, měi tiān xiàwǔ yǒu liǎng gè lǎorén zuò zài tóng yì zhāng zhuōzi pángbiān. Yí gè hē lǜchá, yí gè hē hóngchá. Tāmen hěn shǎo shuōhuà, ǒu'ěr kàn yi kàn duìfāng, diǎn yíxià tóu. Yǒu yì tiān zhǐyǒu hē lǜchá de lǎorén lái le. Cháguǎn de fúwùyuán háishi fàng le liǎng bēi chá zài zhuō shàng. Wǒ wèn fúwùyuán wèishénme, tā shuō: tāmen yǐjīng zhèyàng hē le bā nián le, shǎo yì bēi chá bú duì. Dì èr tiān, liǎng gè rén dōu lái le.
text_en: In the teahouse downstairs, every afternoon two old men sit at the same table. One drinks green tea, one drinks black tea. They rarely talk, occasionally glancing at each other and nodding. One day only the green tea drinker came. The teahouse server still placed two cups of tea on the table. I asked the server why, and she said: they've been drinking like this for eight years — one fewer cup wouldn't be right. The next day, both came.

questions.q_zh: 两个老人经常说话吗？
questions.q_en: Do the two old men talk often?
questions.explanation: Look for 「很少说话」 — it confirms the answer: rarely talking.

questions.q_zh: 为什么服务员还是放了两杯茶？
questions.q_en: Why did the server still put two cups of tea?

options.text: 她不知道另一个人没来
options.pinyin: tā bù zhīdào lìng yī ge rén méi lái
options.text_en: she didn't know the other person wasn't coming

options.pinyin: yīnwèi shǎo yī bēi bú duì
options.text_en: because one fewer cup wouldn't be right

options.text: 另一个人打电话说要来
options.pinyin: lìng yī ge rén dǎ diànhuà shuō yào lái
options.text_en: the other person called and said they'd come

questions.explanation: 因为 (because) directly states the reason: because one fewer cup wouldn't be right.

title: Shadows at Noon
text_zh: 中午十二点的时候，影子最短。我在广场上发现了一个有意思的事情：中午的时候，一个很高的人和一个很矮的人的影子差不多一样长。早上和下午就不一样了，高的人的影子比矮的人长很多。所以，如果你不想让别人知道你有多高，中午出门最好。我跟我的朋友说了这个发现，他说：你是不是太闲了？
text_pinyin: Zhōngwǔ shí'èr diǎn de shíhòu, yǐngzi zuì duǎn. Wǒ zài guǎngchǎng shàng fāxiàn le yí gè yǒu yìsi de shìqíng: zhōngwǔ de shíhòu, yí gè hěn gāo de rén hé yí gè hěn ǎi de rén de yǐngzi chàbuduō yíyàng cháng. Zǎoshang hé xiàwǔ jiù bù yíyàng le, gāo de rén de yǐngzi bǐ ǎi de rén cháng hěn duō. Suǒyǐ, rúguǒ nǐ bù xiǎng ràng biérén zhīdào nǐ yǒu duō gāo, zhōngwǔ chūmén zuì hǎo. Wǒ gēn wǒ de péngyǒu shuō le zhège fāxiàn, tā shuō: nǐ shì bu shì tài xián le?
text_en: At noon, shadows are shortest. I discovered something interesting in the square: at noon, a tall person and a short person have shadows that are almost the same length. In the morning and afternoon it's different — the tall person's shadow is much longer than the short person's. So if you don't want others to know how tall you are, it's best to go out at noon. I told my friend about this discovery, and he said: do you have too much free time?

questions.q_zh: 中午的时候，高人和矮人的影子怎么样？
questions.q_en: At noon, how are the tall and short person's shadows?

options.pinyin: gāo rén de yǐngzi cháng hěn duō
options.text_en: tall people's shadows are much longer

options.pinyin: ǎi rén de yǐngzi gèng cháng

questions.explanation: The passage shows this through: i discovered something interesting in the square: at noon.

questions.q_zh: 朋友听了以后说什么？
questions.q_en: What did the friend say after hearing this?

options.pinyin: nǐ shì bu shì tài xián le
options.text_en: don't you have too much free time

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「你是不是太闲了」, indicating don't you have too much free time.

title: Spring in a Puddle
text_zh: 昨天下了一场大雨，路上有很多水洼。今天早上出门的时候，我看到一个水洼里有一朵粉色的花。花是从旁边的树上掉下来的。水洼像一面小镜子，花的倒影让这个普通的水洼变得特别好看。一个小女孩走过来，蹲下看了很久。她妈妈说：快走，上学要迟到了。女孩说：妈妈你看，水里也有春天。
text_pinyin: Zuótiān xià le yì chǎng dà yǔ, lù shàng yǒu hěn duō shuǐwā. Jīntiān zǎoshang chūmén de shíhòu, wǒ kàn dào yí gè shuǐwā lǐ yǒu yì duǒ fěnsè de huā. Huā shì cóng pángbiān de shù shàng diào xiàlái de. Shuǐwā xiàng yí miàn xiǎo jìngzi, huā de dàoyǐng ràng zhège pǔtōng de shuǐwā biàn de tèbié hǎokàn. Yí gè xiǎo nǚhái zǒu guòlái, dūn xià kàn le hěn jiǔ. Tā māmā shuō: kuài zǒu, shàngxué yào chídào le. Nǚhái shuō: māmā nǐ kàn, shuǐ lǐ yě yǒu chūntiān.
text_en: Yesterday there was heavy rain, and today the road has many puddles. When I went out this morning, I saw a pink flower in one puddle. The flower had fallen from a nearby tree. The puddle was like a small mirror, and the flower's reflection made this ordinary puddle look especially beautiful. A little girl walked over and crouched down looking at it for a long time. Her mother said: hurry up, you'll be late for school. The girl said: mama look, there's spring in the water too.

questions.q_zh: 故事里最重要的不是发生了什么，而是什么？
questions.q_en: The most important thing in this story is not what happened, but what?
questions.explanation: The factual events (rain, puddle, flower) matter less than the girl's perception — she sees 水里也有春天 (spring in the water), revealing her imaginative inner world.

questions.q_zh: 小女孩说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the little girl say?

options.pinyin: wǒ xiǎng yào nà duǒ huā

options.text_en: there's spring in the water too
I don't want to go to school

questions.explanation: The girl tells her mother 妈妈你看，水里也有春天 — 'mama look, there's spring in the water too,' seeing the reflected flower as a sign of spring.

title: The Bicycle Repairman's Radio
text_zh: 路口有一个修自行车的师傅。他旁边总是放着一个旧收音机，声音不大。他修车的时候听新闻，休息的时候听音乐。我问他为什么不用手机听，他说手机要用手拿，修车的时候不方便。收音机放在那里就行了。他说得对，有时候旧的东西比新的更方便。他用这个收音机已经用了二十年了。
text_pinyin: Lùkǒu yǒu yí gè xiū zìxíngchē de shīfù. Tā pángbiān zǒngshì fàngzhe yí gè jiù shōuyīnjī, shēngyīn bú dà. Tā xiū chē de shíhòu tīng xīnwén, xiūxí de shíhòu tīng yīnyuè. Wǒ wèn tā wèishénme bú yòng shǒujī tīng, tā shuō shǒujī yào yòng shǒu ná, xiū chē de shíhòu bù fāngbiàn. Shōuyīnjī fàng zài nàlǐ jiù xíng le. Tā shuō de duì, yǒu shíhòu jiù de dōngxī bǐ xīn de gèng fāngbiàn. Tā yòng zhège shōuyīnjī yǐjīng yòng le èrshí nián le.
text_en: At the intersection there's a bicycle repairman. He always has an old radio beside him, not too loud. He listens to news while repairing bikes and music while resting. I asked why he doesn't use his phone to listen. He said phones need to be held, which isn't convenient when repairing bikes. Just put the radio there and it works. He's right — sometimes old things are more convenient than new ones. He's been using this radio for twenty years.

questions.q_zh: 如果故事继续，最可能发生什么？
questions.q_en: If the story continued, what would most likely happen?

options.pinyin: huì yǒu hěn dà de wèntí

questions.explanation: The clue is that at the intersection there's a bicycle repairman — from there you can infer the answer.

questions.q_zh: 他为什么不用手机听？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't he use a phone to listen?

options.text_en: his phone is out of battery

options.text: 修车时手机要用手拿，不方便
options.pinyin: xiū chē shí shǒujī yào yòng shǒu ná, bù fāngbiàn
options.text_en: when fixing bikes you need your hands, a phone isn't convenient

questions.explanation: The key phrase 「手机要用手拿，」 gives the reason — when fixing bikes you need your hands, a phone isn't convenient.

title: The Dog That Knows the Schedule
text_zh: 我们小区有一只黄色的狗。它没有主人，但是每个人都认识它。每天早上它在学校门口等，因为有孩子会给它东西吃。中午它去菜市场，那里的老板会给它一些骨头。下午它在公园里睡觉。晚上它在小区门口，等下班回来的人摸摸它。这只狗比很多人都更了解这个小区的生活。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xiǎoqū yǒu yì zhī huángsè de gǒu. Tā méiyǒu zhǔrén, dànshì měi gè rén dōu rènshi tā. Měi tiān zǎoshang tā zài xuéxiào ménkǒu děng, yīnwèi yǒu háizi huì gěi tā dōngxī chī. Zhōngwǔ tā qù cài shìchǎng, nàlǐ de lǎobǎn huì gěi tā yìxiē gǔtou. Xiàwǔ tā zài gōngyuán lǐ shuìjiào. Wǎnshàng tā zài xiǎoqū ménkǒu, děng xiàbān huílái de rén mō mo tā. Zhè zhī gǒu bǐ hěn duō rén dōu gèng liǎojiě zhège xiǎoqū de shēnghuó.
text_en: Our neighborhood has a yellow dog. It has no owner, but everyone knows it. Every morning it waits at the school entrance because children give it food. At noon it goes to the wet market, where the shop owners give it bones. In the afternoon it sleeps in the park. In the evening it's at the neighborhood entrance, waiting for people coming home from work to pet it. This dog understands the life of this neighborhood better than many people.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的语气/氛围是怎样的？
questions.q_en: What is the tone/atmosphere of this passage?
questions.explanation: Their behavior — our neighborhood has a yellow dog — shows that attitude.

questions.q_zh: 这只狗有主人吗？
questions.q_en: Does this dog have an owner?
questions.explanation: Look for 「没有主人」 — it confirms the answer: no owner.

title: Fog Morning
text_zh: 今天早上有雾。平时能看到的那座山完全看不见了。走在路上，前面的人走了十几步就消失在雾里。我觉得雾把这个城市变成了一个新的地方。平时很吵的马路变得安静了，因为大家开车都很慢。最有意思的是，雾里面的声音听起来更近。一个人在很远的地方说话，我觉得他就在我旁边。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān zǎoshang yǒu wù. Píngshí néng kàn dào de nà zuò shān wánquán kàn bú jiàn le. Zǒu zài lù shàng, qiánmiàn de rén zǒu le shí jǐ bù jiù xiāoshī zài wù lǐ. Wǒ juéde wù bǎ zhège chéngshì biànchéng le yí gè xīn de dìfāng. Píngshí hěn chǎo de mǎlù biàn de ānjìng le, yīnwèi dàjiā kāi chē dōu hěn màn. Zuì yǒu yìsi de shì, wù lǐmiàn de shēngyīn tīng qǐlái gèng jìn. Yí gè rén zài hěn yuǎn de dìfāng shuōhuà, wǒ juéde tā jiù zài wǒ pángbiān.
text_en: This morning there was fog. The mountain I can usually see completely disappeared. Walking on the road, people ahead vanished into the fog after just a dozen steps. I felt the fog turned this city into a new place. The usually noisy road became quiet because everyone drove slowly. The most interesting thing is that sounds in fog seem closer. Someone speaking far away felt like they were right beside me.

questions.q_zh: 为什么马路变安静了？
questions.q_en: Why did the road become quiet?

options.pinyin: dàjiā kāi chē dōu hěn màn

questions.explanation: 因为 (because) directly states the reason: everyone drives slowly.

questions.q_zh: 雾里的声音听起来怎么样？
questions.q_en: How do sounds in fog seem?
questions.explanation: 最有意思的是，雾里面的声音听起来更近 — sounds in fog 'seem closer,' so distant speech felt like it was right beside the narrator.

title: The Umbrella Stand
text_zh: 我们办公室门口有一个伞架。下雨天可以看到很多不同的伞。有的很新，有的很旧。我注意到一把蓝色的伞已经在那里放了三个月了。没有人拿走它，也没有人扔掉它。也许它的主人已经不在这里工作了，但是伞还在等他回来。每次我看到那把蓝色的伞，就觉得有点难过。有些东西不知道自己已经被忘记了。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen bàngōngshì ménkǒu yǒu yí gè sǎn jià. Xià yǔ tiān kěyǐ kàn dào hěn duō bù tóng de sǎn. Yǒude hěn xīn, yǒude hěn jiù. Wǒ zhùyì dào yì bǎ lánsè de sǎn yǐjīng zài nàlǐ fàng le sān gè yuè le. Méiyǒu rén ná zǒu tā, yě méiyǒu rén rēng diào tā. Yěxǔ tā de zhǔrén yǐjīng bú zài zhèlǐ gōngzuò le, dànshì sǎn hái zài děng tā huílái. Měi cì wǒ kàn dào nà bǎ lánsè de sǎn, jiù juéde yǒudiǎn nánguò. Yǒuxiē dōngxī bù zhīdào zìjǐ yǐjīng bèi wàngjì le.
text_en: There's an umbrella stand outside our office. On rainy days you can see many different umbrellas. Some are new, some are old. I noticed a blue umbrella has been there for three months. Nobody has taken it, and nobody has thrown it away. Maybe its owner doesn't work here anymore, but the umbrella is still waiting for them to come back. Every time I see that blue umbrella, I feel a little sad. Some things don't know they've been forgotten.

questions.q_zh: 蓝色的伞在那里多长时间了？
questions.q_en: How long has the blue umbrella been there?
questions.explanation: The number 「三个月」 answers directly — the blue umbrella has been sitting there for three months.

questions.q_zh: 作者看到那把伞的时候感觉怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does the author feel seeing that umbrella?
questions.explanation: Their behavior — every time i see that blue umbrella, i feel a little sad — shows that attitude.

title: Where Birds Sleep
text_zh: 我一直想知道城市里的鸟晚上睡在哪里。有一天晚上我特别注意看了一下。我发现很多鸟睡在空调外机的上面，因为冬天那里暖和。有的鸟睡在商店的招牌后面，因为那里没有风。最聪明的鸟睡在地铁站的出口上面，因为那里一直有热气往上走。鸟和人一样，都会选择最舒服的地方住。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yìzhí xiǎng zhīdào chéngshì lǐ de niǎo wǎnshàng shuì zài nǎlǐ. Yǒu yì tiān wǎnshàng wǒ tèbié zhùyì kàn le yíxià. Wǒ fāxiàn hěn duō niǎo shuì zài kōngtiáo wàijī de shàngmiàn, yīnwèi dōngtiān nàlǐ nuǎnhuo. Yǒude niǎo shuì zài shāngdiàn de zhāopai hòumiàn, yīnwèi nàlǐ méiyǒu fēng. Zuì cōngmíng de niǎo shuì zài dìtiě zhàn de chūkǒu shàngmiàn, yīnwèi nàlǐ yìzhí yǒu rèqì wǎng shàng zǒu. Niǎo hé rén yíyàng, dōu huì xuǎnzé zuì shūfú de dìfāng zhù.
text_en: I always wanted to know where city birds sleep at night. One evening I paid special attention. I found that many birds sleep on top of air conditioner units because they're warm in winter. Some birds sleep behind shop signs because there's no wind there. The smartest birds sleep above subway exits because warm air always rises there. Birds are like people — they all choose the most comfortable place to live.

questions.q_zh: 这段话用了什么方式让读者理解人物？
questions.q_en: How does the passage help the reader understand the character?

options.pinyin: tōngguò miáoxiě tā zuò de shì

options.text: 直接说他/她是什么样的人
options.pinyin: zhíjiē shuō tā shì shénme yàng de rén
options.text_en: by directly stating what kind of person they are

questions.explanation: Rather than telling us about the narrator directly, we learn about them through their actions — systematically investigating where birds sleep, checking AC units, shop signs, and subway exits.

questions.q_zh: 鸟为什么睡在空调外机上？
questions.q_en: Why do birds sleep on AC units?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi néng kàn dào hěn yuǎn

questions.explanation: 很多鸟睡在空调外机的上面，因为冬天那里暖和 — the AC units radiate heat, making them warm spots for birds in winter.

title: The Grocery Store Closing Time
text_zh: 我家旁边的杂货店说九点关门，但是其实从来没有准时关过。如果九点的时候还有人在买东西，老板就不关。如果外面下雨，他会早一点关，因为不会有人来了。如果天气好，他会晚一点关，因为散步的人可能会进来。他的关门时间不看钟，看天气和人。我觉得这才是真正的做生意的方法。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā pángbiān de záhuò diàn shuō jiǔ diǎn guān mén, dànshì qíshí cónglái méiyǒu zhǔnshí guān guò. Rúguǒ jiǔ diǎn de shíhòu hái yǒu rén zài mǎi dōngxī, lǎobǎn jiù bù guān. Rúguǒ wàimiàn xià yǔ, tā huì zǎo yìdiǎn guān, yīnwèi bú huì yǒu rén lái le. Rúguǒ tiānqì hǎo, tā huì wǎn yìdiǎn guān, yīnwèi sànbù de rén kěnéng huì jìnlái. Tā de guān mén shíjiān bú kàn zhōng, kàn tiānqì hé rén. Wǒ juéde zhè cái shì zhēnzhèng de zuò shēngyì de fāngfǎ.
text_en: The grocery store next to my home says it closes at nine, but it has never actually closed on time. If there are still people shopping at nine, the boss doesn't close. If it's raining outside, he closes early because nobody will come. If the weather is nice, he closes late because people out for walks might come in. His closing time doesn't follow the clock — it follows the weather and the people. I think this is the real way to do business.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么选择写这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why did the author choose to include these details?

options.text_en: to help the reader feel the scene
because it'll be on the test

questions.explanation: The specific details — closing late when people stroll, early when it rains, ignoring the clock — build a vivid scene that lets the reader feel the shop's rhythm and the boss's character.

questions.q_zh: 老板的关门时间看什么？
questions.q_en: What determines the boss's closing time?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「看天气和人」, indicating watch the weather and people.

title: The Alley That Wakes Up Last
text_zh: 在城市的中心，有一条很窄的巷子。跟旁边热闹的大街比较，这条巷子好像总是慢一拍。早上九点，大街上的人已经在忙了，但是巷子里的店还没有开门。十点左右，第一家早餐店才把桌子搬到外面。老板慢慢地擦桌子，一边跟隔壁的老板聊天。住在这里的人好像都不着急。他们说：「城市跑得太快了，我们这条巷子选择慢慢走。」每次走进这条巷子，我都觉得时间被放慢了。外面的世界还在跑，但是在这里，你可以停下来喝一碗粥，看一看天空。
text_pinyin: Zài chéngshì de zhōngxīn, yǒu yì tiáo hěn zhǎi de xiàngzi. Gēn pángbiān rènao de dà jiē bǐjiào, zhè tiáo xiàngzi hǎoxiàng zǒngshì màn yí pāi. Zǎoshang jiǔ diǎn, dà jiē shàng de rén yǐjīng zài máng le, dànshì xiàngzi lǐ de diàn hái méiyǒu kāi mén. Shí diǎn zuǒyòu, dì yī jiā zǎocān diàn cái bǎ zhuōzi bān dào wàimiàn. Lǎobǎn mànman de cā zhuōzi, yìbiān gēn gébì de lǎobǎn liáotiān. Zhù zài zhèlǐ de rén hǎoxiàng dōu bù zhāojí. Tāmen shuō:「Chéngshì pǎo de tài kuài le, wǒmen zhè tiáo xiàngzi xuǎnzé mànman zǒu.」 Měi cì zǒu jìn zhè tiáo xiàngzi, wǒ dōu juéde shíjiān bèi fàng màn le. Wàimiàn de shìjiè hái zài pǎo, dànshì zài zhèlǐ, nǐ kěyǐ tíng xiàlái hē yì wǎn zhōu, kàn yi kàn tiānkōng.
text_en: In the center of the city, there's a narrow alley. The bustling main street beside it is already busy at nine, but shops here haven't opened yet. Around ten, the first breakfast shop moves its tables outside. The owner wipes tables while chatting with the shop next door. People here never seem hurried. They say: 'The city runs too fast — our alley chooses to walk slowly.' Every time I walk in, I feel time has slowed down. The outside world is still running, but here you can stop for a bowl of porridge and look at the sky.

questions.q_zh: 跟大街比较，这条巷子怎么样？
questions.q_en: How is this alley compared to the main street?
questions.explanation: The passage shows this through: the bustling main street beside it is already busy at nine, but shops here haven't opened yet.

questions.q_zh: 住在巷子里的人怎么说？
questions.q_en: What do people living in the alley say?

options.text: 城市跑得太快了，他们选择慢慢走
options.pinyin: chéngshì pǎo de tài kuài le, tāmen xuǎnzé mànman zǒu
options.text_en: the city runs too fast, they choose to walk slowly

options.pinyin: tāmen yě xiǎng pǎo kuài yìdiǎn
options.text_en: they want to run faster too

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「城市跑得太快了，」, indicating the city runs too fast, they choose to walk slowly.

questions.q_zh: 走进巷子以后，「我」有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' feel after entering the alley?

options.pinyin: juéde shíjiān bèi fàng màn le
options.text_en: feels time has been slowed down

questions.explanation: The words and actions (' every time i walk in, i feel time has slowed down) point to that.

title: The Rooftop Garden
text_zh: 我家楼顶有一个天台。以前那里什么都没有，只有灰尘和旧东西。后来六楼的王奶奶把一些旧盆子搬上去，开始种菜。一开始只种了几棵葱。慢慢地，西红柿、黄瓜、辣椒都有了。现在整个天台变成了一个小菜园。邻居们越来越感兴趣，有人帮忙浇水，有人带来了新的种子。王奶奶把种出来的菜分给大家。以前大家住在一栋楼里都不说话，现在因为这个菜园，邻居们开始互相认识了。一个小小的天台，被一个老人变成了整栋楼最温暖的地方。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā lóudǐng yǒu yí gè tiāntái. Yǐqián nàlǐ shénme dōu méiyǒu, zhǐ yǒu huīchén hé jiù dōngxi. Hòulái liù lóu de Wáng nǎinai bǎ yìxiē jiù pénzi bān shàngqù, kāishǐ zhòng cài. Yì kāishǐ zhǐ zhòng le jǐ kē cōng. Mànman de, xīhóngshì, huángguā, làjiāo dōu yǒu le. Xiànzài zhěng gè tiāntái biàn chéng le yí gè xiǎo càiyuán. Línjūmen yuè lái yuè gǎn xìngqù, yǒu rén bāngmáng jiāo shuǐ, yǒu rén dài lái le xīn de zhǒngzi. Wáng nǎinai bǎ zhòng chūlái de cài fēn gěi dàjiā. Yǐqián dàjiā zhù zài yí dòng lóu lǐ dōu bù shuō huà, xiànzài yīnwèi zhège càiyuán, línjūmen kāishǐ hùxiāng rènshi le. Yí gè xiǎoxiǎo de tiāntái, bèi yí gè lǎorén biàn chéng le zhěng dòng lóu zuì wēnnuǎn de dìfang.
text_en: My building's rooftop has a terrace. Before, nothing was there — just dust and old things. Then Grandma Wang from the sixth floor carried old pots up and started growing vegetables. At first just a few green onions. Gradually, tomatoes, cucumbers, and peppers appeared. Now the whole terrace has become a small garden. Neighbors grew more and more interested — some help water, some bring new seeds. Grandma Wang shares what she grows with everyone. Before, people in the same building never talked; now because of this garden, neighbors have started getting to know each other. A small terrace was turned by one old person into the warmest place in the whole building.

questions.q_zh: 王奶奶一开始种了什么？
questions.q_en: What did Grandma Wang plant first?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「几棵葱」, indicating a few green onions.

questions.q_zh: 菜园给邻居们带来了什么变化？
questions.q_en: What change did the garden bring to the neighbors?

options.text: 邻居们开始互相认识了
options.text_en: neighbors started getting to know each other

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「邻居们开始互相认识了」, indicating neighbors started getting to know each other.

questions.q_zh: 天台被变成了什么？
questions.q_en: What was the terrace turned into?

options.pinyin: zhěng dòng lóu zuì wēnnuǎn de dìfang
options.text_en: the warmest place in the building

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「整栋楼最温暖的地方」, indicating the warmest place in the building.

title: The Underground Musician
text_zh: 地铁站和商场之间有一条地下通道。每天下班的时候，那里都站着一个拉小提琴的男人。他穿得很干净，眼睛闭着，身体跟着音乐轻轻地摇。大部分人走过去的时候不会停下来，但是他好像并不在乎。有一天我停下来听了一首完整的曲子。他拉完以后睁开眼睛看到我，微微笑了一下。我往他的琴盒里放了钱。他说：「谢谢你愿意停下来。其实我不是为了钱——我只是想让走过的人的心情好一点。」从那以后，我每次经过都会放慢脚步，让那段音乐陪我走一小段路。
text_pinyin: Dìtiě zhàn hé shāngchǎng zhījiān yǒu yì tiáo dìxià tōngdào. Měi tiān xiàbān de shíhou, nàlǐ dōu zhànzhe yí gè lā xiǎotíqín de nánrén. Tā chuān de hěn gānjìng, yǎnjing bìzhe, shēntǐ gēnzhe yīnyuè qīngqīng de yáo. Dà bùfen rén zǒu guòqù de shíhou bú huì tíng xiàlái, dànshì tā hǎoxiàng bìng bú zàihū. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ tíng xiàlái tīng le yì shǒu wánzhěng de qǔzi. Tā lā wán yǐhòu zhēng kāi yǎnjing kàn dào wǒ, wēiwēi xiào le yíxià. Wǒ wǎng tā de qínhé lǐ fàng le qián. Tā shuō:「Xièxie nǐ yuànyì tíng xiàlái. Qíshí wǒ búshì wèile qián——wǒ zhǐshì xiǎng ràng zǒu guò de rén de xīnqíng hǎo yìdiǎn.」 Cóng nà yǐhòu, wǒ měi cì jīngguò dōu huì fàng màn jiǎobù, ràng nà duàn yīnyuè péi wǒ zǒu yì xiǎo duàn lù.
text_en: Between the subway station and the mall, there's an underground passage. Every evening at rush hour, a man stands there playing the violin. He dresses neatly, eyes closed, body gently swaying with the music. Most people pass without stopping, but he doesn't seem to mind. One day I stopped and listened to a complete piece. When he finished and opened his eyes, he saw me and gave a small smile. I put money in his case. He said: 'Thank you for being willing to stop. Actually I'm not doing this for money — I just want people passing by to feel a little better.' Since then, every time I pass I slow my steps and let that music walk with me a short way.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么选择写这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why did the author choose to include these details?

options.text_en: to help the reader feel the scene
because it'll be on the test

questions.explanation: 他说：'谢谢你愿意停下来。其实我不是为了钱' and the narrator's change of pace afterward show that stopping to listen brought quiet joy — happiness from a simple, unforced moment.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话你能了解到人物什么？
questions.q_en: What can you learn about the character from this passage?

options.text: 他/她是一个有感情的人
options.pinyin: tā shì yí gè yǒu gǎnqíng de rén
options.text_en: they are a person with feelings

questions.explanation: The violinist plays with eyes closed, body swaying, says he's not doing it for money but to brighten people's moods — his sensitivity and care for strangers reveal a deeply feeling person.

questions.q_zh: 这段话说明了什么道理？
questions.q_en: What principle does this passage illustrate?

options.text: 简单的生活也能很快乐
options.pinyin: jiǎndān de shēnghuó yě néng hěn kuàilè
options.text_en: a simple life can also be happy

options.pinyin: yí gè rén bù néng kuàilè

questions.explanation: The violinist plays not for money but to make passersby 心情好一点 (feel a little better), and the narrator starts slowing down to listen — simple music in a tunnel becomes a source of genuine contentment.

title: The Corner Mailbox
text_zh: 我家附近有一个绿色的老信箱。现在大家都用手机发信息了，但是那个信箱还在。有一天我看到一个小女孩站在信箱前面，踮着脚往里面塞了一封信。她的妈妈站在旁边笑。我走过去问她寄给谁。小女孩说：「寄给圣诞老人。」她妈妈小声对我说：「其实信寄不出去，但是我不想告诉她。让她相信一些美好的事情，再多一年也好。」我突然觉得，那个旧信箱虽然不再被用来寄信了，但是它装着一个孩子的梦。有些东西的价值不在于它还有没有用，而在于它还能不能承载人们的感情。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā fùjìn yǒu yí gè lǜsè de lǎo xìnxiāng. Xiànzài dàjiā dōu yòng shǒujī fā xìnxī le, dànshì nàge xìnxiāng hái zài. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ kàn dào yí gè xiǎo nǚhái zhàn zài xìnxiāng qiánmiàn, diǎnzhe jiǎo wǎng lǐmiàn sāi le yì fēng xìn. Tā de māma zhàn zài pángbiān xiào. Wǒ zǒu guòqù wèn tā jì gěi shéi. Xiǎo nǚhái shuō:「Jì gěi Shèngdàn lǎorén.」 Tā māma xiǎo shēng duì wǒ shuō:「Qíshí xìn jì bù chūqù, dànshì wǒ bù xiǎng gàosu tā. Ràng tā xiāngxìn yìxiē měihǎo de shìqing, zài duō yì nián yě hǎo.」 Wǒ tūrán juéde, nàge jiù xìnxiāng suīrán bú zài bèi yòng lái jì xìn le, dànshì tā zhuāngzhe yí gè háizi de mèng. Yǒuxiē dōngxi de jiàzhí bú zàiyú tā hái yǒu méiyǒu yòng, ér zàiyú tā hái néng bù néng chéngzài rénmen de gǎnqíng.
text_en: Near my home there's a green old mailbox. Everyone uses phones to send messages now, but that mailbox is still there. One day I saw a little girl standing before it, on tiptoe, pushing a letter inside. Her mother stood nearby, smiling. I walked over and asked who the letter was for. The girl said: 'Santa Claus.' Her mother whispered to me: 'Actually the letter won't get delivered, but I don't want to tell her. Letting her believe in something beautiful for one more year is fine.' I suddenly felt that although the old mailbox is no longer used for sending letters, it holds a child's dream. The value of some things isn't whether they're still useful, but whether they can still carry people's feelings.

questions.q_zh: 小女孩把信寄给谁？
questions.q_en: Who did the girl send the letter to?
questions.explanation: 「圣诞老人」 tells you who — the girl sent her letter to Santa Claus.

questions.q_zh: 妈妈为什么不告诉女儿信寄不出去？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the mother tell her daughter the letter won't be delivered?

options.text: 想让女儿多相信美好的事情一年
options.pinyin: xiǎng ràng nǚ'ér duō xiāngxìn měihǎo de shìqing yì nián
options.text_en: wants her to believe in beautiful things one more year
because the letter can be sent

questions.explanation: The mother whispers 让她相信一些美好的事情，再多一年也好 — she'd rather protect her daughter's innocent belief in something beautiful for one more year than correct her.

questions.q_zh: 关系中的小细节说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What do the small details in the relationship suggest?

options.text_en: they don't really like each other

questions.explanation: The clue is that near my home there's a green old mailbox — from there you can infer the answer.

title: The Night Bus Home
text_zh: 晚上十一点半的末班车上，人不多。每个人都很累，有的人靠着窗户睡着了，有的人戴着耳机闭着眼睛。司机开得很稳，好像知道车上的人需要安静。车窗外面是一排排的路灯，一闪一闪地往后退。我喜欢坐在最后一排，看着前面那些疲惫的身影。虽然我们谁都不认识，但是在这辆车上，我们都是一样的——都是想回家的人。末班车比较慢，但是没有人抱怨。大概是因为大家都知道，不管多晚，只要还有一辆车，就能到家。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang shíyī diǎn bàn de mòbānchē shàng, rén bù duō. Měi gè rén dōu hěn lèi, yǒu de rén kàozhe chuānghu shuìzháo le, yǒu de rén dàizhe ěrjī bìzhe yǎnjing. Sījī kāi de hěn wěn, hǎoxiàng zhīdào chē shàng de rén xūyào ānjìng. Chēchuāng wàimiàn shì yì pái pái de lùdēng, yí shǎn yí shǎn de wǎng hòu tuì. Wǒ xǐhuan zuò zài zuìhòu yì pái, kànzhe qiánmiàn nàxiē píbèi de shēnyǐng. Suīrán wǒmen shéi dōu bú rènshi, dànshì zài zhè liàng chē shàng, wǒmen dōu shì yíyàng de——dōu shì xiǎng huí jiā de rén. Mòbānchē bǐjiào màn, dànshì méiyǒu rén bàoyuàn. Dàgài shì yīnwèi dàjiā dōu zhīdào, bùguǎn duō wǎn, zhǐ yào hái yǒu yí liàng chē, jiù néng dào jiā.
text_en: On the last bus at eleven thirty, there aren't many people. Everyone is tired — some lean against windows asleep, some wear earphones with eyes closed. The driver drives steadily, as if knowing the passengers need quiet. Outside the windows, rows of streetlights flash past one by one. I like sitting in the last row, watching the tired silhouettes ahead. Although none of us know each other, on this bus we're all the same — all people who want to get home. The last bus is rather slow, but no one complains. Probably because everyone knows that no matter how late it is, as long as there's still one bus running, you can get home.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的主要感受是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the main feeling of this passage?
questions.explanation: The words and actions (on the last bus at eleven thirty, there aren't many people) point to that.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得车上的人都一样，是因为什么？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' think everyone on the bus is the same?

options.pinyin: dōu zài tóng yì jiā gōngsī shàng bān
options.text_en: all work at the same company

options.pinyin: dōu shì xiǎng huí jiā de rén
dōu zhù zài yí gè dìfang
options.text_en: all live in the same place

questions.explanation: The 虽然...但是 (although...but) structure contrasts two ideas — the 但是 clause gives the real point.

questions.q_zh: 为什么末班车慢也没有人抱怨？
questions.q_en: Why does no one complain about the slow last bus?

options.text: 因为只要有车就能到家
options.pinyin: yīnwèi zhǐ yào yǒu chē jiù néng dào jiā
options.text_en: as long as there's a bus, you can get home
no one is in a rush

questions.explanation: 因为 (because) directly states the reason: as long as there's a bus, you can get home.

title: The Hospital Cafeteria at 3 AM
text_zh: 医院的食堂二十四小时都开着。凌晨三点的时候，里面只有几个人。有刚做完手术的医生，手还在轻轻地抖。有在走廊里等了一整夜的家属，眼睛红红的。食堂阿姨给每个人端上热粥，不多问。她说：「在这个时间来吃饭的人，都有自己的难处。我能做的就是让粥热一点。」我爸爸住院的那几天，我每天凌晨都来这里。那碗粥的味道很普通，但是在那个时候，它比任何东西都温暖。有些安慰不需要说出来，一碗热粥就够了。
text_pinyin: Yīyuàn de shítáng èrshísì xiǎoshí dōu kāizhe. Língchén sān diǎn de shíhou, lǐmiàn zhǐ yǒu jǐ gè rén. Yǒu gāng zuò wán shǒushù de yīshēng, shǒu hái zài qīngqīng de dǒu. Yǒu zài zǒuláng lǐ děng le yì zhěng yè de jiāshǔ, yǎnjing hónghóng de. Shítáng āyí gěi měi gè rén duān shàng rè zhōu, bù duō wèn. Tā shuō:「Zài zhège shíjiān lái chī fàn de rén, dōu yǒu zìjǐ de nánchù. Wǒ néng zuò de jiùshì ràng zhōu rè yìdiǎn.」 Wǒ bàba zhùyuàn de nà jǐ tiān, wǒ měi tiān língchén dōu lái zhèlǐ. Nà wǎn zhōu de wèidào hěn pǔtōng, dànshì zài nàge shíhou, tā bǐ rènhé dōngxi dōu wēnnuǎn. Yǒuxiē ānwèi bù xūyào shuō chūlái, yì wǎn rè zhōu jiù gòu le.
text_en: The hospital cafeteria stays open twenty-four hours. At three in the morning, only a few people are inside. A doctor who just finished surgery, hands still trembling slightly. Family members who waited in the corridor all night, eyes red. The cafeteria auntie brings everyone hot porridge without asking much. She says: 'People who come to eat at this hour all have their own hardships. All I can do is make the porridge a little hotter.' During the days my father was hospitalized, I came here every early morning. That porridge tasted ordinary, but at that moment, it was warmer than anything. Some comfort doesn't need words — a bowl of hot porridge is enough.

questions.q_zh: 关系中的小细节说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What do the small details in the relationship suggest?

options.text_en: they don't really like each other

questions.explanation: The detail 「他们很了解对方」 reveals the underlying meaning — they know each other well.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的主要感受是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the main feeling of this passage?
questions.explanation: The passage describes that porridge tasted ordinary, but at that moment, it was warmer than anything, which reveals that feeling.

questions.q_zh: 如果故事继续，最可能发生什么？
questions.q_en: If the story continued, what would most likely happen?

options.pinyin: huì yǒu hěn dà de wèntí

questions.explanation: The cafeteria auntie serves hot porridge without questions, night after night; the narrator calls it 比任何东西都温暖 — this steady, wordless kindness would naturally continue unchanged.

title: The Missing Library Book
text_zh: 图书馆的王阿姨发现有一本书不见了。那本书已经很旧了，除了一个老人以外，没有别人借过。王阿姨把借书的记录一页一页地看了一遍。她发现那个老人上个月来还书的时候，把两本书放在了桌子上，但是只有一本被扫了码。王阿姨越想越觉得奇怪——那个老人每次来都很小心。她去问前台的小李，小李说：「那天我帮他扫码的时候，有人来问路，我一边回答一边扫的，可能漏了一本。」书找到了，就在还书的架子后面，被别的书挡住了。
text_pinyin: Túshūguǎn de Wáng āyí fāxiàn yǒu yì běn shū bújiàn le. Nà běn shū yǐjīng hěn jiù le, chúle yí gè lǎorén yǐwài, méiyǒu biéren jiè guò. Wáng āyí bǎ jiè shū de jìlù yí yè yí yè de kàn le yí biàn. Tā fāxiàn nàge lǎorén shàng gè yuè lái huán shū de shíhou, bǎ liǎng běn shū fàng zài le zhuōzi shàng, dànshì zhǐyǒu yì běn bèi sǎo le mǎ. Wáng āyí yuè xiǎng yuè juéde qíguài——nàge lǎorén měi cì lái dōu hěn xiǎoxīn. Tā qù wèn qiántái de Xiǎo Lǐ, Xiǎo Lǐ shuō:「Nà tiān wǒ bāng tā sǎo mǎ de shíhou, yǒu rén lái wèn lù, wǒ yìbiān huídá yìbiān sǎo de, kěnéng lòu le yì běn.」Shū zhǎo dào le, jiù zài huán shū de jiàzi hòumiàn, bèi biéde shū dǎng zhù le.
text_en: Librarian Auntie Wang noticed a book was missing. It was an old book — no one had borrowed it except one elderly man. She went through the lending records page by page. She found that when the old man returned books last month, he placed two on the desk, but only one was scanned. The more she thought about it, the stranger it seemed — that old man was always careful. She asked Xiao Li at the front desk. Xiao Li said: 'That day when I was scanning for him, someone came to ask for directions. I was answering and scanning at the same time — I probably missed one.' The book was found behind the return shelf, hidden by other books.

questions.q_en: Who was the only person who had borrowed the book?
questions.explanation: The passage identifies this by saying it was an old book — no one had borrowed it except one elderly man.

options.text_en: Xiao Li was answering someone's question while scanning and missed one

questions.explanation: The passage describes librarian auntie wang noticed a book was missing, which reveals that feeling.

title: Who Watered the Flowers?
text_zh: 楼下花园里的花越来越好看了，但是没有人知道是谁在浇花。物业的人说不是他们。住在一楼的张奶奶说她每天早上五点就醒了，但是从来没有看到有人浇花。我决定自己找一找。我发现花旁边的地比较湿，但只有早上才湿。我又发现花的旁边有一根很细的管子，管子从二楼的窗户那里接下来的。我去敲了二楼的门。开门的是一个害羞的小女孩。她说：「我把洗菜的水留下来，每天早上从窗户倒下去。妈妈说不能浪费水。」
text_pinyin: Lóu xià huāyuán lǐ de huā yuè lái yuè hǎokàn le, dànshì méiyǒu rén zhīdào shì shéi zài jiāo huā. Wùyè de rén shuō búshì tāmen. Zhù zài yī lóu de Zhāng nǎinai shuō tā měi tiān zǎoshang wǔ diǎn jiù xǐng le, dànshì cónglái méiyǒu kàn dào yǒu rén jiāo huā. Wǒ juédìng zìjǐ zhǎo yì zhǎo. Wǒ fāxiàn huā pángbiān de dì bǐjiào shī, dàn zhǐyǒu zǎoshang cái shī. Wǒ yòu fāxiàn huā de pángbiān yǒu yì gēn hěn xì de guǎnzi, guǎnzi cóng èr lóu de chuānghu nàlǐ jiē xiàlái de. Wǒ qù qiāo le èr lóu de mén. Kāi mén de shì yí gè hàixiū de xiǎo nǚhái. Tā shuō:「Wǒ bǎ xǐ cài de shuǐ liú xiàlái, měi tiān zǎoshang cóng chuānghu dào xiàqu. Māma shuō bù néng làngfèi shuǐ.」
text_en: The flowers in the garden downstairs were getting prettier and prettier, but nobody knew who was watering them. The property management said it wasn't them. Grandma Zhang on the first floor said she woke at five every morning but never saw anyone watering. I decided to investigate. I noticed the ground near the flowers was rather wet, but only in the morning. I also found a thin tube running down from a second-floor window. I knocked on the second-floor door. A shy little girl answered. She said: 'I save the water from washing vegetables and pour it down from the window every morning. Mom says we shouldn't waste water.'

questions.q_zh: 花园里的花怎么样了？
questions.q_en: What happened to the flowers in the garden?

options.pinyin: yuè lái yuè bù hǎokàn le

questions.explanation: The phrase 越来越 (more and more) signals a gradual change: getting prettier.

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「洗菜的水」, indicating vegetable-washing water.

title: The Extra Cup
text_zh: 办公室里每天下午都会多出来一个干净的杯子，放在茶水间的桌子上。同事们觉得很奇怪。小王说：「可能是清洁阿姨放的。」但是清洁阿姨说不是她。我比较好奇，所以下午两点的时候，我没有去开会，一个人坐在茶水间旁边看。三点左右，我看到隔壁公司的一个女生走了进来。她把自己带的杯子洗干净，放在桌子上，然后又走了。第二天我问她为什么。她不好意思地说：「我每天来借你们的热水喝，觉得应该留一个干净的杯子，算是谢谢你们。」
text_pinyin: Bàngōngshì lǐ měi tiān xiàwǔ dōu huì duō chūlái yí gè gānjìng de bēizi, fàng zài cháshuǐjiān de zhuōzi shàng. Tóngshìmen juéde hěn qíguài. Xiǎo Wáng shuō:「Kěnéng shì qīngjié āyí fàng de.」Dànshì qīngjié āyí shuō búshì tā. Wǒ bǐjiào hàoqí, suǒyǐ xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn de shíhou, wǒ méiyǒu qù kāi huì, yí gè rén zuò zài cháshuǐjiān pángbiān kàn. Sān diǎn zuǒyòu, wǒ kàn dào gébì gōngsī de yí gè nǚshēng zǒu le jìnlái. Tā bǎ zìjǐ dài de bēizi xǐ gānjìng, fàng zài zhuōzi shàng, ránhòu yòu zǒu le. Dì-èr tiān wǒ wèn tā wèishénme. Tā bù hǎoyìsi de shuō:「Wǒ měi tiān lái jiè nǐmen de rè shuǐ hē, juéde yīnggāi liú yí gè gānjìng de bēizi, suànshì xièxie nǐmen.」
text_en: Every afternoon, an extra clean cup appeared on the break room table. Colleagues found it strange. Xiao Wang said: 'Maybe the cleaning lady left it.' But the cleaning lady said it wasn't her. I was rather curious, so at two in the afternoon I skipped the meeting and sat near the break room watching. Around three, I saw a woman from the company next door walk in. She washed a cup she'd brought, left it on the table, then left. The next day I asked her why. She said shyly: 'I come every day to use your hot water. I felt I should leave a clean cup as a thank-you.'

questions.q_zh: 这段话说明了什么道理？
questions.q_en: What principle does this passage illustrate?

options.text: 简单的生活也能很快乐
options.pinyin: jiǎndān de shēnghuó yě néng hěn kuàilè
options.text_en: a simple life can also be happy

options.pinyin: yí gè rén bù néng kuàilè

questions.explanation: A woman from another company secretly leaves a clean cup daily as thanks for using their hot water — this small, unasked-for gesture of gratitude shows that simple acts of consideration bring quiet satisfaction.

questions.explanation: 我没有去开会，一个人坐在茶水间旁边看 — skipping the meeting to stake out the break room at two PM is how the narrator caught the woman in the act.

options.text_en: she borrowed hot water daily and left cups as thanks

title: Two Names
text_zh: 我有两个名字。在公司，大家叫我「David」。在家里，爸妈叫我「大卫」。小时候我觉得有两个名字很酷，但是越长越大，越来越不知道哪个才是真正的自己。有一次，一个外国同事问我：「你更喜欢别人叫你什么？」我想了很久，说：「都可以。」但是回家以后，妈妈叫了一声「大卫」，我忽然觉得很温暖。也许名字不重要，重要的是叫你名字的那个人，把多少感情放在了里面。比较起来，「大卫」这个名字听起来更像家。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu liǎng gè míngzi. Zài gōngsī, dàjiā jiào wǒ「David」. Zài jiā lǐ, bà mā jiào wǒ「Dàwèi」. Xiǎo shíhou wǒ juéde yǒu liǎng gè míngzi hěn kù, dànshì yuè zhǎng yuè dà, yuè lái yuè bù zhīdào nǎge cái shì zhēnzhèng de zìjǐ. Yǒu yí cì, yí gè wàiguó tóngshì wèn wǒ:「Nǐ gèng xǐhuan biéren jiào nǐ shénme?」Wǒ xiǎng le hěn jiǔ, shuō:「Dōu kěyǐ.」Dànshì huí jiā yǐhòu, māma jiào le yì shēng「Dàwèi」, wǒ hūrán juéde hěn wēnnuǎn. Yěxǔ míngzi bú zhòngyào, zhòngyào de shì jiào nǐ míngzi de nàge rén, bǎ duōshao gǎnqíng fàng zài le lǐmiàn. Bǐjiào qǐlái,「Dàwèi」zhège míngzi tīng qǐlái gèng xiàng jiā.
text_en: I have two names. At the office, everyone calls me 'David.' At home, my parents call me 'Dawei.' As a kid I thought having two names was cool, but the older I got, the less I knew which one was really me. Once, a foreign colleague asked: 'What do you prefer people call you?' I thought for a long time and said: 'Either is fine.' But when I got home and Mom called out 'Dawei,' I suddenly felt warm. Maybe the name doesn't matter — what matters is how much feeling the person calling it puts into it. Comparatively, 'Dawei' sounds more like home.

questions.q_zh: 这些人之间最重要的是什么？
questions.q_en: What matters most between these people?
questions.explanation: 妈妈叫了一声「大卫」，我忽然觉得很温暖 — the warmth the narrator feels when Mom uses that name shows what matters most is the love and connection behind how someone calls you.

questions.q_zh: 「我」长大以后觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel growing up?

options.text_en: how much feeling the caller puts in

options.text_en: whether to use Chinese or English

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「多少感情」, indicating how much feeling the caller puts in.

title: The Left-Handed Girl
text_zh: 我从小就用左手写字。小时候，老师让我把笔换到右手，说这样比较正常。我试了，但是右手写的字又丑又慢。妈妈对老师说：「除了用左手以外，她什么都跟别人一样。」后来我长大了，发现用左手的人其实不少。我们切菜、写字、拿筷子都用左手，虽然有时候不太方便——比如说，剪刀是给右手设计的，被我用起来总是不太顺。但是我越来越觉得，这就是我。不需要被改变，也不需要跟别人一样。
text_pinyin: Wǒ cóng xiǎo jiù yòng zuǒ shǒu xiě zì. Xiǎo shíhou, lǎoshī ràng wǒ bǎ bǐ huàn dào yòu shǒu, shuō zhèyàng bǐjiào zhèngcháng. Wǒ shì le, dànshì yòu shǒu xiě de zì yòu chǒu yòu màn. Māma duì lǎoshī shuō:「Chúle yòng zuǒ shǒu yǐwài, tā shénme dōu gēn biéren yíyàng.」Hòulái wǒ zhǎng dà le, fāxiàn yòng zuǒ shǒu de rén qíshí bù shǎo. Wǒmen qiē cài, xiě zì, ná kuàizi dōu yòng zuǒ shǒu, suīrán yǒu shíhou bú tài fāngbiàn——bǐrú shuō, jiǎndāo shì gěi yòu shǒu shèjì de, bèi wǒ yòng qǐlái zǒng shì bú tài shùn. Dànshì wǒ yuè lái yuè juéde, zhè jiùshì wǒ. Bù xūyào bèi gǎibiàn, yě bù xūyào gēn biéren yíyàng.
text_en: I've written with my left hand since I was small. The teacher told me to switch to my right hand, saying that was more normal. I tried, but my right-hand writing was ugly and slow. Mom told the teacher: 'Besides using her left hand, she's the same as everyone else.' Growing up, I found a few other left-handed people. We chop vegetables, write, and hold chopsticks with our left hand — though sometimes it's inconvenient. Scissors are designed for right hands and never feel right when I use them. But this is simply who I am. I don't need to be changed.

questions.q_zh: 老师让「我」做什么？
questions.q_en: What did the teacher ask 'I' to do?

options.pinyin: bǎ bǐ huàn dào yòu shǒu
options.text_en: switch the pen to the right hand

questions.explanation: 老师让我把笔换到右手，说这样比较正常 — the teacher asked her to switch her pen to the right hand, calling it 'more normal.'

questions.q_zh: 妈妈怎么说的？
questions.q_en: What did Mom say?

options.text_en: besides using her left hand, she's the same as everyone

options.pinyin: tā huì mànman gǎi guòlái de
yòng zuǒ shǒu bǐ yòu shǒu hǎo
options.text_en: left hand is better than right

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「除了用左手以外，她什」, indicating besides using her left hand, she's the same as everyone.

questions.q_zh: 从前后的对比可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we learn from the before-and-after contrast?

options.pinyin: jīnglì dàilái le xīn de rènshi

questions.explanation: 其实 (actually/in fact) signals a correction — what follows is the real situation: experience brought new understanding.

title: The Accent
text_zh: 我在北京工作了五年，普通话说得越来越好。但是每次打电话回家，一听到爸爸的声音，我的口音就会变回来。同事们第一次听到我打电话的样子，都很惊讶：「你平时说话不是这样的啊！」我笑了笑，不知道怎么解释。其实我比较喜欢我的方言，因为那是我小时候学会的第一种语言。有人说方言不好听，但是对我来说，方言就像妈妈做的菜——也许卖相不好，但是味道是最对的。我不想把它改掉，那是我来的地方。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài Běijīng gōngzuò le wǔ nián, pǔtōnghuà shuō de yuè lái yuè hǎo. Dànshì měi cì dǎ diànhuà huí jiā, yì tīng dào bàba de shēngyīn, wǒ de kǒuyīn jiù huì biàn huílái. Tóngshìmen dì-yī cì tīng dào wǒ dǎ diànhuà de yàngzi, dōu hěn jīngyà:「Nǐ píngshí shuō huà búshì zhèyàng de a!」Wǒ xiào le xiào, bù zhīdào zěnme jiěshì. Qíshí wǒ bǐjiào xǐhuan wǒ de fāngyán, yīnwèi nà shì wǒ xiǎo shíhou xuéhuì de dì-yī zhǒng yǔyán. Yǒu rén shuō fāngyán bù hǎo tīng, dànshì duì wǒ lái shuō, fāngyán jiù xiàng māma zuò de cài——yěxǔ màixiàng bù hǎo, dànshì wèidào shì zuì duì de. Wǒ bù xiǎng bǎ tā gǎi diào, nà shì wǒ lái de dìfang.
text_en: I've worked in Beijing for five years and my Mandarin has gotten better and better. But every time I call home and hear Dad's voice, my accent switches back. The first time my colleagues heard me on the phone, they were surprised: 'You don't usually talk like that!' I smiled, not knowing how to explain. Actually I rather like my dialect — it was the first language I learned as a child. Some people say dialects don't sound nice, but to me, dialect is like Mom's cooking — maybe it doesn't look fancy, but the taste is exactly right. I don't want to change it. It's where I come from.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话你能了解到人物什么？
questions.q_en: What can you learn about the character from this passage?

options.text: 他/她是一个有感情的人
options.pinyin: tā shì yí gè yǒu gǎnqíng de rén
options.text_en: they are a person with feelings

questions.explanation: The detail 「他/她是一个有感」 reveals the underlying meaning — they are a person with feelings.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中，什么是最有意思的部分？
questions.q_en: What is the most interesting part of this passage?

options.text: 平常中有不平常的东西
options.pinyin: píngcháng zhōng yǒu bù píngcháng de dōngxi
fāshēng le hěn dà de shì

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「平常中有不平常的东西」, indicating something unusual within the ordinary.

questions.q_en: Does 'I' want to change the dialect?

yes, because colleagues find it odd

title: The Voice Message
text_zh: 我妈妈刚学会用手机发语音消息。但是她有一个习惯——每次说完以后，都会加一句「好了，就这样」，然后按停。问题是，她经常不小心又按了一下，把「好了，就这样」录了两遍。所以我每天收到的语音都是：「今天天气不错，你多穿一件。好了，就这样。好了，就这样。」我姐姐说：「你应该教妈妈怎么用。」但是我觉得比较好笑，不想改。有一天妈妈给我发了一条很长的语音，最后说：「这次我只说一遍——好了，就这样。就这样。就……」她又说了三遍。
text_pinyin: Wǒ māma gāng xuéhuì yòng shǒujī fā yǔyīn xiāoxi. Dànshì tā yǒu yí gè xíguàn——měi cì shuō wán yǐhòu, dōu huì jiā yí jù「hǎo le, jiù zhèyàng」, ránhòu àn tíng. Wèntí shì, tā jīngcháng bù xiǎoxīn yòu àn le yí xià, bǎ「hǎo le, jiù zhèyàng」lù le liǎng biàn. Suǒyǐ wǒ měi tiān shōu dào de yǔyīn dōu shì:「Jīntiān tiānqì búcuò, nǐ duō chuān yí jiàn. Hǎo le, jiù zhèyàng. Hǎo le, jiù zhèyàng.」Wǒ jiějie shuō:「Nǐ yīnggāi jiāo māma zěnme yòng.」Dànshì wǒ juéde bǐjiào hǎoxiào, bù xiǎng gǎi. Yǒu yì tiān māma gěi wǒ fā le yì tiáo hěn cháng de yǔyīn, zuìhòu shuō:「Zhè cì wǒ zhǐ shuō yí biàn——hǎo le, jiù zhèyàng. Jiù zhèyàng. Jiù……」Tā yòu shuō le sān biàn.
text_en: My mom just learned to send voice messages on her phone. But she has a habit — after every message, she adds 'Okay, that's it,' then hits stop. The problem is, she often accidentally presses again and records 'Okay, that's it' twice. So every voice message I get is: 'Nice weather today, wear an extra layer. Okay, that's it. Okay, that's it.' My sister said: 'You should teach Mom how to use it.' But I thought it was rather funny and didn't want to change it. One day Mom sent me a long voice message, ending with: 'This time I'll only say it once — okay, that's it. That's it. That's...' She said it three more times.

questions.q_zh: 妈妈的语音消息有什么问题？
questions.q_en: What's the problem with Mom's voice messages?

options.text: 总是「好了，就这样」说两遍
options.pinyin: zǒng shì「hǎo le, jiù zhèyàng」shuō liǎng biàn
shuō de huà tīng bù qīngchu
fā gěi le cuò de rén

questions.explanation: The story centers on the problem is, she often accidentally presses again and records 'okay, that's it' twice, which carries that theme.

questions.q_zh: 姐姐觉得应该怎么做？
questions.q_en: What did the sister think should be done?

options.text_en: teach Mom how to use it

questions.explanation: 姐姐说：'你应该教妈妈怎么用' — the sister's practical suggestion was to teach Mom proper voice message technique.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的主要感受是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the main feeling of this passage?
questions.explanation: The words and actions (my mom just learned to send voice messages on her phone) point to that.

title: The Group Photo
text_zh: 公司要拍集体照，大家站了三排。摄影师说：「高的站后面，矮的站前面。」这本来很简单，但是我们部门的小张和小李差不多高，两个人都不想站前面。小张说：「我比你高一点。」小李说：「你穿了高跟鞋，把鞋脱了我们再比。」摄影师等了十分钟，越来越着急。最后经理走过来说：「你们两个都站中间，问题不就解决了吗？」大家都笑了。照片拍出来以后，小张发现自己闭了眼睛，要求再拍一张。小李小声说：「比身高的时候那么认真，拍照的时候怎么不认真？」
text_pinyin: Gōngsī yào pāi jítǐ zhào, dàjiā zhàn le sān pái. Shèyǐngshī shuō:「Gāo de zhàn hòumiàn, ǎi de zhàn qiánmiàn.」Zhè běnlái hěn jiǎndān, dànshì wǒmen bùmén de Xiǎo Zhāng hé Xiǎo Lǐ chàbuduō gāo, liǎng gè rén dōu bù xiǎng zhàn qiánmiàn. Xiǎo Zhāng shuō:「Wǒ bǐ nǐ gāo yìdiǎn.」Xiǎo Lǐ shuō:「Nǐ chuān le gāogēn xié, bǎ xié tuō le wǒmen zài bǐ.」Shèyǐngshī děng le shí fēnzhōng, yuè lái yuè zhāojí. Zuìhòu jīnglǐ zǒu guòlái shuō:「Nǐmen liǎng gè dōu zhàn zhōngjiān, wèntí bú jiù jiějué le ma?」Dàjiā dōu xiào le. Zhàopiàn pāi chūlái yǐhòu, Xiǎo Zhāng fāxiàn zìjǐ bì le yǎnjīng, yāoqiú zài pāi yì zhāng. Xiǎo Lǐ xiǎo shēng shuō:「Bǐ shēngāo de shíhou nàme rènzhēn, pāi zhào de shíhou zěnme bú rènzhēn?」
text_en: The company needed a group photo. The photographer said: 'Tall ones in the back, short ones in front.' Xiao Zhang and Xiao Li were about the same height, and neither wanted to stand in front. Xiao Zhang said: 'I'm a little taller than you.' Xiao Li said: 'You're wearing heels — take them off and let's compare.' The photographer waited ten minutes. Finally the manager said: 'You two both stand in the middle — problem solved.' When the photo came out, Xiao Zhang had closed her eyes and asked to retake it. Xiao Li whispered: 'So serious about height, but not about the photo?'

questions.q_en: Why did Xiao Zhang and Xiao Li argue?

options.pinyin: liǎng gè rén dōu bù xiǎng zhàn qiánmiàn
options.text_en: neither wanted to stand in front

options.pinyin: liǎng gè rén dōu xiǎng zhàn zhōngjiān
options.text_en: both wanted to stand in the middle
they didn't want to take the photo

questions.explanation: 有一天下雨，面包师在门口放了一个纸箱子 — on a rainy day the baker placed a cardboard shelter at the door, showing care beyond just feeding.

questions.q_en: How did the manager solve it?

had them both stand in back

options.text_en: told them not to be in the photo

questions.explanation: The passage shows this through: finally the manager said: 'you two both stand in the middle — problem solved.

questions.q_en: What was wrong with the photo?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「小张闭了眼睛」, indicating Xiao Zhang closed her eyes.

title: The Last Bus
text_zh: 每天晚上十一点半，最后一班公交车从我家门口经过。司机是一个四十多岁的女人，她每次到站都会多等一分钟。有人问她为什么，她说：「赶最后一班车的人，一定是今天过得不太容易的人。多等一分钟，也许就能让他们不用打车回家。」我有一次加班到很晚，跑到车站的时候，车已经关了门。但是司机从后视镜里看到我了，又把门打开了。我上车以后，她笑着说：「赶上了。」那天晚上，那辆公交车比较空，窗外的路灯一盏一盏地过去，我觉得这座城市没有那么冷。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān wǎnshang shíyī diǎn bàn, zuìhòu yì bān gōngjiāo chē cóng wǒ jiā ménkǒu jīngguò. Sījī shì yí gè sìshí duō suì de nǚrén, tā měi cì dào zhàn dōu huì duō děng yì fēnzhōng. Yǒu rén wèn tā wèishénme, tā shuō:「Gǎn zuìhòu yì bān chē de rén, yídìng shì jīntiān guò de bú tài róngyì de rén. Duō děng yì fēnzhōng, yěxǔ jiù néng ràng tāmen bú yòng dǎchē huí jiā.」Wǒ yǒu yí cì jiābān dào hěn wǎn, pǎo dào chēzhàn de shíhou, chē yǐjīng guān le mén. Dànshì sījī cóng hòushìjìng lǐ kàn dào wǒ le, yòu bǎ mén dǎkāi le. Wǒ shàng chē yǐhòu, tā xiàozhe shuō:「Gǎn shàng le.」Nà tiān wǎnshang, nà liàng gōngjiāo chē bǐjiào kōng, chuāng wài de lùdēng yì zhǎn yì zhǎn de guòqu, wǒ juéde zhè zuò chéngshì méiyǒu nàme lěng.
text_en: Every night at eleven-thirty, the last bus passes by my door. The driver is a woman in her forties who always waits an extra minute at each stop. Someone asked her why. She said: 'People catching the last bus must have had a hard day. Waiting one more minute might save them from needing a taxi home.' One night I worked late and ran to the stop, but the doors had already closed. The driver saw me in the rearview mirror and opened the doors again. After I got on, she smiled and said: 'You made it.' That night the bus was rather empty. The streetlights passed one by one outside the window, and I felt this city wasn't so cold after all.

questions.q_zh: 司机为什么每次多等一分钟？
questions.q_en: Why does the driver wait an extra minute?
