options.pinyin: tā juéde gǎn zuìhòu yì bān chē de rén jīntiān bù róngyì
options.text_en: she thinks last-bus riders had a hard day

options.pinyin: tā bù xiǎng tài zǎo huí jiā
options.text_en: she doesn't want to go home early

questions.explanation: She explains 坐末班车的人一定很辛苦 — last-bus riders must have had a hard day, so the extra minute gives them a chance to catch it.

questions.q_zh: 「我」跑到车站的时候，车怎么了？
questions.q_en: What happened when 'I' arrived at the stop?
questions.explanation: 我跑到站台的时候，车门已经关了 — the narrator arrived to find the doors already closed, but the driver reopened them after that extra minute of waiting.

questions.q_zh: 「我」坐在公交车上有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' feel on the bus?

options.text_en: the city didn't feel so cold

options.pinyin: juéde gōngjiāo chē tài màn le

questions.explanation: The passage describes the streetlights passed one by one outside the window, which reveals that feeling.

title: The Overpass Musician
text_zh: 每天傍晚六点，地铁站旁边的天桥上都有一个人拉二胡。他穿得比较干净，不像是没有工作的人。他面前没有放钱的盒子，也没有写「请帮帮我」的牌子。他只是一边拉琴，一边看着下面来来去去的人。有一次我停下来听了一会儿。他拉完一首曲子以后，对我笑了笑。我问他：「你为什么在这里拉琴？」他说：「退休了，在家太安静了。这里人多，我拉琴给他们听，他们走路给我看。大家都不孤单。」我越想越觉得他说得对。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān bàngwǎn liù diǎn, dìtiě zhàn pángbiān de tiānqiáo shàng dōu yǒu yí gè rén lā èrhú. Tā chuān de bǐjiào gānjìng, bú xiàng shì méiyǒu gōngzuò de rén. Tā miànqián méiyǒu fàng qián de hézi, yě méiyǒu xiě「qǐng bāngbāng wǒ」de páizi. Tā zhǐshì yìbiān lā qín, yìbiān kànzhe xiàmiàn lái lái qù qù de rén. Yǒu yí cì wǒ tíng xiàlái tīng le yíhuìr. Tā lā wán yì shǒu qǔzi yǐhòu, duì wǒ xiào le xiào. Wǒ wèn tā:「Nǐ wèishénme zài zhèlǐ lā qín?」Tā shuō:「Tuìxiū le, zài jiā tài ānjìng le. Zhèlǐ rén duō, wǒ lā qín gěi tāmen tīng, tāmen zǒu lù gěi wǒ kàn. Dàjiā dōu bú gūdān.」Wǒ yuè xiǎng yuè juéde tā shuō de duì.
text_en: Every evening at six, someone plays the erhu on the overpass near the subway station. He dresses rather neatly — doesn't look like someone without work. There's no money box in front of him, no sign saying 'please help.' He just plays while watching the people coming and going below. Once I stopped to listen for a while. After he finished a piece, he smiled at me. I asked: 'Why do you play here?' He said: 'I'm retired. It's too quiet at home. There are lots of people here — I play for them, they walk for me. Nobody's lonely.' The more I thought about it, the more I felt he was right.

questions.q_zh: 拉二胡的人面前有什么？
questions.q_en: What was in front of the erhu player?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「什么都没有」, meaning nothing at all — unlike most performers, no hat or box for money.

questions.q_zh: 他为什么来天桥拉琴？
questions.q_en: Why does he play on the overpass?

options.pinyin: tuìxiū le, zài jiā tài ānjìng

questions.explanation: The key phrase 「退休了，在家太安静」 gives the reason — retired, too quiet at home.

questions.q_zh: 他说大家为什么不孤单？
questions.q_en: Why did he say nobody is lonely?

options.text: 他拉琴给人听，人走路给他看
options.pinyin: tā lā qín gěi rén tīng, rén zǒu lù gěi tā kàn
options.text_en: he plays for them, they walk for him

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tiānqiáo shàng rén hěn duō
options.text_en: lots of people on the overpass

questions.explanation: He says 我给他们拉琴，他们给我散步 — 'I play for them, they walk for me,' meaning the relationship is reciprocal, so nobody is truly alone.

title: The Rooftop Garden
text_zh: 我们办公楼的楼顶有一个小花园，但是很少有人知道。我是去年夏天发现的——那天太热了，我想找一个安静的地方吃午饭。楼顶有几盆花、两把旧椅子和一张小桌子。不知道是谁放的。从那以后，我每天中午都上去坐一会儿。后来我发现，除了我以外，还有两个人也经常来。一个是楼下咖啡店的女孩，她来这里看书。一个是九楼的程序员，他来这里睡午觉。我们三个人从来不说话，但是都把这个地方当成了自己的秘密花园。城市越来越忙，但是总有一些地方，安静地等着需要它的人。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen bàngōng lóu de lóudǐng yǒu yí gè xiǎo huāyuán, dànshì hěn shǎo yǒu rén zhīdào. Wǒ shì qùnián xiàtiān fāxiàn de——nà tiān tài rè le, wǒ xiǎng zhǎo yí gè ānjìng de dìfang chī wǔfàn. Lóudǐng yǒu jǐ pén huā, liǎng bǎ jiù yǐzi hé yì zhāng xiǎo zhuōzi. Bù zhīdào shì shéi fàng de. Cóng nà yǐhòu, wǒ měi tiān zhōngwǔ dōu shàngqu zuò yíhuìr. Hòulái wǒ fāxiàn, chúle wǒ yǐwài, hái yǒu liǎng gè rén yě jīngcháng lái. Yí gè shì lóu xià kāfēi diàn de nǚhái, tā lái zhèlǐ kàn shū. Yí gè shì jiǔ lóu de chéngxùyuán, tā lái zhèlǐ shuì wǔjiào. Wǒmen sān gè rén cónglái bù shuō huà, dànshì dōu bǎ zhège dìfang dāng chéng le zìjǐ de mìmì huāyuán. Chéngshì yuè lái yuè máng, dànshì zǒng yǒu yìxiē dìfang, ānjìng de děngzhe xūyào tā de rén.
text_en: On our office building's rooftop there's a small garden, but few people know about it. I found it last summer — too hot, I wanted a quiet place for lunch. The roof had a few potted flowers, two old chairs, and a small table. Since then, I go up every noon. Later I found two others came regularly. The girl from the coffee shop downstairs reads here. The programmer from the 9th floor naps here. We never talk, but we've all made this place our secret garden. The city gets busier, but some places quietly wait for those who need them.

questions.q_zh: 楼顶有什么？
questions.q_en: What was on the rooftop?

options.text: 几盆花、两把旧椅子、一张小桌子
options.pinyin: jǐ pén huā, liǎng bǎ jiù yǐzi, yì zhāng xiǎo zhuōzi
options.text_en: potted flowers, two old chairs, a small table

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「几盆花、两把旧椅子」, indicating potted flowers, two old chairs, a small table.

questions.q_zh: 除了「我」以外，还有谁来？
questions.q_en: Besides 'me,' who else comes?

options.text: 咖啡店的女孩和九楼的程序员
options.pinyin: kāfēi diàn de nǚhái hé jiǔ lóu de chéngxùyuán

questions.explanation: 「咖啡店的女孩」 identifies who — coffee shop girl and 9th-floor programmer.

questions.q_zh: 三个人在楼顶做什么？
questions.q_en: What do the three people do on the roof?

options.pinyin: gè zuò gè de, cónglái bù shuō huà
options.text_en: do their own things, never talk

questions.explanation: Look for 从来不 (never) in the text — it tells you this has never happened.

title: The Neighborhood Cat
text_zh: 我们小区有一只橘色的猫，没有人知道它是谁的。早上它在一楼王奶奶家门口等吃的，中午它在花园里的椅子上睡觉，晚上它去三楼李叔叔家看电视。王奶奶说：「这是我的猫。」李叔叔说：「明明是我的猫。」物业的人说：「这只猫谁的都不是，它是小区的猫。」我觉得物业说得比较对。这只猫把每个人的家都当成自己的家，它比我们都更了解这个小区。它知道谁家的鱼最好吃，谁家的沙发最舒服，谁晚上最晚关灯。它被所有人喜欢，因为它对所有人都一样好。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xiǎoqū yǒu yì zhī júsè de māo, méiyǒu rén zhīdào tā shì shéi de. Zǎoshang tā zài yī lóu Wáng nǎinai jiā ménkǒu děng chī de, zhōngwǔ tā zài huāyuán lǐ de yǐzi shàng shuì jiào, wǎnshang tā qù sān lóu Lǐ shūshu jiā kàn diànshì. Wáng nǎinai shuō:「Zhè shì wǒ de māo.」Lǐ shūshu shuō:「Míngmíng shì wǒ de māo.」Wùyè de rén shuō:「Zhè zhī māo shéi de dōu búshì, tā shì xiǎoqū de māo.」Wǒ juéde wùyè shuō de bǐjiào duì. Zhè zhī māo bǎ měi gè rén de jiā dōu dāng chéng zìjǐ de jiā, tā bǐ wǒmen dōu gèng liǎojiě zhège xiǎoqū. Tā zhīdào shéi jiā de yú zuì hǎochī, shéi jiā de shāfā zuì shūfu, shéi wǎnshang zuì wǎn guān dēng. Tā bèi suǒyǒu rén xǐhuan, yīnwèi tā duì suǒyǒu rén dōu yíyàng hǎo.
text_en: Our complex has an orange cat nobody claims. Mornings it waits at Grandma Wang's door for food. At noon it sleeps on a garden chair. Evenings it goes to Uncle Li's to watch TV. Grandma Wang says: 'This is my cat.' Uncle Li says: 'It's obviously my cat.' The property manager says: 'This cat belongs to nobody — it's the complex's cat.' This cat treats everyone's home as its own. It knows whose fish tastes best, whose sofa is most comfortable, who turns off the lights last. It's loved by everyone because it's equally nice to everyone.

questions.q_zh: 食物在这段话中代表什么？
questions.q_en: What does food represent in this passage?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「关爱和温暖」, indicating care and warmth.

questions.q_zh: 物业的人怎么说？
questions.q_en: What did the property manager say?

options.text: 猫谁的都不是，是小区的猫
options.pinyin: māo shéi de dōu búshì, shì xiǎoqū de māo
options.text_en: belongs to nobody, it's the complex's cat

questions.explanation: 物业的人说：'这只猫不属于任何人——它是小区的猫' — the property manager defines it as belonging to the whole complex, not to any individual.

questions.q_zh: 猫为什么被所有人喜欢？
questions.q_en: Why is the cat loved by everyone?

options.text: 因为它对所有人都一样好
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā duì suǒyǒu rén dōu yíyàng hǎo
options.text_en: because it's equally nice to everyone

questions.explanation: 因为 (because) directly states the reason: because it's equally nice to everyone.

title: The School Gate Guard
text_zh: 我们学校门口的保安姓刘，大家都叫他刘大爷。他每天早上六点半就到了，比所有的老师和学生都早。他记得每一个学生的名字。每天早上他会跟每个人说早上好，叫出他们的名字。有新来的学生，他一定会问：「你叫什么名字？哪个班的？」然后把名字记在一个小本子上。有人问他：「你记这些名字做什么？」他说：「除了家长以外，我可能是第一个每天跟他们说话的大人。我想让他们觉得，进了学校的门，就有人在等他们。」那个小本子比较旧了，但是上面写满了名字。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xuéxiào ménkǒu de bǎo'ān xìng Liú, dàjiā dōu jiào tā Liú dàye. Tā měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn bàn jiù dào le, bǐ suǒyǒu de lǎoshī hé xuéshēng dōu zǎo. Tā jìde měi yí gè xuéshēng de míngzi. Měi tiān zǎoshang tā huì gēn měi gè rén shuō zǎoshang hǎo, jiào chū tāmen de míngzi. Yǒu xīn lái de xuéshēng, tā yídìng huì wèn:「Nǐ jiào shénme míngzi? Nǎge bān de?」Ránhòu bǎ míngzi jì zài yí gè xiǎo běnzi shàng. Yǒu rén wèn tā:「Nǐ jì zhèxiē míngzi zuò shénme?」Tā shuō:「Chúle jiāzhǎng yǐwài, wǒ kěnéng shì dì-yī gè měi tiān gēn tāmen shuō huà de dàrén. Wǒ xiǎng ràng tāmen juéde, jìn le xuéxiào de mén, jiù yǒu rén zài děng tāmen.」Nàge xiǎo běnzi bǐjiào jiù le, dànshì shàngmiàn xiě mǎn le míngzi.
text_en: The security guard at our school gate is surnamed Liu — everyone calls him Grandpa Liu. He arrives at six-thirty every morning, earlier than all the teachers and students. He remembers every student's name. Every morning he greets each person by name. When new students come, he always asks: 'What's your name? Which class?' Then he writes it in a small notebook. Someone asked him: 'Why do you record all these names?' He said: 'Besides their parents, I might be the first adult who speaks to them every day. I want them to feel that once they walk through the school gate, someone is waiting for them.' That notebook is rather old, but it's filled with names.

questions.q_zh: 刘大爷每天几点到学校？
questions.q_en: What time does Grandpa Liu arrive?
questions.explanation: The number 「六点半」 answers directly — Grandpa Liu arrives at six-thirty every morning.

questions.q_zh: 你觉得这个人以后会怎样？
questions.q_en: What do you think will happen to this person in the future?

options.pinyin: huì bān dào bié de dìfang

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「继续这样平静地生活」, indicating continue living peacefully like this.

questions.q_zh: 刘大爷为什么记学生的名字？
questions.q_en: Why does Grandpa Liu remember students' names?

options.text: 想让学生觉得有人在等他们
options.pinyin: xiǎng ràng xuéshēng juéde yǒu rén zài děng tāmen
options.text_en: wants students to feel someone is waiting for them

options.text: 他记性很好，喜欢记东西
options.pinyin: tā jìxing hěn hǎo, xǐhuan jì dōngxi
options.text_en: he has good memory and likes recording things

questions.explanation: 他说 我想让他们觉得，走进校门就有人在等他们 — he wants each student to feel that someone is specifically waiting for them, which is why he memorizes every name.

title: The Hospital Café
text_zh: 医院一楼有一家很小的咖啡店。店主是个三十多岁的女人，她以前是这家医院的护士。她说开咖啡店是因为她知道在医院等消息的人有多难受。她的咖啡比外面的便宜一半。晚上十点以后，她会把卖不完的面包免费送给在走廊等着的家属。有人说：「这样你赚不到钱。」她笑着说：「我在医院工作了八年，除了治病以外，我觉得最重要的是让等的人不那么害怕。一杯热咖啡做不了什么大事，但是它比较温暖。」这家店越来越有名了，很多人专门来喝她的咖啡。
text_pinyin: Yīyuàn yī lóu yǒu yì jiā hěn xiǎo de kāfēi diàn. Diànzhǔ shì ge sānshí duō suì de nǚrén, tā yǐqián shì zhè jiā yīyuàn de hùshi. Tā shuō kāi kāfēi diàn shì yīnwèi tā zhīdào zài yīyuàn děng xiāoxi de rén yǒu duō nánshòu. Tā de kāfēi bǐ wàimiàn de piányi yíbàn. Wǎnshang shí diǎn yǐhòu, tā huì bǎ mài bù wán de miànbāo miǎnfèi sòng gěi zài zǒuláng děngzhe de jiāshǔ. Yǒu rén shuō:「Zhèyàng nǐ zhuàn bú dào qián.」Tā xiàozhe shuō:「Wǒ zài yīyuàn gōngzuò le bā nián, chúle zhì bìng yǐwài, wǒ juéde zuì zhòngyào de shì ràng děng de rén bú nàme hàipà. Yì bēi rè kāfēi zuò bù liǎo shénme dà shì, dànshì tā bǐjiào wēnnuǎn.」Zhè jiā diàn yuè lái yuè yǒumíng le, hěn duō rén zhuānmén lái hē tā de kāfēi.
text_en: On the hospital's first floor there's a tiny coffee shop. The owner, a woman in her thirties, used to be a nurse here. She says she knows how hard it is for people waiting for news. Her coffee costs half what it does outside. After ten at night, she gives unsold bread free to families waiting in corridors. Someone said: 'You won't make money this way.' She smiled: 'I worked here for eight years. Besides treating illness, the most important thing is making waiting people less afraid. A cup of hot coffee can't do anything big, but it's warm.'

questions.q_zh: 店主以前做什么工作？
questions.q_en: What did the shop owner used to do?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「护士」, meaning nurse — that was her profession before opening the shop.

questions.q_zh: 如果故事继续，最可能发生什么？
questions.q_en: If the story continued, what would most likely happen?

options.pinyin: huì yǒu hěn dà de wèntí

questions.explanation: The clue is that on the hospital's first floor there's a tiny coffee shop — from there you can infer the answer.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中，什么是最有意思的部分？
questions.q_en: What is the most interesting part of this passage?

options.text: 平常中有不平常的东西
options.pinyin: píngcháng zhōng yǒu bù píngcháng de dōngxi
fāshēng le hěn dà de shì

questions.explanation: A coffee shop inside a hospital is inherently surprising — the juxtaposition of a comforting, everyday ritual (coffee) within a place of illness creates the passage's most interesting tension.

title: The Post Office Window
text_zh: 老城区的邮局只剩一个窗口了。以前有四个，但是现在寄信的人越来越少，三个窗口被关了。窗口后面坐着一个快要退休的阿姨。她说她在这里工作了三十年。以前每天要处理几百封信，现在一天可能只有十几封。来寄信的大多是老人，他们不太会用手机。有一个老爷爷每个月都来寄一封信给在国外的女儿。阿姨帮他把信封写好，把邮票贴好。老爷爷说：「打电话比较快，但是我觉得收到信比较开心。信是可以留下来的。」阿姨把每封信都仔细地盖上邮戳，好像在做一件很重要的事情。
text_pinyin: Lǎo chéngqū de yóujú zhǐ shèng yí gè chuāngkǒu le. Yǐqián yǒu sì gè, dànshì xiànzài jì xìn de rén yuè lái yuè shǎo, sān gè chuāngkǒu bèi guān le. Chuāngkǒu hòumiàn zuòzhe yí gè kuài yào tuìxiū de āyí. Tā shuō tā zài zhèlǐ gōngzuò le sānshí nián. Yǐqián měi tiān yào chǔlǐ jǐ bǎi fēng xìn, xiànzài yì tiān kěnéng zhǐyǒu shí jǐ fēng. Lái jì xìn de dàduō shì lǎorén, tāmen bú tài huì yòng shǒujī. Yǒu yí gè lǎoyéye měi gè yuè dōu lái jì yì fēng xìn gěi zài guówài de nǚ'ér. Āyí bāng tā bǎ xìnfēng xiě hǎo, bǎ yóupiào tiē hǎo. Lǎoyéye shuō:「Dǎ diànhuà bǐjiào kuài, dànshì wǒ juéde shōu dào xìn bǐjiào kāixīn. Xìn shì kěyǐ liú xiàlái de.」Āyí bǎ měi fēng xìn dōu zǐxì de gài shàng yóuchuō, hǎoxiàng zài zuò yí jiàn hěn zhòngyào de shìqing.
text_en: The old district post office has only one window left. There used to be four, but fewer and fewer people send letters, so three were closed. Behind the window sits an auntie nearing retirement. She says she's worked here thirty years. She used to handle hundreds of letters daily; now there might only be a dozen. Most who come are elderly people who aren't good with phones. One grandfather comes every month to mail a letter to his daughter abroad. The auntie helps him write the envelope and stick on the stamp. The grandfather says: 'Phone calls are faster, but I think receiving a letter is more joyful. Letters can be kept.' The auntie carefully stamps each letter with the postmark, as if doing something important.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中，什么是最有意思的部分？
questions.q_en: What is the most interesting part of this passage?

options.text: 平常中有不平常的东西
options.pinyin: píngcháng zhōng yǒu bù píngcháng de dōngxi
fāshēng le hěn dà de shì

questions.explanation: The phrase 越来越 (more and more) signals a gradual change: something unusual within the ordinary.

questions.q_zh: 来寄信的大多是什么人？
questions.q_en: Who mostly comes to mail letters?
questions.explanation: The passage identifies this by saying most who come are elderly people who aren't good with phones.

questions.q_zh: 老爷爷为什么寄信而不打电话？
questions.q_en: Why does the grandfather mail letters instead of calling?

options.text: 他觉得收到信比较开心，信可以留下来
options.pinyin: tā juéde shōu dào xìn bǐjiào kāixīn, xìn kěyǐ liú xiàlái
options.text_en: receiving letters is more joyful, letters can be kept

options.pinyin: nǚ'ér ràng tā jì xìn de

questions.explanation: 爷爷说：'打电话快，但是我觉得收到信更开心。信可以留下来' — letters bring more joy than calls because they can be kept and reread.

title: The Rain Watcher
text_zh: 下雨的时候，我喜欢坐在窗户旁边看外面。雨比较小的时候，路上的人不着急，有人没带伞，就一边走一边用手挡着头。雨比较大的时候，大家都跑起来了，跑到最近的屋檐下面等。我发现人在雨里会变得不一样——平时不说话的邻居，会跟陌生人一起挤在屋檐下面，有时候还聊几句。有一次我看到一个小男孩把自己的伞给了旁边的老奶奶，自己淋着雨跑回家。妈妈问他为什么，他说：「老师说下雨的时候要帮助别人。」雨停了以后，一切又回到了平时的样子。但是我觉得，下雨的时候，才能看到人真正的样子。
text_pinyin: Xià yǔ de shíhou, wǒ xǐhuan zuò zài chuānghu pángbiān kàn wàimiàn. Yǔ bǐjiào xiǎo de shíhou, lù shàng de rén bù zhāojí, yǒu rén méi dài sǎn, jiù yìbiān zǒu yìbiān yòng shǒu dǎngzhe tóu. Yǔ bǐjiào dà de shíhou, dàjiā dōu pǎo qǐlái le, pǎo dào zuì jìn de wūyán xiàmiàn děng. Wǒ fāxiàn rén zài yǔ lǐ huì biàn de bù yíyàng——píngshí bù shuō huà de línjū, huì gēn mòshēng rén yìqǐ jǐ zài wūyán xiàmiàn, yǒu shíhou hái liáo jǐ jù. Yǒu yí cì wǒ kàn dào yí gè xiǎo nánhái bǎ zìjǐ de sǎn gěi le pángbiān de lǎo nǎinai, zìjǐ línzhe yǔ pǎo huí jiā. Māma wèn tā wèishénme, tā shuō:「Lǎoshī shuō xià yǔ de shíhou yào bāngzhù biéren.」Yǔ tíng le yǐhòu, yíqiè yòu huí dào le píngshí de yàngzi. Dànshì wǒ juéde, xià yǔ de shíhou, cái néng kàn dào rén zhēnzhèng de yàngzi.
text_en: When it rains, I like to sit by the window and watch outside. When the rain is light, people on the street aren't hurried — some without umbrellas just walk while shielding their heads with their hands. When it rains hard, everyone starts running, ducking under the nearest eaves. I've noticed people become different in the rain — neighbors who normally don't talk will crowd under an eave with strangers, sometimes even chatting. Once I saw a little boy give his umbrella to an elderly woman beside him and run home in the rain. His mom asked why, and he said: 'Teacher says when it rains, help others.' After the rain stops, everything returns to normal. But I think it's only when it rains that you see people's true selves.

questions.q_zh: 雨比较大的时候，人们怎么做？
questions.q_en: What do people do in heavy rain?

options.text: 跑到最近的屋檐下面等
options.pinyin: pǎo dào zuì jìn de wūyán xiàmiàn děng

questions.explanation: 下大雨的时候，大家都开始跑，躲到最近的屋檐下面 — everyone runs and ducks under the nearest eaves when it pours.

questions.q_zh: 小男孩为什么把伞给了老奶奶？
questions.q_en: Why did the boy give his umbrella to the elderly woman?

options.text: 老师说下雨的时候要帮助别人
options.pinyin: lǎoshī shuō xià yǔ de shíhou yào bāngzhù biéren
options.text_en: teacher said to help others when it rains

options.pinyin: lǎo nǎinai wèn tā jiè de
options.text_en: the old woman asked to borrow it

questions.explanation: The boy explains 老师说下雨的时候要帮助别人 — his teacher taught him to help others when it rains, which is why he gave up his umbrella.

questions.q_zh: 你觉得这个人以后会怎样？
questions.q_en: What do you think will happen to this person in the future?

options.pinyin: huì bān dào bié de dìfang

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「继续这样平静地生活」, indicating continue living peacefully like this.

title: The Old Couple at the Park
text_zh: 每天早上七点，公园的长椅上都坐着一对老夫妻。老爷爷看报纸，老奶奶织毛衣。他们很少说话，但是老奶奶织累了的时候，老爷爷会把自己的热水倒一杯给她。老奶奶也不说谢谢，只是接过来喝一口。我每天跑步经过他们旁边，慢慢地发现了一些细节——老奶奶织的毛衣都是老爷爷的大小，老爷爷的报纸看完以后，会把有趣的新闻折一个角，留给老奶奶看。他们好像把几十年的感情，都放在了这些不说话的小动作里面。比起说「我爱你」，我觉得这样比较真。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang qī diǎn, gōngyuán de cháng yǐ shàng dōu zuòzhe yí duì lǎo fūqī. Lǎoyéye kàn bàozhǐ, lǎo nǎinai zhī máoyī. Tāmen hěn shǎo shuō huà, dànshì lǎo nǎinai zhī lèi le de shíhou, lǎoyéye huì bǎ zìjǐ de rè shuǐ dào yì bēi gěi tā. Lǎo nǎinai yě bù shuō xièxie, zhǐshì jiē guòlái hē yì kǒu. Wǒ měi tiān pǎo bù jīngguò tāmen pángbiān, mànman de fāxiàn le yìxiē xìjié——lǎo nǎinai zhī de máoyī dōu shì lǎoyéye de dàxiǎo, lǎoyéye de bàozhǐ kàn wán yǐhòu, huì bǎ yǒuqù de xīnwén zhé yí gè jiǎo, liú gěi lǎo nǎinai kàn. Tāmen hǎoxiàng bǎ jǐ shí nián de gǎnqíng, dōu fàng zài le zhèxiē bù shuō huà de xiǎo dòngzuò lǐmiàn. Bǐ qǐ shuō「wǒ ài nǐ」, wǒ juéde zhèyàng bǐjiào zhēn.
text_en: Every morning at seven, an elderly couple sits on the park bench. The grandfather reads the newspaper; the grandmother knits. They rarely speak, but when she tires of knitting, he pours her a cup of his hot water. She doesn't say thank you — just takes it and sips. Jogging past them daily, I gradually noticed details: the sweaters she knits are always his size; after he finishes the paper, he folds a corner on interesting articles for her to read. They seem to have packed decades of feeling into these wordless little gestures. Compared to saying 'I love you,' I think this is more genuine.

questions.q_zh: 重复的行为暗示了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the repeated behavior imply?

options.text_en: this matters a lot to them

options.pinyin: tā méiyǒu bié de shì zuò
options.text_en: they have nothing else to do
others require them to do it

questions.explanation: The detail 「这对他/她很重要」 reveals the underlying meaning — this matters a lot to them.

questions.q_zh: 如果打破了这个习惯，可能会怎样？
questions.q_en: What might happen if this routine were broken?

options.pinyin: huì zhǎodào gèng hǎo de xíguàn

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「他/她会觉得不自在」, indicating they would feel unsettled.

questions.q_zh: 这段话用了什么方式让读者理解人物？
questions.q_en: How does the passage help the reader understand the character?

options.pinyin: tōngguò miáoxiě tā zuò de shì

options.text: 直接说他/她是什么样的人
options.pinyin: zhíjiē shuō tā shì shénme yàng de rén
options.text_en: by directly stating what kind of person they are

questions.explanation: We learn about the grandparents through their actions — 爷爷看报纸，奶奶织毛衣 — rather than direct description, their daily habits reveal who they are.

title: Grandmother's Dumplings
text_zh: 每年过年，我们全家都会一起包饺子。奶奶的饺子包得最好看——每个都一样大，一样漂亮。我从小就跟奶奶学包饺子，但是我包的总是比较丑。奶奶说：「饺子好不好看不重要，重要的是一家人一边包一边聊天。」爸爸负责和面，妈妈负责切菜，我和姐姐负责包。奶奶坐在旁边看着我们，偶尔帮我们把包得不好的修一修。去年奶奶生病了，不能包饺子了。过年的时候，我把饺子端到她面前。她吃了一口，说：「味道跟我包的一样。」我知道不一样，但是我没有说。除了味道以外，那些一起包饺子的下午，才是我最想留住的。
text_pinyin: Měi nián guò nián, wǒmen quán jiā dōu huì yìqǐ bāo jiǎozi. Nǎinai de jiǎozi bāo de zuì hǎokàn——měi gè dōu yíyàng dà, yíyàng piàoliang. Wǒ cóng xiǎo jiù gēn nǎinai xué bāo jiǎozi, dànshì wǒ bāo de zǒng shì bǐjiào chǒu. Nǎinai shuō:「Jiǎozi hǎo bù hǎokàn bú zhòngyào, zhòngyào de shì yì jiā rén yìbiān bāo yìbiān liáotiān.」Bàba fùzé huó miàn, māma fùzé qiē cài, wǒ hé jiějie fùzé bāo. Nǎinai zuò zài pángbiān kànzhe wǒmen, ǒu'ěr bāng wǒmen bǎ bāo de bù hǎo de xiū yì xiū. Qùnián nǎinai shēng bìng le, bù néng bāo jiǎozi le. Guò nián de shíhou, wǒ bǎ jiǎozi duān dào tā miànqián. Tā chī le yì kǒu, shuō:「Wèidào gēn wǒ bāo de yíyàng.」Wǒ zhīdào bù yíyàng, dànshì wǒ méiyǒu shuō. Chúle wèidào yǐwài, nàxiē yìqǐ bāo jiǎozi de xiàwǔ, cái shì wǒ zuì xiǎng liú zhù de.
text_en: Every Chinese New Year, our whole family makes dumplings together. Grandmother's are the prettiest — each one the same size. I learned from her, but mine always come out ugly. Grandmother says: 'How dumplings look doesn't matter — what matters is chatting while making them.' Dad kneads dough, Mom chops vegetables, my sister and I wrap. Grandmother watches, occasionally fixing our poorly wrapped ones. Last year Grandmother got sick. During New Year, I brought dumplings to her. She took a bite: 'Tastes just like mine.' I knew it wasn't the same, but I didn't say so. Those afternoons making dumplings together — that's what I most want to hold onto.

title: The Breakfast Stall
text_zh: 公司楼下有一个卖煎饼的早餐摊。老板是一对夫妻，丈夫摊煎饼，妻子收钱。每天早上七点到九点，那里排的队越来越长。我每次都点一样的——一个鸡蛋煎饼，不要辣。时间长了，我还没开口，妻子就对丈夫说：「一个鸡蛋的，不要辣。」有一天我想换一个口味，说：「今天加辣。」他们两个都看了我一眼，好像我做了一件很奇怪的事情。丈夫笑着说：「你确定吗？你吃了两年不辣的了。」我忽然觉得，被一个卖煎饼的人记住自己的口味，是一件比较幸福的事情。
text_pinyin: Gōngsī lóu xià yǒu yí gè mài jiānbing de zǎocān tān. Lǎobǎn shì yí duì fūqī, zhàngfu tān jiānbing, qīzi shōu qián. Měi tiān zǎoshang qī diǎn dào jiǔ diǎn, nàlǐ pái de duì yuè lái yuè cháng. Wǒ měi cì dōu diǎn yíyàng de——yí gè jīdàn jiānbing, bú yào là. Shíjiān cháng le, wǒ hái méi kāi kǒu, qīzi jiù duì zhàngfu shuō:「Yí gè jīdàn de, bú yào là.」Yǒu yì tiān wǒ xiǎng huàn yí gè kǒuwèi, shuō:「Jīntiān jiā là.」Tāmen liǎng gè dōu kàn le wǒ yì yǎn, hǎoxiàng wǒ zuò le yí jiàn hěn qíguài de shìqing. Zhàngfu xiàozhe shuō:「Nǐ quèdìng ma? Nǐ chī le liǎng nián bú là de le.」Wǒ hūrán juéde, bèi yí gè mài jiānbing de rén jì zhù zìjǐ de kǒuwèi, shì yí jiàn bǐjiào xìngfú de shìqing.
text_en: Below our office building there's a breakfast stall selling jianbing. The owners are a married couple — the husband makes the crepes, the wife collects money. Every morning from seven to nine, the line gets longer and longer. I always order the same thing — one egg jianbing, no spice. After a while, before I even spoke, the wife would tell the husband: 'One egg, no spice.' One day I wanted to try something different and said: 'Add spice today.' They both looked at me as if I'd done something very strange. The husband smiled: 'Are you sure? You've had no spice for two years.' I suddenly felt that having a jianbing seller remember your taste is a rather fortunate thing.

title: The Balcony Light
text_zh: 对面那栋楼的三楼，有一盏阳台上的灯每天晚上都亮着。我搬来这个小区以后就注意到了。不管刮风还是下雨，那盏灯从来没有关过。我一直很好奇，后来问了楼下的王阿姨。她说：「那是老张家的灯。他女儿在外地工作，每年只回来一两次。老张怕女儿回来的时候找不到家，所以一直开着灯。」我听了以后，觉得心里比较暖。从那以后，每次我晚上回家，抬头看到那盏灯，就觉得不是只有我一个人在等什么人。那盏灯越亮越久，但它不是浪费，它是一个父亲的等待。
text_pinyin: Duìmiàn nà dòng lóu de sān lóu, yǒu yì zhǎn yángtái shàng de dēng měi tiān wǎnshang dōu liàngzhe. Wǒ bān lái zhège xiǎoqū yǐhòu jiù zhùyì dào le. Bùguǎn guā fēng háishì xià yǔ, nà zhǎn dēng cónglái méiyǒu guān guò. Wǒ yìzhí hěn hàoqí, hòulái wèn le lóu xià de Wáng āyí. Tā shuō:「Nà shì lǎo Zhāng jiā de dēng. Tā nǚ'ér zài wàidì gōngzuò, měi nián zhǐ huílái yí liǎng cì. Lǎo Zhāng pà nǚ'ér huílái de shíhou zhǎo bú dào jiā, suǒyǐ yìzhí kāizhe dēng.」Wǒ tīng le yǐhòu, juéde xīnlǐ bǐjiào nuǎn. Cóng nà yǐhòu, měi cì wǒ wǎnshang huí jiā, táitóu kàn dào nà zhǎn dēng, jiù juéde búshì zhǐyǒu wǒ yí gè rén zài děng shénme rén. Nà zhǎn dēng yuè liàng yuè jiǔ, dàn tā búshì làngfèi, tā shì yí gè fùqīn de děngdài.
text_en: On the third floor across from mine, a balcony light stays on every night. I noticed it when I moved here. Rain or wind, that light has never been off. I asked Auntie Wang downstairs. She said: 'That's Old Zhang's light. His daughter works in another city and comes back once or twice a year. He's afraid she won't find home, so he keeps the light on.' Since then, every time I come home at night and see that light, I feel I'm not the only one waiting for someone. That light isn't waste — it's a father's waiting.

title: The Shoe Repairman's Radio
text_zh: 路口有一个修鞋的师傅，旁边总是放着一台旧收音机。那台收音机比较破了，声音有时候清楚有时候不清楚。但是师傅从来不换。有人说：「现在手机什么都能听，你为什么还用收音机？」师傅一边修鞋一边说：「这台收音机是我爸爸的。我小时候，他也是在路边修鞋的。他一边修鞋，一边听收音机。我现在做一样的事情，听一样的声音，就好像他还在旁边。」我走的时候，回头看了一眼。阳光照在那台旧收音机上面，修鞋的声音和收音机的声音混在一起，听起来很安静。
text_pinyin: Lùkǒu yǒu yí gè xiū xié de shīfu, pángbiān zǒng shì fàngzhe yì tái jiù shōuyīnjī. Nà tái shōuyīnjī bǐjiào pò le, shēngyīn yǒu shíhou qīngchu yǒu shíhou bù qīngchu. Dànshì shīfu cónglái bú huàn. Yǒu rén shuō:「Xiànzài shǒujī shénme dōu néng tīng, nǐ wèishénme hái yòng shōuyīnjī?」Shīfu yìbiān xiū xié yìbiān shuō:「Zhè tái shōuyīnjī shì wǒ bàba de. Wǒ xiǎo shíhou, tā yě shì zài lù biān xiū xié de. Tā yìbiān xiū xié, yìbiān tīng shōuyīnjī. Wǒ xiànzài zuò yíyàng de shìqing, tīng yíyàng de shēngyīn, jiù hǎoxiàng tā hái zài pángbiān.」Wǒ zǒu de shíhou, huítóu kàn le yì yǎn. Yángguāng zhào zài nà tái jiù shōuyīnjī shàngmiàn, xiū xié de shēngyīn hé shōuyīnjī de shēngyīn hùn zài yìqǐ, tīng qǐlái hěn ānjìng.
text_en: At the street corner there's a shoe repairman who always has an old radio beside him. The radio is rather beaten up — the sound is sometimes clear, sometimes not. But he never replaces it. Someone said: 'Phones can play anything now — why still use a radio?' The repairman said while fixing shoes: 'This radio was my father's. When I was little, he also repaired shoes by the road. He'd fix shoes while listening to the radio. Now I do the same thing, hear the same sounds — it's as if he's still beside me.' As I left, I glanced back. Sunlight fell on the old radio, the sounds of shoe repair and the radio blending together — it sounded very peaceful.

title: The Window Seat
text_zh: 我每天在同一家咖啡店写东西，坐的都是靠窗的那个位子。那个位子比较小，只能坐一个人，但是窗外刚好能看到一棵大树。春天树上有新叶子，夏天有很多鸟，秋天叶子变黄，冬天只剩下树枝。我觉得那棵树就像一个安静的朋友，陪我写了一整年的东西。有一天，咖啡店要重新装修，老板问我：「你有什么建议吗？」我说：「除了那个靠窗的位子以外，你什么都可以改。」老板笑了。装修以后，那个位子还在。窗外的树也还在。有些东西不需要被改变，它们已经刚刚好了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān zài tóng yì jiā kāfēi diàn xiě dōngxi, zuò de dōu shì kào chuāng de nàge wèizi. Nàge wèizi bǐjiào xiǎo, zhǐ néng zuò yí gè rén, dànshì chuāng wài gānghǎo néng kàn dào yì kē dà shù. Chūntiān shù shàng yǒu xīn yèzi, xiàtiān yǒu hěn duō niǎo, qiūtiān yèzi biàn huáng, dōngtiān zhǐ shèng xià shùzhī. Wǒ juéde nà kē shù jiù xiàng yí gè ānjìng de péngyou, péi wǒ xiě le yì zhěng nián de dōngxi. Yǒu yì tiān, kāfēi diàn yào chóngxīn zhuāngxiū, lǎobǎn wèn wǒ:「Nǐ yǒu shénme jiànyì ma?」Wǒ shuō:「Chúle nàge kào chuāng de wèizi yǐwài, nǐ shénme dōu kěyǐ gǎi.」Lǎobǎn xiào le. Zhuāngxiū yǐhòu, nàge wèizi hái zài. Chuāng wài de shù yě hái zài. Yǒuxiē dōngxi bù xūyào bèi gǎibiàn, tāmen yǐjīng gānggāng hǎo le.
text_en: Every day I write at the same coffee shop, always in the window seat. That seat is rather small — only fits one person — but through the window you can see a big tree. In spring it has new leaves, in summer many birds, in autumn the leaves turn yellow, in winter only bare branches remain. I feel that tree is like a quiet friend that kept me company through a whole year of writing. One day the coffee shop was going to renovate. The owner asked: 'Any suggestions?' I said: 'Besides that window seat, you can change anything.' The owner laughed. After renovation, that seat was still there. The tree outside was still there too. Some things don't need to be changed — they're already just right.

title: The Subway Reader
text_zh: 每天早上坐地铁的时候，我注意到同一节车厢里有一个看书的女生。她大概二十多岁，每天拿着不同的书。地铁很挤，但她总能找到一个角落，把自己缩得很小，安安静静地看。我很佩服她——旁边的人都在看手机，只有她在看纸书。有一次地铁忽然停了，车厢里很吵。但是她好像什么都没听到，还在看她的书。我忍不住看了一眼书的名字——是一本关于旅行的书。也许她的身体被困在地铁里，但是她的心已经去了很远的地方。我觉得这比较浪漫。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang zuò dìtiě de shíhou, wǒ zhùyì dào tóng yí jié chēxiāng lǐ yǒu yí gè kàn shū de nǚshēng. Tā dàgài èrshí duō suì, měi tiān názhe bùtóng de shū. Dìtiě hěn jǐ, dàn tā zǒng néng zhǎo dào yí gè jiǎoluò, bǎ zìjǐ suō de hěn xiǎo, ān'ānjìngjìng de kàn. Wǒ hěn pèifú tā——pángbiān de rén dōu zài kàn shǒujī, zhǐyǒu tā zài kàn zhǐ shū. Yǒu yí cì dìtiě hūrán tíng le, chēxiāng lǐ hěn chǎo. Dànshì tā hǎoxiàng shénme dōu méi tīng dào, hái zài kàn tā de shū. Wǒ rěn bú zhù kàn le yì yǎn shū de míngzi——shì yì běn guānyú lǚxíng de shū. Yěxǔ tā de shēntǐ bèi kùn zài dìtiě lǐ, dànshì tā de xīn yǐjīng qù le hěn yuǎn de dìfang. Wǒ juéde zhè bǐjiào làngmàn.
text_en: Every morning on the subway, I notice a girl reading in the same car. She's probably in her twenties, carrying a different book each day. The subway is crowded, but she always finds a corner, makes herself small, and reads quietly. I admire her — everyone around is on their phones, only she reads paper books. Once the subway suddenly stopped and the car got noisy. But she seemed to hear nothing, still reading. I couldn't help glancing at the book title — it was about travel. Maybe her body is stuck in the subway, but her heart has already gone somewhere far away. I find that rather romantic.

questions.q_en: Does the girl carry the same book every day?

questions.q_en: What was she reading?

title: The Night Market Closing
text_zh: 夜市最热闹的时候是晚上八九点，但是我比较喜欢十一点的夜市——收摊的时候。那个时候，卖烤串的大叔把没卖完的几串送给旁边卖水果的阿姨。卖水果的阿姨把最后几个苹果分给帮忙收拾桌子的年轻人。大家一边收摊一边聊天，说今天生意好不好，明天天气怎么样。有一个卖面的老板，每天收摊以后都会把地扫得很干净。有人问他为什么，他说：「明天还要在这里做生意，把地方弄干净，是对这条街的尊重。」夜市的灯一盏一盏地关了，但是那些人和人之间的温暖，比灯还亮。
text_pinyin: Yèshì zuì rènao de shíhou shì wǎnshang bā jiǔ diǎn, dànshì wǒ bǐjiào xǐhuan shíyī diǎn de yèshì——shōu tān de shíhou. Nàge shíhou, mài kǎo chuàn de dàshū bǎ méi mài wán de jǐ chuàn sòng gěi pángbiān mài shuǐguǒ de āyí. Mài shuǐguǒ de āyí bǎ zuìhòu jǐ gè píngguǒ fēn gěi bāngmáng shōushi zhuōzi de niánqīng rén. Dàjiā yìbiān shōu tān yìbiān liáotiān, shuō jīntiān shēngyì hǎo bu hǎo, míngtiān tiānqì zěnmeyàng. Yǒu yí gè mài miàn de lǎobǎn, měi tiān shōu tān yǐhòu dōu huì bǎ dì sǎo de hěn gānjìng. Yǒu rén wèn tā wèishénme, tā shuō:「Míngtiān hái yào zài zhèlǐ zuò shēngyì, bǎ dìfang nòng gānjìng, shì duì zhè tiáo jiē de zūnzhòng.」Yèshì de dēng yì zhǎn yì zhǎn de guān le, dànshì nàxiē rén hé rén zhījiān de wēnnuǎn, bǐ dēng hái liàng.
text_en: The night market is liveliest around eight or nine, but I prefer it at eleven — closing time. That's when the kebab uncle gives his unsold skewers to the fruit auntie next to him. The fruit auntie shares her last apples with the young people helping clean up tables. Everyone chats while packing up — how business was today, what the weather will be tomorrow. One noodle vendor sweeps his spot perfectly clean after closing every night. Someone asked why. He said: 'I'll do business here again tomorrow. Keeping the place clean is respect for this street.' The night market lights go off one by one, but the warmth between people shines brighter than the lights.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话你能了解到人物什么？
questions.q_en: What can you learn about the character from this passage?

options.text: 他/她是一个有感情的人
options.pinyin: tā shì yí gè yǒu gǎnqíng de rén
options.text_en: they are a person with feelings

questions.explanation: The narrator prefers the night market at closing time (eleven PM) and notices the vendors' quiet rituals — this sensitivity to overlooked moments reveals someone with deep emotional awareness.

questions.q_zh: 这个场景给你的第一印象是什么？
questions.q_en: What is your first impression of this scene?

questions.q_en: Why does the noodle vendor sweep clean every day?

questions.explanation: 他说 把地方收拾干净，是对这条街的尊重 — he sees cleaning up as showing respect for the street itself, not just a practical chore.

title: The Quiet One in the Group
text_zh: 每次朋友聚会，我都是最安静的那个人。别人在聊天、唱歌的时候，我喜欢坐在旁边听。有人觉得我不开心，但其实不是。我只是比较喜欢听别人说话。我能听出来谁今天特别高兴，谁最近有心事。有一次聚会结束以后，朋友小美跟我说：「你知道吗？每次聚会我都会看你在不在。因为你虽然不怎么说话，但是你在的时候，我觉得比较安心。」我听了以后很感动。也许安静不是一个问题，而是另一种陪伴。不是所有的关心都需要被说出来。有时候，在场就够了。
text_pinyin: Měi cì péngyou jùhuì, wǒ dōu shì zuì ānjìng de nàge rén. Biéren zài liáotiān, chàng gē de shíhou, wǒ xǐhuan zuò zài pángbiān tīng. Yǒu rén juéde wǒ bù kāixīn, dàn qíshí búshì. Wǒ zhǐshì bǐjiào xǐhuan tīng biéren shuō huà. Wǒ néng tīng chūlái shéi jīntiān tèbié gāoxìng, shéi zuìjìn yǒu xīnshì. Yǒu yí cì jùhuì jiéshù yǐhòu, péngyou Xiǎo Měi gēn wǒ shuō:「Nǐ zhīdào ma? Měi cì jùhuì wǒ dōu huì kàn nǐ zài bú zài. Yīnwèi nǐ suīrán bù zěnme shuō huà, dànshì nǐ zài de shíhou, wǒ juéde bǐjiào ānxīn.」Wǒ tīng le yǐhòu hěn gǎndòng. Yěxǔ ānjìng búshì yí gè wèntí, ér shì lìng yì zhǒng péibàn. Búshì suǒyǒu de guānxīn dōu xūyào bèi shuō chūlái. Yǒu shíhou, zài chǎng jiù gòu le.
text_en: At every friend gathering, I'm the quietest person. When others chat and sing, I like to sit nearby and listen. Some think I'm unhappy, but that's not it. I just rather prefer listening to others talk. I can tell who's especially happy today, who has something on their mind recently. Once after a gathering, my friend Xiao Mei told me: 'You know what? Every time we get together, I check whether you're there. Because even though you barely speak, when you're there, I feel more at ease.' I was very touched. Maybe being quiet isn't a problem — it's another kind of companionship. Not all care needs to be spoken. Sometimes, being present is enough.

questions.q_zh: 这个场景给你的第一印象是什么？
questions.q_en: What is your first impression of this scene?
questions.explanation: The passage describes at every friend gathering, i'm the quietest person, which reveals that feeling.

questions.q_zh: 小美说「我」在的时候她觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does Xiao Mei feel when 'I' is present?
questions.explanation: Their behavior — because even though you barely speak, when you're there, i feel more at ease — shows that attitude.

questions.q_zh: 这个场景给你的第一印象是什么？
questions.q_en: What is your first impression of this scene?
questions.explanation: Their behavior — at every friend gathering, i'm the quietest person — shows that attitude.

title: The Disappearing Lunch
text_zh: 办公室的冰箱里，我的午饭被人吃了。这已经是这个星期第三次了。我把饭盒上写了名字，还是没有用。同事们都说不是他们。我决定做一个实验：我买了两份一样的午饭，一份放在冰箱里，一份放在自己桌子的抽屉里。中午的时候，冰箱里的又不见了。我去看了一下垃圾桶，发现了一个空的饭盒——上面有一家外卖店的标志。那家店只有实习生小刘经常点。我去找小刘，他红着脸说：「对不起，我以为冰箱里的东西是公司的。」从那以后，我每天多带一份饭，给小刘一份。他比较不好意思，但是也开始带午饭了。
text_pinyin: Bàngōngshì de bīngxiāng lǐ, wǒ de wǔfàn bèi rén chī le. Zhè yǐjīng shì zhège xīngqī dì-sān cì le. Wǒ bǎ fànhé shàng xiě le míngzi, háishi méiyǒu yòng. Tóngshìmen dōu shuō búshì tāmen. Wǒ juédìng zuò yí gè shíyàn: wǒ mǎi le liǎng fèn yíyàng de wǔfàn, yí fèn fàng zài bīngxiāng lǐ, yí fèn fàng zài zìjǐ zhuōzi de chōuti lǐ. Zhōngwǔ de shíhou, bīngxiāng lǐ de yòu bújiàn le. Wǒ qù kàn le yíxià lājī tǒng, fāxiàn le yí gè kōng de fànhé——shàngmiàn yǒu yì jiā wàimài diàn de biāozhì. Nà jiā diàn zhǐyǒu shíxíshēng Xiǎo Liú jīngcháng diǎn. Wǒ qù zhǎo Xiǎo Liú, tā hóngzhe liǎn shuō:「Duìbuqǐ, wǒ yǐwéi bīngxiāng lǐ de dōngxi shì gōngsī de.」Cóng nà yǐhòu, wǒ měi tiān duō dài yí fèn fàn, gěi Xiǎo Liú yí fèn. Tā bǐjiào bù hǎoyìsi, dànshì yě kāishǐ dài wǔfàn le.
text_en: Someone ate my lunch from the office fridge. That was the third time this week. I wrote my name on the container — still no use. All colleagues denied it. I decided to run an experiment: I bought two identical lunches, put one in the fridge and one in my desk drawer. At noon, the fridge one was gone again. I checked the trash and found an empty container with a delivery shop's logo — a shop only intern Xiao Liu regularly ordered from. I went to find Xiao Liu. He said, blushing: 'Sorry, I thought fridge stuff was for the company.' After that, I started bringing an extra lunch for Xiao Liu every day. He was rather embarrassed, but he started bringing his own lunch too.

title: The Tea House Rule
text_zh: 老城区有一家很旧的茶馆，里面有一个规矩：不能看手机。老板在门口贴了一张纸，上面写着：「进了这扇门，请把手机放下。」刚开始很多人觉得不方便，但是坐下来以后，发现没有手机的时候，人和人之间的距离比较近了。我第一次去的时候，旁边坐着一个老大爷。他一边喝茶一边跟我说了很多他年轻时候的故事。我听了一个下午，觉得比看一天手机有意思得多。老板说：「以前的茶馆就是这样的——大家来喝茶，也来听故事。我只是想把那种感觉找回来。」除了茶以外，这家店卖的其实是慢下来的时间。
text_pinyin: Lǎo chéngqū yǒu yì jiā hěn jiù de cháguǎn, lǐmiàn yǒu yí gè guījǔ: bù néng kàn shǒujī. Lǎobǎn zài ménkǒu tiē le yì zhāng zhǐ, shàngmiàn xiězhe:「Jìn le zhè shàn mén, qǐng bǎ shǒujī fàng xià.」Gāng kāishǐ hěn duō rén juéde bù fāngbiàn, dànshì zuò xiàlái yǐhòu, fāxiàn méiyǒu shǒujī de shíhou, rén hé rén zhījiān de jùlí bǐjiào jìn le. Wǒ dì-yī cì qù de shíhou, pángbiān zuòzhe yí gè lǎo dàye. Tā yìbiān hē chá yìbiān gēn wǒ shuō le hěn duō tā niánqīng shíhou de gùshi. Wǒ tīng le yí gè xiàwǔ, juéde bǐ kàn yì tiān shǒujī yǒuyìsi de duō. Lǎobǎn shuō:「Yǐqián de cháguǎn jiùshì zhèyàng de——dàjiā lái hē chá, yě lái tīng gùshi. Wǒ zhǐshì xiǎng bǎ nà zhǒng gǎnjué zhǎo huílái.」Chúle chá yǐwài, zhè jiā diàn mài de qíshí shì màn xiàlái de shíjiān.
text_en: In the old district there's a old tea house with one rule: no phones. The owner posted a note at the door: 'Once through this door, please put your phone down.' At first many found it inconvenient, but after sitting down, they discovered that without phones, the distance between people got rather closer. My first time there, an old gentleman sat beside me. He sipped tea while telling me many stories from his youth. I listened all afternoon and found it far more interesting than a day on my phone. The owner said: 'Tea houses used to be like this — people came for tea and for stories. I just want to bring that feeling back.' Besides tea, what this shop really sells is slowed-down time.

questions.q_zh: 茶馆有什么规矩？
questions.q_en: What's the tea house rule?
questions.explanation: 老城区有一家老茶馆，有一个规矩：不能用手机 — the tea house's one rule is simply 'no phones.'

questions.q_zh: 没有手机以后，人和人之间怎么样了？
questions.q_en: What happened between people without phones?

options.pinyin: dàjiā dōu bù shuō huà le

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「距离比较近了」, indicating the distance got closer.

questions.q_zh: 为什么作者注意到这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why does the author notice these details?

options.text: 这些细节揭示了更深的意义
options.pinyin: zhèxiē xìjié jiēshì le gèng shēn de yìyì

questions.explanation: The no-phone rule forces customers to taste the tea, hear conversations, and notice the old wooden furniture — these sensory details reveal how slowing down uncovers meaning in a simple setting.

title: The Sleeping Taxi Driver
text_zh: 有一次我打车去机场，上车以后发现司机的副驾驶座上放着一个枕头和一条毯子。我问他：「你是不是经常在车里睡觉？」他说：「我每天从早上五点开到晚上十二点。中间累了就在车里睡半个小时。」我有点心疼，问他：「为什么要开这么长时间？」他笑着说：「女儿今年要考大学，学费比较贵。她成绩很好，我不想因为钱的问题让她去不了好学校。」到了机场，我把车费给他，他找了钱。我说：「不用找了。」他摇摇头说：「该多少就是多少。我女儿说，做人要诚实。」那天我一直在想那个枕头——它被压得很扁了，但是很干净。
text_pinyin: Yǒu yí cì wǒ dǎchē qù jīchǎng, shàng chē yǐhòu fāxiàn sījī de fù jiàshǐ zuò shàng fàngzhe yí gè zhěntou hé yì tiáo tǎnzi. Wǒ wèn tā:「Nǐ shì bú shì jīngcháng zài chē lǐ shuì jiào?」Tā shuō:「Wǒ měi tiān cóng zǎoshang wǔ diǎn kāi dào wǎnshang shí'èr diǎn. Zhōngjiān lèi le jiù zài chē lǐ shuì bàn gè xiǎoshí.」Wǒ yǒudiǎn xīnténg, wèn tā:「Wèishénme yào kāi zhème cháng shíjiān?」Tā xiàozhe shuō:「Nǚ'ér jīnnián yào kǎo dàxué, xuéfèi bǐjiào guì. Tā chéngjì hěn hǎo, wǒ bù xiǎng yīnwèi qián de wèntí ràng tā qù bù liǎo hǎo xuéxiào.」Dào le jīchǎng, wǒ bǎ chē fèi gěi tā, tā zhǎo le qián. Wǒ shuō:「Bú yòng zhǎo le.」Tā yáoyao tóu shuō:「Gāi duōshao jiùshì duōshao. Wǒ nǚ'ér shuō, zuò rén yào chéngshí.」Nà tiān wǒ yìzhí zài xiǎng nàge zhěntou——tā bèi yā de hěn biǎn le, dànshì hěn gānjìng.
text_en: I took a taxi to the airport. After getting in, I noticed a pillow and blanket on the passenger seat. I asked: 'Do you often sleep in the car?' He said: 'I drive from five in the morning to midnight. When I'm tired, I nap for half an hour.' I asked why. He smiled: 'My daughter is taking the university entrance exam. Tuition is expensive. She has great grades — I don't want money to keep her from a good school.' At the airport, I said: 'Keep the change.' He shook his head: 'The fare is what it is. My daughter says: be honest.' That pillow was pressed flat, but clean.

questions.q_zh: 司机的副驾驶座上有什么？
questions.q_en: What was on the passenger seat?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「枕头和毯子」, indicating a pillow and blanket.

questions.q_zh: 司机为什么开那么长时间的车？
questions.q_en: Why does the driver work such long hours?

options.text_en: wants to earn tuition for his daughter

questions.explanation: 他笑了：'我女儿要考大学' — he works long hours because his daughter is preparing for the university entrance exam and he wants to earn her tuition.

questions.q_zh: 司机为什么不要多的钱？
questions.q_en: Why didn't the driver keep the extra money?

options.pinyin: nǚ'ér shuō zuò rén yào chéngshí

questions.explanation: 女儿说：做人要老实 — his daughter's principle of honesty is why he returned the extra money rather than keeping it.

title: The Stray Dog and the Baker
text_zh: 面包店门口有一只流浪狗，每天下午都来。它不叫，也不进去，就安安静静地趴在门口。面包师每天下午三点会拿一个卖不出去的面包出来，放在地上。狗闻一闻，慢慢吃完，然后走了。邻居问面包师：「你每天都喂它吗？」面包师说：「它从来不进来吵我，也不去翻垃圾桶。它比很多人都有礼貌。我觉得它值得被好好对待。」有一天下雨了，面包师把一个纸箱子放在门口，让狗可以躲雨。那只狗进了箱子以后，回头看了面包师一眼。面包师说：「你看，它比较懂得感谢。」
text_pinyin: Miànbāo diàn ménkǒu yǒu yì zhī liúlàng gǒu, měi tiān xiàwǔ dōu lái. Tā bú jiào, yě bú jìnqù, jiù ān'ānjìngjìng de pā zài ménkǒu. Miànbāoshī měi tiān xiàwǔ sān diǎn huì ná yí gè mài bù chūqù de miànbāo chūlái, fàng zài dì shàng. Gǒu wén yì wén, mànman chī wán, ránhòu zǒu le. Línjū wèn miànbāoshī:「Nǐ měi tiān dōu wèi tā ma?」Miànbāoshī shuō:「Tā cónglái bú jìnlái chǎo wǒ, yě bú qù fān lājī tǒng. Tā bǐ hěn duō rén dōu yǒu lǐmào. Wǒ juéde tā zhíde bèi hǎohǎo duìdài.」Yǒu yì tiān xià yǔ le, miànbāoshī bǎ yí gè zhǐ xiāngzi fàng zài ménkǒu, ràng gǒu kěyǐ duǒ yǔ. Nà zhī gǒu jìn le xiāngzi yǐhòu, huítóu kàn le miànbāoshī yì yǎn. Miànbāoshī shuō:「Nǐ kàn, tā bǐjiào dǒngde gǎnxiè.」
text_en: A stray dog comes to the bakery door every afternoon. It doesn't bark or go inside — just lies quietly at the entrance. Every day at three, the baker brings out an unsold bread roll. The dog sniffs it, eats slowly, then leaves. A neighbor asked: 'Do you feed it every day?' The baker said: 'It never comes in to bother me, never rummages through trash. It's more polite than many people. It deserves to be treated well.' One rainy day, the baker put a cardboard box at the door for shelter. After the dog went in, it turned back and looked at the baker.

questions.q_zh: 你觉得这个人以后会怎样？
questions.q_en: What do you think will happen to this person in the future?

options.pinyin: huì bān dào bié de dìfang

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「继续这样平静地生活」, indicating continue living peacefully like this.

questions.q_zh: 面包师为什么喂它？
questions.q_en: Why does the baker feed it?

options.text: 因为觉得它有礼貌，值得被好好对待
options.pinyin: yīnwèi juéde tā yǒu lǐmào, zhíde bèi hǎohǎo duìdài
options.text_en: thinks it's polite and deserves good treatment

options.pinyin: yīnwèi línjū ràng tā wèi de

questions.explanation: 因为 (because) directly states the reason: thinks it's polite and deserves good treatment.

questions.q_zh: 下雨的时候面包师做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the baker do when it rained?

options.text: 把纸箱子放在门口让狗躲雨
options.pinyin: bǎ zhǐ xiāngzi fàng zài ménkǒu ràng gǒu duǒ yǔ
options.text_en: put a cardboard box at the door for shelter

questions.explanation: 有一天下雨，面包师在门口放了一个纸箱子给它挡雨 — the baker placed a cardboard box at the door to shelter the dog from rain.

title: The Automatic Reply
text_zh: 我给老板发了一条消息，问他明天的会议几点开始。他回了一个字：「好。」我不知道他是说好还是在忙。我又问了一遍：「是上午九点还是十点？」他又回了：「好。」我开始觉得比较奇怪。我打电话给他，他没有接。半个小时以后，他打电话回来说：「不好意思，我手机掉水里了，现在只能按到一个键。」我问他：「那你刚才怎么回的消息？」他说：「我设了自动回复，不管别人说什么，都回'好'。」我笑了半天。这大概是最没有用的自动回复了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ gěi lǎobǎn fā le yì tiáo xiāoxi, wèn tā míngtiān de huìyì jǐ diǎn kāishǐ. Tā huí le yí gè zì:「Hǎo.」Wǒ bù zhīdào tā shì shuō hǎo háishì zài máng. Wǒ yòu wèn le yí biàn:「Shì shàngwǔ jiǔ diǎn háishì shí diǎn?」Tā yòu huí le:「Hǎo.」Wǒ kāishǐ juéde bǐjiào qíguài. Wǒ dǎ diànhuà gěi tā, tā méiyǒu jiē. Bàn gè xiǎoshí yǐhòu, tā dǎ diànhuà huílái shuō:「Bù hǎoyìsi, wǒ shǒujī diào shuǐ lǐ le, xiànzài zhǐ néng àn dào yí gè jiàn.」Wǒ wèn tā:「Nà nǐ gāngcái zěnme huí de xiāoxi?」Tā shuō:「Wǒ shè le zìdòng huífù, bùguǎn biéren shuō shénme, dōu huí 'hǎo'.」Wǒ xiào le bàntiān. Zhè dàgài shì zuì méiyǒu yòng de zìdòng huífù le.
text_en: I messaged my boss asking what time tomorrow's meeting starts. He replied with one word: 'OK.' I didn't know if he meant OK or was just busy. I asked again: 'Nine or ten in the morning?' He replied: 'OK.' I started feeling rather confused. I called him — no answer. Half an hour later he called back: 'Sorry, my phone fell in water. I can only press one button now.' I asked: 'Then how did you reply to messages?' He said: 'I set up auto-reply. No matter what anyone says, it replies OK.' I laughed for ages. That's probably the most useless auto-reply ever.

questions.q_zh: 老板为什么只回「好」？
questions.q_en: Why did the boss only reply 'OK'?

options.text: 手机掉水里了，设了自动回复
options.pinyin: shǒujī diào shuǐ lǐ le, shè le zìdòng huífù
options.text_en: phone fell in water, set auto-reply

questions.explanation: The key phrase 「手机掉水里了，」 gives the reason — phone fell in water, set auto-reply.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的语气/氛围是怎样的？
questions.q_en: What is the tone/atmosphere of this passage?
questions.explanation: The words and actions (i messaged my boss asking what time tomorrow's meeting starts) point to that.

questions.q_zh: 为什么这段话值得仔细读第二遍？
questions.q_en: Why is this passage worth reading a second time?

options.text: 能发现更多隐含的意义
options.pinyin: néng fāxiàn gèng duō yǐnhán de yìyì
options.text_en: you can discover more hidden meanings

options.text: 内容太复杂，一遍读不懂
options.pinyin: nèiróng tài fùzá, yí biàn dú bù dǒng
options.text_en: it's too complex to understand in one reading

options.pinyin: bù zhíde dú dì èr biàn

questions.explanation: The humor of the boss's ambiguous one-word replies (OK, 嗯, a period) rewards re-reading because each message layer reveals more about miscommunication patterns.

title: The Cooking Competition
text_zh: 我和室友决定比赛做饭。规则很简单：每人做一个菜，让楼下的保安大叔来当裁判。我做了一个番茄炒蛋，味道还可以，但是鸡蛋炒得比较碎。室友做了一个酸辣土豆丝，看起来不错，但是他忘了放盐。保安大叔先吃了我的，点了点头。又吃了室友的，喝了一大口水。我以为自己赢了。但是保安大叔说：「两个都不怎么好吃。不过你们请我吃了饭，所以我两个都给满分。」室友不服气地说：「那到底谁赢了？」保安大叔笑着说：「我赢了——我不用做饭就吃了两个菜。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ hé shìyǒu juédìng bǐsài zuò fàn. Guīzé hěn jiǎndān: měi rén zuò yí gè cài, ràng lóu xià de bǎo'ān dàshū lái dāng cáipàn. Wǒ zuò le yí gè fānqié chǎo dàn, wèidào hái kěyǐ, dànshì jīdàn chǎo de bǐjiào suì. Shìyǒu zuò le yí gè suānlà tǔdòu sī, kàn qǐlái búcuò, dànshì tā wàng le fàng yán. Bǎo'ān dàshū xiān chī le wǒ de, diǎn le diǎn tóu. Yòu chī le shìyǒu de, hē le yí dà kǒu shuǐ. Wǒ yǐwéi zìjǐ yíng le. Dànshì bǎo'ān dàshū shuō:「Liǎng gè dōu bù zěnme hǎochī. Búguò nǐmen qǐng wǒ chī le fàn, suǒyǐ wǒ liǎng gè dōu gěi mǎn fēn.」Shìyǒu bù fúqì de shuō:「Nà dàodǐ shéi yíng le?」Bǎo'ān dàshū xiàozhe shuō:「Wǒ yíng le——wǒ bú yòng zuò fàn jiù chī le liǎng gè cài.」
text_en: My roommate and I decided to have a cooking competition. Simple rules: each makes one dish, and the security guard downstairs judges. I made scrambled eggs with tomato — tasted OK, but the eggs were rather crumbly. My roommate made hot and sour shredded potatoes — looked good, but he forgot the salt. The guard ate mine first and nodded. Then ate my roommate's and took a big gulp of water. I thought I'd won. But the guard said: 'Neither is that great. But you treated me to a meal, so I give both full marks.' My roommate said indignantly: 'Then who won?' The guard smiled: 'I won — I ate two dishes without cooking.'

questions.q_zh: 室友的菜有什么问题？
questions.q_en: What was wrong with the roommate's dish?

options.pinyin: tǔdòu qiē de tài dà le

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「忘了放盐」, indicating forgot to add salt.

questions.q_zh: 保安大叔给了什么分数？
questions.q_en: What score did the guard give?

options.pinyin: wǒ mǎn fēn, shìyǒu líng fēn
options.text_en: full marks for me, zero for roommate

options.pinyin: liǎng gè dōu gěi le mǎn fēn
liǎng gè dōu gěi le líng fēn

questions.explanation: The guard says 但是你请我吃饭了，所以两个都是满分 — he gave full marks to both dishes because the meal was a treat, not because of the cooking quality.

questions.q_zh: 保安大叔说谁赢了？
questions.q_en: Who did the guard say won?
questions.explanation: 「保安大叔自己」 tells you who — the guard claimed he himself was the real winner.

title: The Museum's Quiet Room
text_zh: 我们城市的博物馆最近做了一件有意思的事情——在二楼开了一个「安静房间」。这个房间里什么展品都没有，只有几把椅子和一扇大窗户。墙上写着：「这个房间是给需要安静的人准备的。」刚开始大家觉得很奇怪：博物馆为什么要有一个空房间？但是越来越多的人开始去那里坐一坐。有人在那里看窗外的天空，有人闭着眼睛休息，有人只是发呆。馆长说：「除了看展品以外，我们觉得人也需要一个什么都不做的地方。」那个房间比较小，但是它给了很多人一个在城市里停下来的机会。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen chéngshì de bówùguǎn zuìjìn zuò le yí jiàn yǒuyìsi de shìqing——zài èr lóu kāi le yí gè「ānjìng fángjiān」. Zhège fángjiān lǐ shénme zhǎnpǐn dōu méiyǒu, zhǐyǒu jǐ bǎ yǐzi hé yí shàn dà chuānghu. Qiáng shàng xiězhe:「Zhège fángjiān shì gěi xūyào ānjìng de rén zhǔnbèi de.」Gāng kāishǐ dàjiā juéde hěn qíguài: bówùguǎn wèishénme yào yǒu yí gè kōng fángjiān? Dànshì yuè lái yuè duō de rén kāishǐ qù nàlǐ zuò yí zuò. Yǒu rén zài nàlǐ kàn chuāng wài de tiānkōng, yǒu rén bìzhe yǎnjīng xiūxi, yǒu rén zhǐshì fādāi. Guǎnzhǎng shuō:「Chúle kàn zhǎnpǐn yǐwài, wǒmen juéde rén yě xūyào yí gè shénme dōu bú zuò de dìfang.」Nàge fángjiān bǐjiào xiǎo, dànshì tā gěi le hěn duō rén yí gè zài chéngshì lǐ tíng xiàlái de jīhuì.
text_en: Our city's museum recently did something interesting — opened a 'quiet room' on the second floor. There are no exhibits in it, just a few chairs and a large window. On the wall it says: 'This room is for people who need quiet.' At first everyone found it strange: why would a museum have an empty room? But more and more people started going to sit there. Some watch the sky through the window, some rest with eyes closed, some just space out. The director said: 'Besides viewing exhibits, we think people also need a place to do nothing.' That room is rather small, but it gave many people a chance to pause in the city.

questions.q_zh: 安静房间里有什么？
questions.q_en: What's in the quiet room?

options.text: 几把椅子和一扇大窗户
options.pinyin: jǐ bǎ yǐzi hé yí shàn dà chuānghu
options.text_en: a few chairs and a large window

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「几把椅子和一扇大窗户」, indicating a few chairs and a large window.

questions.q_zh: 人们在那里做什么？
questions.q_en: What do people do there?

options.text_en: watch the sky, rest, space out

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「看天空、休息、发呆」, indicating watch the sky, rest, space out.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的'安静'不只是物理上的安静，还代表什么？
questions.q_en: The 'quiet' in this passage is not just physical silence — what else does it represent?
questions.explanation: The museum's 'quiet room' has no exhibits, just chairs and a window with the sign 这个房间是用来安静的 — the silence isn't just absence of noise, it represents a deliberate space for inner peace.

title: The Corner Bookstore
text_zh: 街角有一家很小的书店，门口的牌子上写着：「只卖旧书。」老板是个五十多岁的女人，她说每一本旧书都被人翻过，里面留着上一个读者的痕迹——有人在书里夹了一张照片，有人在旁边写了一句话。她觉得这比新书有意思。我在那里买过一本旧小说，里面夹着一张电影票。电影票上的日期是十五年前的。我一边看书一边想：十五年前看这本书的人，现在在做什么呢？那家书店比较暗，书架上的灯也不太亮，但是每次走进去，都觉得时间慢了下来。除了书以外，那里卖的是别人的故事和记忆。
text_pinyin: Jiējiǎo yǒu yì jiā hěn xiǎo de shūdiàn, ménkǒu de páizi shàng xiězhe:「Zhǐ mài jiù shū.」Lǎobǎn shì ge wǔshí duō suì de nǚrén, tā shuō měi yì běn jiù shū dōu bèi rén fān guò, lǐmiàn liúzhe shàng yí gè dúzhě de hénjì——yǒu rén zài shū lǐ jiā le yì zhāng zhàopiàn, yǒu rén zài pángbiān xiě le yí jù huà. Tā juéde zhè bǐ xīn shū yǒuyìsi. Wǒ zài nàlǐ mǎi guò yì běn jiù xiǎoshuō, lǐmiàn jiāzhe yì zhāng diànyǐng piào. Diànyǐng piào shàng de rìqī shì shíwǔ nián qián de. Wǒ yìbiān kàn shū yìbiān xiǎng: shíwǔ nián qián kàn zhè běn shū de rén, xiànzài zài zuò shénme ne? Nà jiā shūdiàn bǐjiào àn, shūjià shàng de dēng yě bú tài liàng, dànshì měi cì zǒu jìnqù, dōu juéde shíjiān màn le xiàlái. Chúle shū yǐwài, nàlǐ mài de shì biéren de gùshi hé jìyì.
text_en: On the street corner there's a tiny bookstore. The sign says: 'Only used books.' The owner, a woman in her fifties, says every used book has been touched by someone — someone tucked in a photo, someone wrote a note in the margin. She finds this more interesting than new books. I once bought a used novel with a movie ticket inside. The date was fifteen years ago. I wondered: what is the person who read this fifteen years ago doing now? The store is dim, but every time you walk in, time seems to slow down. That shop sells other people's stories.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么样的变化？
questions.q_en: What kind of change does this passage suggest?
questions.explanation: The bookstore owner says each used book carries someone's traces — photos, notes, underlines — suggesting that encountering others' inner lives through books catalyzes the reader's own inner growth.

questions.q_zh: 「我」买的旧书里夹着什么？
questions.q_en: What was inside the used book 'I' bought?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「一张电影票」, indicating a movie ticket.

questions.q_zh: 老板觉得这家店除了书以外卖的是什么？
questions.q_en: Besides books, what does the owner think the shop sells?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「别人的故事和记忆」, indicating others' stories and memories.

title: The Handwritten Sign
text_zh: 小区门口有一个卖水果的老太太。她不识字，但是她的水果旁边总是放着一个手写的牌子。那些字是她孙子帮她写的。每天早上孙子上学之前，会帮她把今天的水果价格写在一张纸板上。字写得歪歪扭扭的，但是很认真。有一天下雨了，纸板被淋湿了，字看不清了。老太太比较着急，因为她不知道价格怎么写。旁边一个买菜的阿姨说：「没关系，我帮你写。」她拿了一支笔，把价格重新写了一遍。老太太谢了又谢。晚上孙子放学回来，看到牌子上的字不是自己写的，问：「奶奶，今天谁帮你写的？」老太太说：「一个好心的人。」
text_pinyin: Xiǎoqū ménkǒu yǒu yí gè mài shuǐguǒ de lǎo tàitai. Tā bú shì zì, dànshì tā de shuǐguǒ pángbiān zǒng shì fàngzhe yí gè shǒuxiě de páizi. Nàxiē zì shì tā sūnzi bāng tā xiě de. Měi tiān zǎoshang sūnzi shàng xué zhīqián, huì bāng tā bǎ jīntiān de shuǐguǒ jiàgé xiě zài yì zhāng zhǐbǎn shàng. Zì xiě de wāiwāiniǔniǔ de, dànshì hěn rènzhēn. Yǒu yì tiān xià yǔ le, zhǐbǎn bèi lín shī le, zì kàn bù qīng le. Lǎo tàitai bǐjiào zhāojí, yīnwèi tā bù zhīdào jiàgé zěnme xiě. Pángbiān yí gè mǎi cài de āyí shuō:「Méi guānxi, wǒ bāng nǐ xiě.」Tā ná le yì zhī bǐ, bǎ jiàgé chóngxīn xiě le yí biàn. Lǎo tàitai xiè le yòu xiè. Wǎnshang sūnzi fàng xué huílái, kàn dào páizi shàng de zì búshì zìjǐ xiě de, wèn:「Nǎinai, jīntiān shéi bāng nǐ xiě de?」Lǎo tàitai shuō:「Yí gè hǎoxīn de rén.」
text_en: At the complex entrance there's an old woman selling fruit. She can't read, but there's always a handwritten sign by her fruit. Her grandson writes it. Every morning before school, he writes today's prices on a piece of cardboard. The handwriting is crooked but earnest. One rainy day the cardboard got soaked and the writing became illegible. The old woman was rather worried since she didn't know how to write the prices. A woman buying vegetables nearby said: 'Don't worry, I'll write for you.' She took a pen and rewrote the prices. The old woman thanked her again and again. When her grandson came back from school that evening and saw the writing wasn't his, he asked: 'Grandma, who helped you write today?' She said: 'A kind person.'

questions.q_zh: 牌子上的字是谁写的？
questions.q_en: Who writes the sign?
questions.explanation: The passage identifies this by saying her grandson writes it.

questions.q_zh: 下雨以后牌子怎么了？
questions.q_en: What happened to the sign after it rained?

options.pinyin: bèi lín shī le, zì kàn bù qīng le

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「被淋湿了，字看不清了」, indicating got soaked, writing illegible.

questions.q_zh: 谁帮老太太重新写了价格？
questions.q_en: Who rewrote the prices?

options.pinyin: yí gè mǎi cài de āyí

questions.explanation: The passage identifies this by saying a woman buying vegetables nearby said: 'don't worry, i'll write for you.

title: The Photograph on the Fridge
text_zh: 我家冰箱上贴着一张很旧的照片。照片里是我三岁的时候，坐在爸爸肩膀上，笑得很开心。妈妈说那天是我第一次看到大海。我现在看了无数次海了，但是那张照片里的笑容，我再也做不出来了。每次我打开冰箱拿东西的时候，都会看到那张照片。有时候我会想：长大是不是就是慢慢忘记怎么笑的过程？后来我把那张照片拿下来，放在了自己房间的桌子上。不是因为不想看，而是因为我想把它放在一个比较重要的地方。冰箱每天被打开很多次，但有些照片值得你停下来，好好看一看。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā bīngxiāng shàng tiēzhe yì zhāng hěn jiù de zhàopiàn. Zhàopiàn lǐ shì wǒ sān suì de shíhou, zuò zài bàba jiānbǎng shàng, xiào de hěn kāixīn. Māma shuō nà tiān shì wǒ dì-yī cì kàn dào dàhǎi. Wǒ xiànzài kàn le wúshù cì hǎi le, dànshì nà zhāng zhàopiàn lǐ de xiàoróng, wǒ zài yě zuò bù chūlái le. Měi cì wǒ dǎkāi bīngxiāng ná dōngxi de shíhou, dōu huì kàn dào nà zhāng zhàopiàn. Yǒu shíhou wǒ huì xiǎng: zhǎng dà shì bú shì jiùshì mànman wàngjì zěnme xiào de guòchéng? Hòulái wǒ bǎ nà zhāng zhàopiàn ná xiàlái, fàng zài le zìjǐ fángjiān de zhuōzi shàng. Búshì yīnwèi bù xiǎng kàn, ér shì yīnwèi wǒ xiǎng bǎ tā fàng zài yí gè bǐjiào zhòngyào de dìfang. Bīngxiāng měi tiān bèi dǎkāi hěn duō cì, dàn yǒuxiē zhàopiàn zhíde nǐ tíng xiàlái, hǎohǎo kàn yí kàn.
text_en: On our fridge there's a old photo. In it I'm three years old, sitting on Dad's shoulders, smiling with pure happiness. Mom says that was the first time I saw the ocean. I've seen the ocean countless times now, but the smile in that photo — I can never make it again. Every time I open the fridge, I see that photo. Sometimes I wonder: is growing up just the slow process of forgetting how to smile? Later I took the photo down and put it on my bedroom desk. Not because I didn't want to see it, but because I wanted to put it somewhere more important. The fridge gets opened many times a day, but some photos deserve you stopping to take a good look.

questions.q_zh: 根据这段话，人物接下来最可能做什么？
questions.q_en: Based on this passage, what would the character most likely do next?

options.pinyin: kāishǐ zuò wánquán bùtóng de shì

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「继续享受当下的时刻」, indicating continue enjoying the present moment.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么把照片从冰箱上拿下来？
questions.q_en: Why did 'I' take the photo off the fridge?

options.text: 想放在一个比较重要的地方
options.pinyin: xiǎng fàng zài yí gè bǐjiào zhòngyào de dìfang
options.text_en: wanted to put it somewhere more important

questions.explanation: 不是因为不想看，而是想把它放在一个更重要的地方 — not because of wanting to forget, but wanting to place it somewhere more significant, showing deeper attachment.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以推断人物是什么样的人？
questions.q_en: What kind of person can we infer the character is?
questions.explanation: The clue is that on our fridge there's a old photo — from there you can infer the answer.

title: The Lunch Box Note
text_zh: 我上大学的时候，妈妈每个星期会寄一个饭盒给我。饭盒里除了菜以外，总是有一张小纸条。纸条上写的都是很简单的话：「今天冷了，多穿一件。」「这个菜你小时候最爱吃。」「不要太晚睡。」我的室友们都很羡慕。有一次，纸条上写着：「妈妈把盐放多了，不好意思。」我尝了一口，确实比较咸。但是不知道为什么，那天的菜我觉得是最好吃的。因为那张纸条让我笑了。毕业以后，我把所有的纸条都收了起来，放在一个小盒子里。那些纸条被折得皱皱的，但是每一张上面都有妈妈的味道。
text_pinyin: Wǒ shàng dàxué de shíhou, māma měi gè xīngqī huì jì yí gè fànhé gěi wǒ. Fànhé lǐ chúle cài yǐwài, zǒng shì yǒu yì zhāng xiǎo zhǐtiáo. Zhǐtiáo shàng xiě de dōu shì hěn jiǎndān de huà:「Jīntiān lěng le, duō chuān yí jiàn.」「Zhège cài nǐ xiǎo shíhou zuì ài chī.」「Bú yào tài wǎn shuì.」Wǒ de shìyǒumen dōu hěn xiànmù. Yǒu yí cì, zhǐtiáo shàng xiězhe:「Māma bǎ yán fàng duō le, bù hǎoyìsi.」Wǒ cháng le yì kǒu, quèshí bǐjiào xián. Dànshì bù zhīdào wèishénme, nà tiān de cài wǒ juéde shì zuì hǎochī de. Yīnwèi nà zhāng zhǐtiáo ràng wǒ xiào le. Bìyè yǐhòu, wǒ bǎ suǒyǒu de zhǐtiáo dōu shōu le qǐlái, fàng zài yí gè xiǎo hézi lǐ. Nàxiē zhǐtiáo bèi zhé de zhòuzhòu de, dànshì měi yì zhāng shàngmiàn dōu yǒu māma de wèidào.
text_en: When I was in university, Mom sent me a lunch box every week. Besides food, there was always a little note inside. Simple things: 'It's cold today, wear an extra layer.' 'This dish was your childhood favorite.' 'Don't sleep too late.' My roommates were all envious. Once the note said: 'Mom added too much salt, sorry.' I took a bite — it was indeed rather salty. But for some reason, that day's food tasted the best to me. Because that note made me laugh. After graduating, I saved all the notes in a small box. They're creased from folding, but every one carries the scent of Mom.

questions.q_zh: 饭盒里除了菜还有什么？
questions.q_en: What was in the lunch box besides food?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「一张小纸条」, indicating a little note.

questions.q_zh: 那天菜太咸了，「我」为什么觉得最好吃？
questions.q_en: The food was too salty — why did 'I' think it tasted best?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi zhǐtiáo ràng wǒ xiào le

questions.explanation: 因为 (because) directly states the reason: the note made me laugh.

questions.q_zh: 毕业以后「我」怎么处理那些纸条？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' do with the notes after graduation?

options.text: 收起来放在一个小盒子里
options.pinyin: shōu qǐlái fàng zài yí gè xiǎo hézi lǐ
options.text_en: saved them in a small box

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「放在一个小盒子里」, indicating saved them in a small box.

title: The Haircut
text_zh: 我留了三年的长头发，今天剪掉了。理发师问我：「确定吗？长头发很好看。」我说：「确定。」剪完以后，我看着镜子里的自己，觉得有点陌生。同事们的反应也不一样——有人说好看，有人说可惜了。妈妈看到照片以后说：「这样比较精神。」但是她的表情告诉我，她更喜欢长头发的我。我为什么要剪？因为我觉得那个留长头发的「我」已经不是现在的我了。人有时候需要改变一个外在的东西，来告诉自己：我跟以前不一样了。虽然只是头发，但是我把一些旧的东西也一起剪掉了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ liú le sān nián de cháng tóufa, jīntiān jiǎn diào le. Lǐfàshī wèn wǒ:「Quèdìng ma? Cháng tóufa hěn hǎokàn.」Wǒ shuō:「Quèdìng.」Jiǎn wán yǐhòu, wǒ kànzhe jìngzi lǐ de zìjǐ, juéde yǒudiǎn mòshēng. Tóngshìmen de fǎnyìng yě bù yíyàng——yǒu rén shuō hǎokàn, yǒu rén shuō kěxī le. Māma kàn dào zhàopiàn yǐhòu shuō:「Zhèyàng bǐjiào jīngshen.」Dànshì tā de biǎoqíng gàosu wǒ, tā gèng xǐhuan cháng tóufa de wǒ. Wǒ wèishénme yào jiǎn? Yīnwèi wǒ juéde nàge liú cháng tóufa de wǒ yǐjīng búshì xiànzài de wǒ le. Rén yǒu shíhou xūyào gǎibiàn yí gè wàizài de dōngxi, lái gàosu zìjǐ: wǒ gēn yǐqián bù yíyàng le. Suīrán zhǐshì tóufa, dànshì wǒ bǎ yìxiē jiù de dōngxi yě yìqǐ jiǎn diào le.
text_en: I cut my long hair today, after growing it for three years. The stylist asked: 'Are you sure? Long hair looks great.' I said: 'Sure.' After the cut, I looked at myself in the mirror and felt a little unfamiliar. Colleagues reacted differently — some said it looked good, some said it was a shame. Mom saw the photo and said: 'This looks more energetic.' But her expression told me she preferred the long-haired me. Why did I cut it? Because the long-haired 'me' was no longer who I am now. Sometimes people need to change something external to tell themselves: I'm different from before. It was just hair, but I cut away some old things along with it.

questions.q_zh: 「我」留了多久的长头发？
questions.q_en: How long had 'I' grown long hair?
questions.explanation: The number 「三年」 answers directly — three years of growing long hair.

questions.q_zh: 妈妈的真实想法是什么？
questions.q_en: What was Mom's real feeling?
questions.explanation: Their behavior — i cut my long hair today, after growing it for three years — shows that attitude.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么要剪头发？
questions.q_en: Why did 'I' cut the hair?

options.text: 觉得长头发的自己已经不是现在的自己了
options.pinyin: juéde cháng tóufa de zìjǐ yǐjīng búshì xiànzài de zìjǐ le
options.text_en: the long-haired self isn't who I am now

questions.explanation: 因为长头发的「我」已经不是现在的我了 — the long-haired version no longer matched who she'd become, so cutting it was about aligning appearance with current identity.

title: The Night Music
text_zh: 最近每天晚上十点左右，我都能听到一阵很轻的钢琴声。声音从楼上传下来，弹的都是很慢很安静的曲子。我住的是老楼，隔音比较差，但是奇怪的是，其他邻居都说没听到。我开始怀疑是不是自己的问题。有一天我决定上去看看。我住五楼，六楼只有一户人家，是一个刚搬来的年轻人。我敲了门，他开了。我问他是不是在弹琴。他说：「是的，但是我用了耳机，你应该听不到才对。」我说：「但是我确实听到了。」他把我带到他的琴旁边——原来他的耳机线没有完全插进去，声音一直从钢琴的音箱里出来。他比较不好意思，但是我说：「没关系，我觉得比较好听。」
text_pinyin: Zuìjìn měi tiān wǎnshang shí diǎn zuǒyòu, wǒ dōu néng tīng dào yí zhèn hěn qīng de gāngqín shēng. Shēngyīn cóng lóu shàng chuán xiàlái, tán de dōu shì hěn màn hěn ānjìng de qǔzi. Wǒ zhù de shì lǎo lóu, géyīn bǐjiào chà, dànshì qíguài de shì, qítā línjū dōu shuō méi tīng dào. Wǒ kāishǐ huáiyí shì bú shì zìjǐ de wèntí. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ juédìng shàngqu kànkan. Wǒ zhù wǔ lóu, liù lóu zhǐyǒu yì hù rénjiā, shì yí gè gāng bān lái de niánqīng rén. Wǒ qiāo le mén, tā kāi le. Wǒ wèn tā shì bú shì zài tán qín. Tā shuō:「Shì de, dànshì wǒ yòng le ěrjī, nǐ yīnggāi tīng bú dào cái duì.」Wǒ shuō:「Dànshì wǒ quèshí tīng dào le.」Tā bǎ wǒ dài dào tā de qín pángbiān——yuánlái tā de ěrjī xiàn méiyǒu wánquán chā jìnqù, shēngyīn yìzhí cóng gāngqín de yīnxiāng lǐ chūlái. Tā bǐjiào bù hǎoyìsi, dànshì wǒ shuō:「Méi guānxi, wǒ juéde bǐjiào hǎo tīng.」
text_en: Every night around ten, I hear faint piano music from upstairs — always slow, quiet pieces. My building has poor soundproofing, but other neighbors say they don't hear it. One day I went to check. The sixth floor has only one household — a young person who just moved in. I knocked and asked if he was playing piano. He said: 'Yes, but I'm using headphones — you shouldn't hear it.' He took me to his piano — his headphone jack wasn't fully plugged in, so sound had been coming through the speakers all along. He was embarrassed, but I said: 'It sounds nice.'

questions.q_zh: 钢琴声是什么样的？
questions.q_en: What was the piano music like?

options.pinyin: hěn qīng hěn màn hěn ānjìng

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「很轻很慢很安静」, indicating faint, slow, and quiet.

questions.q_zh: 为什么其他邻居没听到？
questions.q_en: Why didn't other neighbors hear it?

options.text: 因为声音很轻，只有楼下的「我」能听到
options.pinyin: yīnwèi shēngyīn hěn qīng, zhǐyǒu lóu xià de wǒ néng tīng dào
options.text_en: sound was faint, only the person directly below could hear

questions.explanation: 因为 (because) directly states the reason: sound was faint, only the person directly below could hear.

questions.q_zh: 声音是怎么传出来的？
questions.q_en: How was the sound leaking?

options.text: 耳机线没有完全插进去
options.pinyin: ěrjī xiàn méiyǒu wánquán chā jìnqù
options.text_en: headphone jack wasn't fully plugged in

questions.explanation: The passage shows this through: his headphone jack wasn't fully plugged in, so sound had been coming through the speakers all along.

title: The Wishing Tree
text_zh: 公园里有一棵很老的树。不知道从什么时候开始，人们在树枝上挂了很多小纸条，上面写着自己的愿望。我有时候会去看那些纸条。大部分写的是：「希望考试通过」「希望爸妈身体健康」「希望找到好工作」。有一张纸条比较特别，上面写着：「希望明天是晴天，因为我想去放风筝。」我笑了。那些大的愿望和小的愿望被风吹得一起飘着，好像都一样重要。有一天我也挂了一张。我写的是：「希望二十年以后，再来看这棵树的时候，我还能记得今天的自己。」那张纸条在风里轻轻动了一下，好像树点了点头。
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yì kē hěn lǎo de shù. Bù zhīdào cóng shénme shíhou kāishǐ, rénmen zài shùzhī shàng guà le hěn duō xiǎo zhǐtiáo, shàngmiàn xiězhe zìjǐ de yuànwàng. Wǒ yǒu shíhou huì qù kàn nàxiē zhǐtiáo. Dàbùfen xiě de shì:「Xīwàng kǎoshì tōngguò」「xīwàng bà mā shēntǐ jiànkāng」「xīwàng zhǎo dào hǎo gōngzuò」. Yǒu yì zhāng zhǐtiáo bǐjiào tèbié, shàngmiàn xiězhe:「Xīwàng míngtiān shì qíngtiān, yīnwèi wǒ xiǎng qù fàng fēngzhēng.」Wǒ xiào le. Nàxiē dà de yuànwàng hé xiǎo de yuànwàng bèi fēng chuī de yìqǐ piāozhe, hǎoxiàng dōu yíyàng zhòngyào. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ yě guà le yì zhāng. Wǒ xiě de shì:「Xīwàng èrshí nián yǐhòu, zài lái kàn zhè kē shù de shíhou, wǒ hái néng jìde jīntiān de zìjǐ.」Nà zhāng zhǐtiáo zài fēng lǐ qīngqīng dòng le yíxià, hǎoxiàng shù diǎn le diǎn tóu.
text_en: In the park there's a old tree. At some point people started hanging small notes on its branches with their wishes. Sometimes I go read them. Most say: 'Hope I pass the exam,' 'Hope Mom and Dad stay healthy,' 'Hope I find a good job.' One note was rather special: 'Hope tomorrow is sunny, because I want to fly a kite.' I smiled. Big wishes and small wishes flutter together in the wind, seeming equally important. One day I hung one too. I wrote: 'I hope twenty years from now, when I come back to see this tree, I'll still remember today's me.' The note swayed gently in the wind, as if the tree nodded.

questions.q_zh: 那张比较特别的纸条写了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the special note say?

options.text: 希望明天是晴天，想去放风筝
options.pinyin: xīwàng míngtiān shì qíngtiān, xiǎng qù fàng fēngzhēng
options.text_en: hope for sunny weather to fly a kite
hope to find a good job

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「希望明天是晴天，」, indicating hope for sunny weather to fly a kite.

questions.q_zh: 「我」写了什么愿望？
questions.q_en: What wish did 'I' write?

options.text: 希望二十年后还能记得今天的自己
options.pinyin: xīwàng èrshí nián hòu hái néng jìde jīntiān de zìjǐ
options.text_en: remember today's self in twenty years
hope to be happy every day

questions.explanation: The narrator wrote 我希望二十年以后再来看这棵树 — hoping to return in twenty years and still remember today's feelings, making the wish about preserving the present self.

questions.q_zh: 作者想通过这个故事探讨什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author want to explore through this story?

options.text: 日常生活中的深层意义
options.pinyin: rìcháng shēnghuó zhōng de shēncéng yìyì

questions.explanation: The story centers on ' one note was rather special: 'hope tomorrow is sunny, because i want to fly a kite, which carries that theme.

title: The Train Window
text_zh: 我坐了一趟很长的火车，从北方到南方。窗外的风景一直在变：先是灰色的城市，然后是黄色的田，再然后是绿色的山。车厢里大部分人在看手机或者睡觉，只有我一个人一直看着窗外。对面坐着一个老奶奶，她看了我半天，忽然说：「年轻人，你是第一个跟我一样喜欢看窗外的。」我笑了。她说：「我每年坐这趟火车去看女儿。每次窗外的景色都不太一样——去年那里还是一片田，今年变成了一栋楼。」她一边说一边看着窗外，好像在跟一个老朋友聊天。火车越走越远，窗外越来越绿，我觉得这比到目的地更有意思。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zuò le yí tàng hěn cháng de huǒchē, cóng běifāng dào nánfāng. Chuāng wài de fēngjǐng yìzhí zài biàn: xiān shì huīsè de chéngshì, ránhòu shì huángsè de tián, zài ránhòu shì lǜsè de shān. Chēxiāng lǐ dàbùfen rén zài kàn shǒujī huòzhě shuì jiào, zhǐyǒu wǒ yí gè rén yìzhí kànzhe chuāng wài. Duìmiàn zuòzhe yí gè lǎo nǎinai, tā kàn le wǒ bàntiān, hūrán shuō:「Niánqīng rén, nǐ shì dì-yī gè gēn wǒ yíyàng xǐhuan kàn chuāng wài de.」Wǒ xiào le. Tā shuō:「Wǒ měi nián zuò zhè tàng huǒchē qù kàn nǚ'ér. Měi cì chuāng wài de jǐngsè dōu bú tài yíyàng——qùnián nàlǐ háishi yí piàn tián, jīnnián biàn chéng le yí dòng lóu.」Tā yìbiān shuō yìbiān kànzhe chuāng wài, hǎoxiàng zài gēn yí gè lǎo péngyou liáotiān. Huǒchē yuè zǒu yuè yuǎn, chuāng wài yuè lái yuè lǜ, wǒ juéde zhè bǐ dào mùdìdì gèng yǒuyìsi.
text_en: I took a long train ride from north to south. The scenery kept changing: grey cities, then yellow fields, then green mountains. Most people were on phones or sleeping — only I kept watching the window. An old grandmother across from me said: 'Young man, you're the first person who likes watching outside as much as I do.' She said: 'I take this train every year to see my daughter. The scenery is always a little different — last year that spot was a field, this year it's a building.' The train went farther, the view greener. This was more interesting than arriving.

questions.q_zh: 车厢里大部分人在做什么？
questions.q_en: What were most people in the car doing?
questions.explanation: 大部分人在看手机或者睡觉——只有我一直看着窗户 describes the contrast: most passengers were on phones or asleep while only the narrator watched out the window.

questions.q_zh: 老奶奶每年坐火车去做什么？
questions.q_en: Why does the grandmother take the train each year?
questions.explanation: The grandmother says 我每年都坐这趟火车去看女儿 — she rides this same train annually to visit her daughter.

questions.q_zh: 这段话想要读者从中获得什么？
questions.q_en: What does the passage want the reader to gain from it?

options.text: 一种新的看事物的方式
options.pinyin: yì zhǒng xīn de kàn shìwù de fāngshì
options.text_en: a new way of seeing things

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「一种新的看事物的方式」, indicating a new way of seeing things.

title: The Alley Barber
text_zh: 老城区的巷子里有一家理发店，只有一把椅子、一面镜子和一位老师傅。剪一次头发只要十五块钱，比外面便宜得多。但是来这里的人不只是因为便宜。老师傅剪头发的时候不说话，手很稳，剪完以后会用热毛巾帮你擦脸。整个过程大概二十分钟，很安静。有一个年轻人从小就在这里剪头发，现在已经三十多岁了。他说：「外面那些理发店又贵又吵，还总是问我要不要做什么项目。这里什么都不问，坐下来就剪。」老师傅听到了，笑着说：「你头发什么样我比你清楚，不需要问。」那家店没有牌子，但是认识它的人越来越多。
text_pinyin: Lǎo chéngqū de xiàngzi lǐ yǒu yì jiā lǐfà diàn, zhǐyǒu yì bǎ yǐzi, yí miàn jìngzi hé yí wèi lǎo shīfu. Jiǎn yí cì tóufa zhǐ yào shíwǔ kuài qián, bǐ wàimiàn piányi de duō. Dànshì lái zhèlǐ de rén bù zhǐshì yīnwèi piányi. Lǎo shīfu jiǎn tóufa de shíhou bù shuō huà, shǒu hěn wěn, jiǎn wán yǐhòu huì yòng rè máojīn bāng nǐ cā liǎn. Zhěng gè guòchéng dàgài èrshí fēnzhōng, hěn ānjìng. Yǒu yí gè niánqīng rén cóng xiǎo jiù zài zhèlǐ jiǎn tóufa, xiànzài yǐjīng sānshí duō suì le. Tā shuō:「Wàimiàn nàxiē lǐfà diàn yòu guì yòu chǎo, hái zǒng shì wèn wǒ yào bu yào zuò shénme xiàngmù. Zhèlǐ shénme dōu bú wèn, zuò xiàlái jiù jiǎn.」Lǎo shīfu tīng dào le, xiàozhe shuō:「Nǐ tóufa shénme yàng wǒ bǐ nǐ qīngchu, bù xūyào wèn.」Nà jiā diàn méiyǒu páizi, dànshì rènshi tā de rén yuè lái yuè duō.
text_en: In an old district alley there's a barbershop with one chair, one mirror, and one old barber. A haircut costs fifteen yuan. But people don't come just for the price. The barber doesn't talk while cutting, his hands are steady, and afterward he wipes your face with a hot towel. The whole process takes twenty minutes, quiet. One young man has come since childhood. He says: 'Outside shops are expensive and noisy, always pushing extra treatments. Here they ask nothing — sit down and cut.' The barber overheard and smiled: 'I know your hair better than you do — no need to ask.'

questions.q_zh: 剪一次头发多少钱？
questions.q_en: How much is a haircut?
questions.explanation: The number 「十五块」 answers directly — fifteen yuan for a haircut.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的节奏感对阅读体验有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the pacing of this passage affect the reading experience?

options.text: 让读者放慢速度去感受
options.pinyin: ràng dúzhě fàng màn sùdù qù gǎnshòu
options.text_en: slows the reader down to feel

options.pinyin: ràng dúzhě xiǎng kuài diǎn dú wán
options.text_en: makes the reader want to finish quickly

questions.explanation: The passage describes here they ask nothing — sit down and cut, showing how.

questions.q_zh: 年轻人为什么喜欢这家店？
questions.q_en: Why does the young man like this shop?
