options.text_en: asks nothing, just sit and cut

questions.explanation: 因为 (because) directly states the reason: asks nothing, just sit and cut.

title: The Wrong Delivery
text_zh: 今天中午我点了一份炒饭。外卖送到的时候，我打开一看——是一碗面条。我打电话给外卖员，他说：「不好意思，可能跟别人的换了。」我说没关系。但是五分钟以后，我接到一个陌生人的电话：「你好，我点的是面条，但是收到了炒饭。你是不是收到了我的面条？」我说是的。她笑了：「那我们换回来？」我们约在楼下见面。到了以后我发现，她就住在我隔壁。她说：「我搬来一年了，今天第一次跟邻居说话。」我们站在走廊里，各自端着对方的午饭。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān zhōngwǔ wǒ diǎn le yí fèn chǎo fàn. Wàimài sòng dào de shíhou, wǒ dǎkāi yí kàn——shì yì wǎn miàntiáo. Wǒ dǎ diànhuà gěi wàimàiyuán, tā shuō:「Bù hǎoyìsi, kěnéng gēn biéren de huàn le.」Wǒ shuō méi guānxi. Dànshì wǔ fēnzhōng yǐhòu, wǒ jiē dào yí gè mòshēng rén de diànhuà:「Nǐ hǎo, wǒ diǎn de shì miàntiáo, dànshì shōu dào le chǎo fàn. Nǐ shì bú shì shōu dào le wǒ de miàntiáo?」Wǒ shuō shì de. Tā xiào le:「Nà wǒmen huàn huílái?」Wǒmen yuē zài lóu xià jiànmiàn. Dào le yǐhòu wǒ fāxiàn, tā jiù zhù zài wǒ gébì. Wǒmen rènshi le shí miǎozhōng jiù bǎ wàimài huàn le huílái. Tā shuō:「Wǒ bān lái yì nián le, jīntiān dì-yī cì gēn línjū shuō huà.」Wǒ shuō:「Rúguǒ búshì sòng cuò le wàimài, wǒmen kěnéng yǒngyuǎn bú huì rènshi.」
text_en: Today at noon I ordered fried rice. When the delivery arrived, I opened it — it was a bowl of noodles. I called the delivery person. He said: 'Sorry, it probably got switched with someone else's.' I said no problem. But five minutes later, a stranger called: 'Hi, I ordered noodles but got fried rice. Did you get my noodles?' I said yes. She laughed: 'Swap back?' We agreed to meet downstairs. When I got there, I found she lives right next door. She said: 'I've lived here a year and this is the first time I've talked to a neighbor.' We stood in the hallway, each holding the other's lunch.

questions.q_zh: 「我」收到了什么？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' receive?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「面条」, meaning noodles — that was the unexpected gift from the neighbor.

questions.q_zh: 这段话用什么来代替直接的情感表达？
questions.q_en: What does this passage use instead of direct emotional expression?
questions.explanation: Their behavior — today at noon i ordered fried rice — shows that attitude.

questions.q_zh: 她搬来多久第一次跟邻居说话？
questions.q_en: How long had she lived there before talking to a neighbor?
questions.explanation: The number 「一年」 answers directly — a full year passed before she spoke to a neighbor.

title: The Kindergarten Crossing Guard
title_zh: 幼儿园门口的交通志愿者
text_zh: 每天早上八点，幼儿园门口都有一个穿黄色马甲的老人在帮小朋友过马路。他不是交警，也不是学校的人——他是住在附近的退休老师。他说：「这条路车比较多，小朋友个子矮，司机不容易看到。」每次有小朋友过马路，他都会举起手里的小旗子，让车停下来。小朋友们都叫他「旗子爷爷」。有一天下大雨，他还是来了，全身都被淋湿了。幼儿园的园长给他送了一把伞，说：「下这么大的雨，您可以不来的。」他说：「越是下雨，路上越危险。除了我以外，还有谁来？」那把伞比较小，但是他把它举在了小朋友头上。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang bā diǎn, yòu'éryuán ménkǒu dōu yǒu yí gè chuān huángsè mǎjiǎ de lǎorén zài bāng xiǎopéngyou guò mǎlù. Tā búshì jiāojǐng, yě búshì xuéxiào de rén——tā shì zhù zài fùjìn de tuìxiū lǎoshī. Tā shuō:「Zhè tiáo lù chē bǐjiào duō, xiǎopéngyou gèzi ǎi, sījī bù róngyì kàn dào.」Měi cì yǒu xiǎopéngyou guò mǎlù, tā dōu huì jǔ qǐ shǒu lǐ de xiǎo qízi, ràng chē tíng xiàlái. Xiǎopéngyoumen dōu jiào tā「qízi yéye」. Yǒu yì tiān xià dà yǔ, tā háishi lái le, quán shēn dōu bèi lín shī le. Yòu'éryuán de yuánzhǎng gěi tā sòng le yì bǎ sǎn, shuō:「Xià zhème dà de yǔ, nín kěyǐ bù lái de.」Tā shuō:「Yuè shì xià yǔ, lù shàng yuè wēixiǎn. Chúle wǒ yǐwài, hái yǒu shéi lái?」Nà bǎ sǎn bǐjiào xiǎo, dànshì tā bǎ tā jǔ zài le xiǎopéngyou tóu shàng.
text_en: Every morning at eight, an old man in a yellow vest helps children cross the road at the kindergarten. He's not a traffic officer — he's a retired teacher. He says: 'This road has a lot of cars, and kids are short — drivers can't easily see them.' He raises his little flag to stop traffic. The kids call him 'Flag Grandpa.' One day it rained hard. He still came, drenched. The principal brought him an umbrella: 'It's raining so hard, you don't have to come.' He said: 'The harder it rains, the more dangerous the road. Who else would come?'

questions.q_zh: 那个老人以前是做什么的？
questions.q_en: What did the old man used to do?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「退休老师」, indicating retired teacher.

questions.q_zh: 小朋友叫他什么？
questions.q_en: What do the children call him?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「旗子爷爷」, indicating Flag Grandpa.

questions.q_zh: 下大雨的时候他怎么做？
questions.q_en: What did he do in the heavy rain?

options.pinyin: bǎ sǎn jǔ zài zìjǐ tóu shàng

options.text: 还是来了，把伞举在小朋友头上
options.pinyin: háishi lái le, bǎ sǎn jǔ zài xiǎopéngyou tóu shàng
options.text_en: still came, held umbrella over children

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「还是来了，」, indicating still came, held umbrella over children.

title: The Night Noodle Stall
text_zh: 凌晨一点，城市大部分地方都关了。但是有一个面摊还亮着灯。老板是一个五十多岁的男人，他只卖一种面——阳春面。就是最简单的那种，面条、葱花、酱油、一点猪油。来吃的人都是深夜下班的人：出租车司机、医院的护士、加班的白领。大家坐在塑料椅子上，谁也不说话，安安静静地吃完就走。但是每个人走的时候，都会对老板说一声「谢谢」或者「明天见」。有人问老板：「你怎么不多卖几种面？」他说：「夜里来的人不需要选择，他们需要的是一碗热的、比较简单的东西。」我觉得他说得比较对。有时候，越简单的东西越温暖。
text_pinyin: Língchén yī diǎn, chéngshì dàbùfen dìfang dōu guān le. Dànshì yǒu yí gè miàn tān hái liàngzhe dēng. Lǎobǎn shì yí gè wǔshí duō suì de nánrén, tā zhǐ mài yì zhǒng miàn——yángchūn miàn. Jiùshì zuì jiǎndān de nà zhǒng, miàntiáo, cōnghuā, jiàngyóu, yìdiǎn zhūyóu. Lái chī de rén dōu shì shēnyè xiàbān de rén: chūzū chē sījī, yīyuàn de hùshi, jiābān de báilǐng. Dàjiā zuò zài sùliào yǐzi shàng, shéi yě bù shuō huà, ān'ānjìngjìng de chī wán jiù zǒu. Dànshì měi gè rén zǒu de shíhou, dōu huì duì lǎobǎn shuō yì shēng「xièxie」huòzhě「míngtiān jiàn」. Yǒu rén wèn lǎobǎn:「Nǐ zěnme bù duō mài jǐ zhǒng miàn?」Tā shuō:「Yè lǐ lái de rén bù xūyào xuǎnzé, tāmen xūyào de shì yì wǎn rè de, bǐjiào jiǎndān de dōngxi.」Wǒ juéde tā shuō de bǐjiào duì. Yǒu shíhou, yuè jiǎndān de dōngxi yuè wēnnuǎn.
text_en: At one in the morning, most of the city is closed. But one noodle stall still has its light on. The owner sells only one kind — plain noodle soup. The simplest kind: noodles, scallion, soy sauce, a bit of lard. The customers are all late-night workers: taxi drivers, nurses, office workers pulling overtime. Nobody talks, everyone quietly finishes and leaves. But every person says 'thank you' or 'see you tomorrow' on the way out. Someone asked: 'Why not sell more kinds?' He said: 'People who come at night don't need choices. They need a bowl of something hot and simple.'

questions.q_zh: 从职业选择可以看出什么？
questions.q_en: What can we infer from the career choice?
questions.explanation: The clue is that at one in the morning, most of the city is closed — from there you can infer the answer.

questions.q_zh: 这段话对工作态度的看法是什么？
questions.q_en: What view of work attitude does this passage present?

options.text_en: work is less important than life

questions.explanation: The words and actions (the customers are all late-night workers: taxi drivers, nurses, office workers pulling overtime) point to that.

questions.q_zh: 老板为什么只卖一种面？
questions.q_en: Why does the owner sell only one kind?

options.pinyin: tā zhǐ huì zuò yì zhǒng
options.text_en: he can only make one kind

options.text: 夜里来的人不需要选择，需要简单的东西
options.pinyin: yè lǐ lái de rén bù xūyào xuǎnzé, xūyào jiǎndān de dōngxi
options.text_en: night customers don't need choices, need simple things

options.pinyin: zhè zhǒng miàn zuì zhuàn qián

questions.explanation: The owner explains 晚上来的人不需要选择，需要简单的东西 — late-night customers need simplicity, not variety, which is why one plain noodle soup is enough.

title: The Old Jacket
text_zh: 我有一件穿了八年的旧外套。它已经比较旧了——颜色变淡了，袖子上有一个小洞，拉链有时候拉不上去。朋友们都让我扔了买新的。但是我不想。这件外套是我大学毕业那年买的，那时候我刚找到第一份工作，用第一个月的工资买的。我穿着它去面试过、搬过家、在冬天的街上走过很远的路。它不好看了，但是它陪我经历了很多事情。有人说衣服只是衣服，但是对我来说，这件外套就像一个朋友。我把袖子上的洞补好了，把拉链修好了。它还能再穿几年。有些东西旧了不是应该扔掉，而是应该修好。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu yí jiàn chuān le bā nián de jiù wàitào. Tā yǐjīng bǐjiào jiù le——yánsè biàn dàn le, xiùzi shàng yǒu yí gè xiǎo dòng, lāliàn yǒu shíhou lā bú shàngqù. Péngyoumen dōu ràng wǒ rēng le mǎi xīn de. Dànshì wǒ bù xiǎng. Zhè jiàn wàitào shì wǒ dàxué bìyè nà nián mǎi de, nà shíhou wǒ gāng zhǎo dào dì-yī fèn gōngzuò, yòng dì-yī gè yuè de gōngzī mǎi de. Wǒ chuānzhe tā qù miànshì guò, bān guò jiā, zài dōngtiān de jiē shàng zǒu guò hěn yuǎn de lù. Tā bù hǎokàn le, dànshì tā péi wǒ jīnglì le hěn duō shìqing. Yǒu rén shuō yīfu zhǐshì yīfu, dànshì duì wǒ lái shuō, zhè jiàn wàitào jiù xiàng yí gè péngyou. Wǒ bǎ xiùzi shàng de dòng bǔ hǎo le, bǎ lāliàn xiū hǎo le. Tā hái néng zài chuān jǐ nián. Yǒuxiē dōngxi jiù le búshì yīnggāi rēng diào, ér shì yīnggāi xiū hǎo.
text_en: I have a jacket I've worn for eight years. The color has faded, there's a small hole in the sleeve, and the zipper sometimes sticks. Friends tell me to throw it out. But I bought it the year I graduated college, with my first month's salary. I've worn it to interviews, moved houses in it, walked long winter streets in it. It doesn't look good anymore, but it's been through a lot with me. Some say clothes are just clothes. I patched the hole, fixed the zipper. It can last a few more years. Some things, when they get old, should be repaired, not thrown away.

questions.q_zh: 这件外套是用什么买的？
questions.q_en: What was the jacket bought with?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「第一个月的工资」, indicating first month's salary.

questions.q_zh: 外套有什么问题？
questions.q_en: What's wrong with the jacket?

options.text: 颜色变淡了，袖子有洞，拉链不好用
options.pinyin: yánsè biàn dàn le, xiùzi yǒu dòng, lāliàn bù hǎo yòng
options.text_en: color faded, hole in sleeve, zipper sticks

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「颜色变淡了，袖子」, indicating color faded, hole in sleeve, zipper sticks.

questions.q_zh: 「我」最后怎么做了？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' do in the end?

options.text_en: threw it out and bought new

options.text: 把洞补好了，拉链修好了
options.pinyin: bǎ dòng bǔ hǎo le, lāliàn xiū hǎo le
options.text_en: patched the hole, fixed the zipper

options.pinyin: fàng zài yīguì lǐ bù chuān le

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「洞补好了，」, indicating patched the hole, fixed the zipper.

title: The Elevator Smile
text_zh: 我每天早上坐电梯下楼。电梯里的人都低着头看手机，谁也不看谁。有一天，一个新搬来的女生进了电梯，对每个人微笑着说了一声「早上好」。大家都愣了一下——在电梯里跟陌生人说话，这在城市里比较少见。有人笑了笑，有人点了点头，有人还是没有反应。但是第二天，她又说了。第三天也是。慢慢地，电梯里的气氛变了。有人开始也说「早上好」了。一个月以后，我发现自己也开始说了。一个人的习惯改变了一部电梯的气氛。有时候让世界变好的方法比较简单——你先开口就行了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān zǎoshang zuò diàntī xià lóu. Diàntī lǐ de rén dōu dīzhe tóu kàn shǒujī, shéi yě bú kàn shéi. Yǒu yì tiān, yí gè xīn bān lái de nǚshēng jìn le diàntī, duì měi gè rén wēixiàozhe shuō le yì shēng「zǎoshang hǎo」. Dàjiā dōu lèng le yíxià——zài diàntī lǐ gēn mòshēng rén shuō huà, zhè zài chéngshì lǐ bǐjiào shǎojiàn. Yǒu rén xiào le xiào, yǒu rén diǎn le diǎn tóu, yǒu rén háishi méiyǒu fǎnyìng. Dànshì dì-èr tiān, tā yòu shuō le. Dì-sān tiān yěshì. Mànman de, diàntī lǐ de qìfēn biàn le. Yǒu rén kāishǐ yě shuō「zǎoshang hǎo」le. Yí gè yuè yǐhòu, wǒ fāxiàn zìjǐ yě kāishǐ shuō le. Yí gè rén de xíguàn gǎibiàn le yí bù diàntī de qìfēn. Yǒu shíhou ràng shìjiè biàn hǎo de fāngfǎ bǐjiào jiǎndān——nǐ xiān kāi kǒu jiù xíng le.
text_en: Every morning I take the elevator down. Everyone looks down at their phones, nobody looks at anyone. One day, a girl who just moved in entered and smiled at everyone saying 'good morning.' People were taken aback — talking to strangers in elevators is rather uncommon in cities. Some smiled back, some nodded, some didn't react. But the next day, she said it again. The third day too. Gradually, the atmosphere in the elevator changed. People started saying 'good morning' too. After a month, I found myself saying it as well. One person's habit changed an entire elevator's atmosphere. Sometimes the way to make the world better is rather simple — you just speak first.

questions.q_zh: 电梯里的人以前做什么？
questions.q_en: What did people in the elevator used to do?

options.text_en: said good morning to each other

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「低着头看手机」, indicating looked down at phones.

questions.q_zh: 那个女生做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the girl do?

options.pinyin: měi tiān zài diàntī lǐ chàng gē
měi tiān wēixiàozhe shuō zǎoshang hǎo
options.text_en: smiled and said good morning daily

options.pinyin: zài diàntī lǐ tiē le zhǐtiáo

questions.explanation: 一个刚搬来的女孩走进来，对每个人微笑说「早上好」 — she broke the silent elevator routine by simply smiling and greeting everyone each morning.

questions.q_zh: 一个月以后电梯里怎么样了？
questions.q_en: What was the elevator like after a month?

options.text: 大家都开始说早上好了
options.pinyin: dàjiā dōu kāishǐ shuō zǎoshang hǎo le

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「大家都开始说早上好了」, indicating everyone started saying good morning.

title: The Umbrella Stand
text_zh: 我们小区的大门旁边出现了一个伞架。上面贴着一张纸：「下雨的时候拿，不下雨的时候还。」没有人知道是谁放的。刚开始只有三把伞，都是旧的。有一天下雨了，伞被人拿走了两把。第二天没下雨，那两把伞被放回来了。而且多了一把新的——有人觉得旧伞不太好用，就放了一把自己的新伞。一个星期以后，伞架上已经有十几把伞了。各种颜色、各种大小。有的人还在自己的伞上写了名字。物业的人说：「我们没有安排这个事情，但是我们觉得应该留着。」那个伞架比较旧了，但是上面的伞越来越多。这大概就是信任吧——你先给出去，然后别人也会给回来。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xiǎoqū de dàmén pángbiān chūxiàn le yí gè sǎn jià. Shàngmiàn tiēzhe yì zhāng zhǐ:「Xià yǔ de shíhou ná, bú xià yǔ de shíhou huán.」Méiyǒu rén zhīdào shì shéi fàng de. Gāng kāishǐ zhǐyǒu sān bǎ sǎn, dōu shì jiù de. Yǒu yì tiān xià yǔ le, sǎn bèi rén ná zǒu le liǎng bǎ. Dì-èr tiān méi xià yǔ, nà liǎng bǎ sǎn bèi fàng huílái le. Érqiě duō le yì bǎ xīn de——yǒu rén juéde jiù sǎn bú tài hǎo yòng, jiù fàng le yì bǎ zìjǐ de xīn sǎn. Yí gè xīngqī yǐhòu, sǎn jià shàng yǐjīng yǒu shí jǐ bǎ sǎn le. Gè zhǒng yánsè, gè zhǒng dàxiǎo. Yǒude rén hái zài zìjǐ de sǎn shàng xiě le míngzi. Wùyè de rén shuō:「Wǒmen méiyǒu ānpái zhège shìqing, dànshì wǒmen juéde yīnggāi liúzhe.」Nàge sǎn jià bǐjiào jiù le, dànshì shàngmiàn de sǎn yuè lái yuè duō. Zhè dàgài jiùshì xìnrèn ba——nǐ xiān gěi chūqù, ránhòu biéren yě huì gěi huílái.
text_en: An umbrella stand appeared by our complex's main gate. A note reads: 'Take when it rains, return when it doesn't.' Nobody knows who put it there. At first there were three old umbrellas. One rainy day, two were taken. The next day, both came back — plus one new one. Someone thought the old ones weren't great, so they added their own. After a week, there were a dozen umbrellas. All colors, all sizes. The property manager said: 'We didn't arrange this, but it should stay.' That stand is old, but the umbrellas keep growing. This is trust — you give first, and others give back.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中什么是'看不见'但能感觉到的？

options.text: 人物的情感和内心世界

title: The Clockmaker
text_zh: 老城区有一个修钟表的老人，今年八十二岁了。他的店只有一张桌子大，桌子上放满了各种钟表的零件。他每天早上九点开门，下午五点关门，中间不休息。他的手比较抖了，但是拿起工具的时候又变得很稳。有人拿了一个不走了的旧钟来找他，说：「这个钟比较旧了，还能修吗？」他把钟打开看了看，说：「只是一个小零件坏了。它还想走，只是需要有人帮它一下。」他花了一个下午把那个钟修好了。走的时候，那个人说：「这个钟是我爷爷的。谢谢您让它又走了起来。」老人笑着说：「不是我厉害，是那个钟不想停。」
text_pinyin: Lǎo chéngqū yǒu yí gè xiū zhōngbiǎo de lǎorén, jīnnián bāshí'èr suì le. Tā de diàn zhǐyǒu yì zhāng zhuōzi dà, zhuōzi shàng fàng mǎn le gè zhǒng zhōngbiǎo de língjiàn. Tā měi tiān zǎoshang jiǔ diǎn kāi mén, xiàwǔ wǔ diǎn guān mén, zhōngjiān bù xiūxi. Tā de shǒu bǐjiào dǒu le, dànshì ná qǐ gōngjù de shíhou yòu biàn de hěn wěn. Yǒu rén ná le yí gè bù zǒu le de jiù zhōng lái zhǎo tā, shuō:「Zhège zhōng bǐjiào jiù le, hái néng xiū ma?」Tā bǎ zhōng dǎkāi kàn le kàn, shuō:「Zhǐshì yí gè xiǎo língjiàn huài le. Tā hái xiǎng zǒu, zhǐshì xūyào yǒu rén bāng tā yíxià.」Tā huā le yí gè xiàwǔ bǎ nàge zhōng xiū hǎo le. Zǒu de shíhou, nàge rén shuō:「Zhège zhōng shì wǒ yéye de. Xièxie nín ràng tā yòu zǒu le qǐlái.」Lǎorén xiàozhe shuō:「Búshì wǒ lìhai, shì nàge zhōng bù xiǎng tíng.」
text_en: In the old district there's a watchmaker, eighty-two this year. His shop is just one table big, covered with clock parts. He opens at nine, closes at five, no break in between. His hands shake rather noticeably now, but when he picks up tools they become steady again. Someone brought a stopped old clock and asked: 'It's rather old — can it still be fixed?' He opened it, looked, and said: 'Just a small part is broken. It still wants to run — it just needs someone to help.' He spent an afternoon fixing it. Leaving, the person said: 'This clock was my grandfather's. Thank you for making it run again.' The old man smiled: 'I'm not that skilled — it's the clock that doesn't want to stop.'

title: The Bus Driver's Farewell
text_zh: 每天早上七点十分，我坐的那班公交车总是同一个司机——一个五十多岁的叔叔。他从来不催人上车，到站的时候会等跑过来的人。有一天早上，他在广播里说：「各位乘客，明天开始我就退休了。谢谢你们这么多年坐我的车。」车上突然安静了。然后一个老太太说了一声「谢谢你」。接着，越来越多的人开始说谢谢。那个司机的眼睛有点红了。他说：「开了二十年的车，你们有些人我虽然不认识，但是我认识你们上车的表情。」有些联系不需要名字，也不需要说话。每天在同一个时间做同一件事，本身就是一种承诺。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang qī diǎn shí fēn, wǒ zuò de nà bān gōngjiāo chē zǒngshì tóng yí gè sījī——yí gè wǔshí duō suì de shūshu. Tā cónglái bù cuī rén shàng chē, dào zhàn de shíhou huì děng pǎo guòlái de rén. Yǒu yì tiān zǎoshang, tā zài guǎngbō lǐ shuō:「Gèwèi chéngkè, míngtiān kāishǐ wǒ jiù tuìxiū le. Xièxie nǐmen zhème duō nián zuò wǒ de chē.」 Chē shàng tūrán ānjìng le. Ránhòu yí gè lǎo tàitai shuō le yì shēng「xièxie nǐ」. Jiēzhe, yuè lái yuè duō de rén kāishǐ shuō xièxie. Nàge sījī de yǎnjing yǒudiǎn hóng le. Tā shuō:「Kāi le èrshí nián de chē, nǐmen yǒuxiē rén wǒ suīrán bú rènshi, dànshì wǒ rènshi nǐmen shàng chē de biǎoqíng.」 Yǒuxiē liánxì bù xūyào míngzi, yě bù xūyào shuō huà. Měi tiān zài tóng yí gè shíjiān zuò tóng yí jiàn shì, běnshēn jiùshì yì zhǒng chéngnuò.
text_en: Every morning at seven ten, my bus always has the same driver — a man in his fifties. He never rushes passengers and waits for people running to catch the bus. One morning he announced: 'Starting tomorrow I'm retiring. Thank you for riding my bus all these years.' The bus went silent. Then an old lady said 'thank you.' More and more people joined. The driver's eyes turned red. He said: 'After twenty years, some of you I don't know by name, but I know the expressions on your faces.' Doing the same thing at the same time every day is itself a kind of promise.

title: The Temple's Free Hot Water
text_zh: 城郊有一座小寺庙，门口放着一个大热水壶，旁边贴着一张纸条：「免费热水，请自取。」不管冬天还是夏天，那壶热水从早到晚都在。来取水的人各种各样——附近的环卫工人、经过的路人、偶尔还有迷路的游客。寺庙的师父说：「我们做不了大事，但是可以做一壶热水。冬天喝了暖身体，夏天泡一杯茶解解渴。」有人问他这样不是很费钱吗，他笑着说：「一壶水的钱不多，但是喝了的人可能会记得这个地方很久。」简单的善意，有时候比复杂的帮助更持久。
text_pinyin: Chéngjiāo yǒu yí zuò xiǎo sìmiào, ménkǒu fàngzhe yí gè dà rè shuǐ hú, pángbiān tiēzhe yì zhāng zhǐtiáo:「Miǎnfèi rè shuǐ, qǐng zì qǔ.」 Bùguǎn dōngtiān háishi xiàtiān, nà hú rè shuǐ cóng zǎo dào wǎn dōu zài. Lái qǔ shuǐ de rén gè zhǒng gè yàng——fùjìn de huánwèi gōngrén, jīngguò de lùrén, ǒu'ěr hái yǒu mílù de yóukè. Sìmiào de shīfu shuō:「Wǒmen zuò bù liǎo dà shì, dànshì kěyǐ zuò yì hú rè shuǐ. Dōngtiān hē le nuǎn shēntǐ, xiàtiān pào yì bēi chá jiě jie kě.」 Yǒu rén wèn tā zhèyàng búshì hěn fèi qián ma, tā xiàozhe shuō:「Yì hú shuǐ de qián bù duō, dànshì hē le de rén kěnéng huì jìde zhège dìfang hěn jiǔ.」 Jiǎndān de shànyì, yǒu shíhou bǐ fùzá de bāngzhù gèng chíjiǔ.
text_en: On the city outskirts there's a small temple with a large hot water thermos at the entrance and a note: 'Free hot water, please help yourself.' Winter or summer, that hot water is there from morning to night. All sorts of people come — nearby sanitation workers, passersby, occasionally lost tourists. The temple monk says: 'We can't do big things, but we can make a pot of hot water. In winter it warms the body; in summer, brew a cup of tea to quench thirst.' Someone asked if it's too expensive. He smiled: 'A pot of water doesn't cost much, but the person who drinks it might remember this place for a long time.' Simple kindness is sometimes more lasting than complicated help.

title: The Bookmark Messages
text_zh: 图书馆的一本旧小说里，我发现了一个书签，上面写着：「这本书的结局会让你哭。做好准备。」我笑了笑，继续看。看完以后，我真的哭了。我在书签的另一面写了：「你说得对。我在图书馆哭了，很丢人。」然后把书签放回去。一个月以后我又来借这本书，发现书签还在，但是下面多了一行字：「别不好意思，我当时也哭了。在这里哭的一共有七个人了，你是第八个。」我又在下面写了一行：「算上你写的这行，现在是第九个了吧？」这种不认识的人之间的对话，温柔得像一首诗。
text_pinyin: Túshūguǎn de yì běn jiù xiǎoshuō lǐ, wǒ fāxiàn le yí gè shūqiān, shàngmiàn xiězhe:「Zhè běn shū de jiéjú huì ràng nǐ kū. Zuò hǎo zhǔnbèi.」 Wǒ xiào le xiào, jìxù kàn. Kàn wán yǐhòu, wǒ zhēn de kū le. Wǒ zài shūqiān de lìng yí miàn xiě le:「Nǐ shuō de duì. Wǒ zài túshūguǎn kū le, hěn diūrén.」 Ránhòu bǎ shūqiān fàng huíqù. Yí gè yuè yǐhòu wǒ yòu lái jiè zhè běn shū, fāxiàn shūqiān hái zài, dànshì xiàmiàn duō le yì háng zì:「Bié bù hǎoyìsi, wǒ dāngshí yě kū le. Zài zhèlǐ kū de yígòng yǒu qī gè rén le, nǐ shì dì bā gè.」 Wǒ yòu zài xiàmiàn xiě le yì háng:「Suàn shàng nǐ xiě de zhè háng, xiànzài shì dì jiǔ gè le ba?」 Zhè zhǒng bú rènshi de rén zhījiān de duìhuà, wēnróu de xiàng yì shǒu shī.
text_en: In an old novel at the library, I found a bookmark that read: 'This book's ending will make you cry. Be prepared.' I smiled and kept reading. When I finished, I really did cry. On the other side of the bookmark I wrote: 'You were right. I cried in the library. Embarrassing.' Then put it back. A month later I borrowed the book again. The bookmark was still there, with a new line: 'Don't be embarrassed, I cried too. Seven people have cried here now. You're the eighth.' I added another line: 'Counting your line, isn't it the ninth now?' This kind of conversation between strangers is gentle as a poem.

title: The Guesthouse Guestbook
text_zh: 我住的民宿有一本留言本。到的第一个晚上，我翻了翻。有人画了一幅小画，是窗外的山。有人写了一首诗。有一个人只写了四个字：「我很累。来到这里以后，终于可以休息了。」看到这句话的时候，我突然觉得眼睛有点酸。因为我也是同样的原因来的。工作太忙了，很久没有好好休息了。这家民宿没有电视，没有网络，只有窗外的山和虫子的声音。第二天早上我在留言本上写了一句：「感谢这里的安静。它比任何药都有用。」有时候我们需要的不是去一个远方，而是找到一个可以安安静静待着的地方。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù de mínsù yǒu yì běn liúyán běn. Dào de dì yī gè wǎnshang, wǒ fān le fān. Yǒu rén huà le yì fú xiǎo huà, shì chuāng wài de shān. Yǒu rén xiě le yì shǒu shī. Yǒu yí gè rén zhǐ xiě le sì gè zì:「Wǒ hěn lèi. Lái dào zhèlǐ yǐhòu, zhōngyú kěyǐ xiūxi le.」 Kàn dào zhè jù huà de shíhou, wǒ tūrán juéde yǎnjing yǒudiǎn suān. Yīnwèi wǒ yě shì tóngyàng de yuányīn lái de. Gōngzuò tài máng le, hěn jiǔ méiyǒu hǎohāo xiūxi le. Zhè jiā mínsù méiyǒu diànshì, méiyǒu wǎngluò, zhǐ yǒu chuāng wài de shān hé chóngzi de shēngyīn. Dì èr tiān zǎoshang wǒ zài liúyán běn shàng xiě le yí jù:「Gǎnxiè zhèlǐ de ānjìng. Tā bǐ rènhé yào dōu yǒu yòng.」 Yǒu shíhou wǒmen xūyào de búshì qù yí gè yuǎnfāng, érshì zhǎo dào yí gè kěyǐ ān'ānjìngjìng dāizhe de dìfang.
text_en: The guesthouse I stayed at had a guestbook. The first night, I flipped through it. Someone drew a small picture of the mountains outside the window. Someone wrote a poem. One person wrote just four characters: 'I'm tired. After coming here, I can finally rest.' Reading that, my eyes suddenly stung. Because I came for the same reason. Work was too busy; I hadn't properly rested in ages. This guesthouse has no TV, no internet — just mountains outside and the sound of insects. The next morning I wrote in the guestbook: 'Thank you for the quiet here. It's more useful than any medicine.' Sometimes what we need isn't going somewhere far away, but finding a place where we can simply be still.

title: The Train Station Piano
text_zh: 在一个欧洲的火车站里，候车大厅放着一架公共钢琴。任何人都可以坐下来弹。我在等车的时候，看到一个背着大书包的年轻人坐了下来。他弹了一首很简单的曲子，不太好听，但是弹完以后他笑了。然后一个穿西装的中年人坐了下来，弹了一首古典音乐，非常好听。大家都停下来听。弹完以后他站起来，拿起行李箱就走了，好像什么都没发生过。我在那里坐了一个小时，看了五个不同的人弹琴。每个人弹的都不一样，但是每个人弹完以后的表情都是一样的——放松、满足。
text_pinyin: Zài yí gè Ōuzhōu de huǒchē zhàn lǐ, hòuchē dàtīng fàngzhe yí jià gōnggòng gāngqín. Rènhé rén dōu kěyǐ zuò xiàlái tán. Wǒ zài děng chē de shíhou, kàn dào yí gè bēizhe dà shūbāo de niánqīng rén zuò le xiàlái. Tā tán le yì shǒu hěn jiǎndān de qǔzi, bú tài hǎotīng, dànshì tán wán yǐhòu tā xiào le. Ránhòu yí gè chuān xīzhuāng de zhōngnián rén zuò le xiàlái, tán le yì shǒu gǔdiǎn yīnyuè, fēicháng hǎotīng. Dàjiā dōu tíng xiàlái tīng. Tán wán yǐhòu tā zhàn qǐlái, ná qǐ xíngli xiāng jiù zǒu le, hǎoxiàng shénme dōu méi fāshēng guò. Wǒ zài nàlǐ zuò le yí gè xiǎoshí, kàn le wǔ gè bùtóng de rén tánqín. Měi gè rén tán de dōu bù yíyàng, dànshì měi gè rén tán wán yǐhòu de biǎoqíng dōu shì yíyàng de——fàngsōng, mǎnzú. Yěxǔ lǚxíng zhōng zuì hǎo de shíkè, búshì dàodá mùdìdì, érshì děngdài de shíhou tūrán tīng dào de yì shǒu qǔzi.
text_en: In a European train station, there's a public piano in the waiting hall. Anyone can sit and play. While waiting for my train, I watched a young person with a big backpack sit down. He played a simple piece, not good, but smiled after finishing. Then a suited middle-aged man sat down and played a classical piece, beautifully. Everyone stopped to listen. When he finished, he stood, grabbed his suitcase, and walked away as if nothing happened. I sat there for an hour, watching five different people play. Each played differently, but everyone's expression after finishing was the same — relaxed, content.

questions.q_zh: 火车站里有什么特别的东西？
questions.q_en: What's special in the train station?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「一个公共钢琴」, indicating a public piano.

questions.q_zh: 每个人弹完以后的表情有什么共同点？
questions.q_en: What do all players' expressions share after playing?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「都很放松、满足」, indicating all relaxed, content.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中什么是'看不见'但能感觉到的？
questions.q_en: What in this passage is 'invisible' but can be felt?

options.text: 人物的情感和内心世界
options.pinyin: rénwù de qínggǎn hé nèixīn shìjiè
options.text_en: the character's emotions and inner world

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「人物的情感和内心世界」, indicating the character's emotions and inner world.

title: The Map That Got Wet
text_zh: 在一个陌生的城市旅行，我的手机没电了，只有一张纸质地图。然后下雨了。地图被雨淋湿了，看不清楚了。我站在路边不知道怎么走。一个卖伞的老人看到我，走过来说：「你要去哪里？」我告诉他以后，他笑着说：「地图看不清没关系，跟着我走就行。」他带我穿过了好几条小巷子，走了大概十分钟。到了以后他说：「年轻人，有时候迷路也不是坏事。如果你没有迷路，你就不会走进这些巷子，也不会认识我。」他说完就走了，消失在雨里。那是我那次旅行中最温暖的十分钟。
text_pinyin: Zài yí gè mòshēng de chéngshì lǚxíng, wǒ de shǒujī méi diàn le, zhǐ yǒu yì zhāng zhǐzhì dìtú. Ránhòu xià yǔ le. Dìtú bèi yǔ lín shī le, kàn bù qīngchu le. Wǒ zhàn zài lù biān bù zhīdào zěnme zǒu. Yí gè mài sǎn de lǎorén kàn dào wǒ, zǒu guòlái shuō:「Nǐ yào qù nǎlǐ?」 Wǒ gàosu tā yǐhòu, tā xiàozhe shuō:「Dìtú kàn bù qīng méi guānxi, gēnzhe wǒ zǒu jiù xíng.」 Tā dài wǒ chuān guò le hǎo jǐ tiáo xiǎo xiàngzi, zǒu le dàgài shí fēnzhōng. Dào le yǐhòu tā shuō:「Niánqīng rén, yǒu shíhou mílù yě búshì huài shì. Rúguǒ nǐ méiyǒu mílù, nǐ jiù bú huì zǒu jìn zhèxiē xiàngzi, yě bú huì rènshi wǒ.」 Tā shuō wán jiù zǒu le, xiāoshī zài yǔ lǐ. Nà shì wǒ nà cì lǚxíng zhōng zuì wēnnuǎn de shí fēnzhōng.
text_en: Traveling in an unfamiliar city, my phone died. I only had a paper map. Then it rained. The map got soaked and became unreadable. I stood by the road not knowing which way to go. An old umbrella seller saw me and came over: 'Where are you going?' After I told him, he smiled: 'Can't read the map? No problem, just follow me.' He led me through several alleys, about ten minutes' walk. When we arrived he said: 'Young person, sometimes getting lost isn't a bad thing. If you hadn't gotten lost, you wouldn't have walked through these alleys, and you wouldn't have met me.' Then he left, disappearing into the rain. Those were the warmest ten minutes of that trip.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么只有纸地图？
questions.q_en: Why did 'I' only have a paper map?
questions.explanation: 在一个陌生的城市旅行，手机没电了 — the phone died while traveling in an unfamiliar city, leaving only the paper map.

questions.q_zh: 老人说迷路为什么不是坏事？
questions.q_en: Why does the old man say getting lost isn't bad?

options.text: 因为不迷路就不会走进巷子也不会认识他
options.pinyin: yīnwèi bù mílù jiù bú huì zǒu jìn xiàngzi yě bú huì rènshi tā
options.text_en: wouldn't walk the alleys or meet him otherwise

options.text: 因为可以看到更多风景
options.pinyin: yīnwèi kěyǐ kàn dào gèng duō fēngjǐng

questions.explanation: The old man reasons 如果你没有迷路，你就不会走过这些小巷子，也不会遇到我 — getting lost led to the alleys and this encounter, turning a mishap into something valuable.

questions.q_zh: 「我」怎么形容那十分钟？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' describe those ten minutes?
questions.explanation: The passage shows this through: those were the warmest ten minutes of that trip.

title: The Bus Stop Conversation
text_zh: 等公交车的时候，旁边一个老奶奶忽然问我：「年轻人，现在几点了？」我看了手机告诉她。然后她又问：「十七路车多久来一趟？」我帮她查了一下，说大概还要等十分钟。她笑着说：「谢谢你。我不太会用手机。」我们就这样聊了起来。她告诉我她要去医院看老伴。她说她每个星期都去，坐公交车要四十分钟。我问她：「为什么不让儿子开车送您？」她说：「儿子很忙。而且我一个人坐公交车，还能跟人说说话。在医院的时候我一直在说话，坐公交车的时候也想说说话。」车来了。她上车以后，回头对我笑了笑。那个笑比较淡，但是很真。
text_pinyin: Děng gōngjiāo chē de shíhou, pángbiān yí gè lǎo nǎinai hūrán wèn wǒ:「Niánqīng rén, xiànzài jǐ diǎn le?」Wǒ kàn le shǒujī gàosu tā. Ránhòu tā yòu wèn:「Shíqī lù chē duō jiǔ lái yí tàng?」Wǒ bāng tā chá le yíxià, shuō dàgài hái yào děng shí fēnzhōng. Tā xiàozhe shuō:「Xièxie nǐ. Wǒ bú tài huì yòng shǒujī.」Wǒmen jiù zhèyàng liáo le qǐlái. Tā gàosu wǒ tā yào qù yīyuàn kàn lǎobàn. Tā shuō tā měi gè xīngqī dōu qù, zuò gōngjiāo chē yào sìshí fēnzhōng. Wǒ wèn tā:「Wèishénme bú ràng érzi kāi chē sòng nín?」Tā shuō:「Érzi hěn máng. Érqiě wǒ yí gè rén zuò gōngjiāo chē, hái néng gēn rén shuō shuō huà. Zài yīyuàn de shíhou wǒ yìzhí zài shuō huà, zuò gōngjiāo chē de shíhou yě xiǎng shuō shuō huà.」Chē lái le. Tā shàng chē yǐhòu, huítóu duì wǒ xiào le xiào. Nàge xiào bǐjiào dàn, dànshì hěn zhēn.
text_en: While waiting for the bus, an old grandmother asked me the time. Then: 'How often does the 17 come?' About ten minutes. She smiled: 'Thank you. I'm not good with phones.' She told me she was going to the hospital to see her husband. She goes every week — forty minutes by bus. I asked why not have her son drive. She said: 'My son is busy. Riding the bus alone, I can still talk to people. At the hospital I'm always talking — on the bus I want to talk too.' After boarding, she turned back and smiled. Faint, but genuine.

questions.q_zh: 这段话用什么来代替直接的情感表达？
questions.q_en: What does this passage use instead of direct emotional expression?
questions.explanation: The passage describes while waiting for the bus, an old grandmother asked me the time, which reveals that feeling.

questions.q_zh: 她为什么不让儿子送？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't she have her son drive her?

options.text_en: doesn't want to trouble her son

options.text: 儿子很忙，而且她想在路上跟人说话
options.pinyin: érzi hěn máng, érqiě tā xiǎng zài lù shàng gēn rén shuō huà
options.text_en: son is busy, and she wants to talk to people on the way

questions.explanation: The key phrase 「儿子很忙」 gives the reason — son is busy, and she wants to talk to people on the way.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得老奶奶的笑怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' describe the grandmother's smile?
questions.explanation: The grandmother's smile is described as 淡淡的，但是真的 — faint yet genuine, suggesting quiet sincerity rather than performative warmth.

title: The Bridge at Night
text_zh: 我住的地方附近有一座旧桥。白天的时候，桥上全是车和人，很吵。但是晚上十点以后，桥变得比较安静了。有时候我会一个人走到桥上去。站在桥中间，能看到两边的城市灯光倒映在河水里。风从河面上吹过来，比较凉。我发现晚上来桥上的人都不赶时间。有人站在那里看风景，有人靠在栏杆上打电话。有一对情侣坐在桥头的台阶上，什么也不说，就看着对面的灯。我觉得每座城市都应该有一座这样的桥——白天是路，晚上是一个可以停下来想事情的地方。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù de dìfang fùjìn yǒu yí zuò jiù qiáo. Báitiān de shíhou, qiáo shàng quán shì chē hé rén, hěn chǎo. Dànshì wǎnshang shí diǎn yǐhòu, qiáo biàn de bǐjiào ānjìng le. Yǒu shíhou wǒ huì yí gè rén zǒu dào qiáo shàng qù. Zhàn zài qiáo zhōngjiān, néng kàn dào liǎng biān de chéngshì dēngguāng dào yìng zài héshuǐ lǐ. Fēng cóng hémiàn shàng chuī guòlái, bǐjiào liáng. Wǒ fāxiàn wǎnshang lái qiáo shàng de rén dōu bù gǎn shíjiān. Yǒu rén zhàn zài nàlǐ kàn fēngjǐng, yǒu rén kào zài lángān shàng dǎ diànhuà. Yǒu yí duì qínglǚ zuò zài qiáotóu de táijiē shàng, shénme yě bù shuō, jiù kànzhe duìmiàn de dēng. Wǒ juéde měi zuò chéngshì dōu yīnggāi yǒu yí zuò zhèyàng de qiáo——báitiān shì lù, wǎnshang shì yí gè kěyǐ tíng xiàlái xiǎng shìqing de dìfang.
text_en: Near where I live there's an old bridge. During the day it's packed with cars and people, very noisy. But after ten at night, it gets rather quiet. Sometimes I walk there alone. Standing in the middle, you can see city lights on both sides reflected in the river. The wind from the water is rather cool. I notice the people who come to the bridge at night are in no rush. Some stand and watch the scenery, some lean on the railing making phone calls. A couple sits on the steps at the bridge's end, saying nothing, just watching the lights across the way. I think every city should have a bridge like this — a road by day, a place to stop and think at night.

questions.q_zh: 白天的桥怎么样？
questions.q_en: What's the bridge like during the day?

options.pinyin: quán shì chē hé rén, hěn chǎo
options.text_en: packed with cars and people, noisy

questions.explanation: 白天它上面挤满了车和人，很吵 directly describes daytime conditions — packed with cars and people, very noisy.

questions.q_zh: 站在桥中间能看到什么？
questions.q_en: What can you see from the middle of the bridge?

options.text: 城市灯光倒映在河水里
options.pinyin: chéngshì dēngguāng dào yìng zài héshuǐ lǐ
options.text_en: city lights reflected in the river

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「城市灯光倒映在河水里」, indicating city lights reflected in the river.

questions.q_zh: 这种感情最可能来自什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most likely source of this feeling?
questions.explanation: The passage describes near where i live there's an old bridge, which reveals that feeling.

title: The Plant That Won't Die
text_zh: 我养植物一直养不好。仙人掌被我养死了，多肉也被我养死了。朋友们都说我是「植物杀手」。但是有一天，我在办公桌上发现了一盆绿色的小植物。不知道是谁放的。我决定试一试。第一个星期，我忘了浇水。它还活着。第二个星期，我浇了太多水。它还活着。第三个星期，我把它放在了没有阳光的角落里。它还活着。我越来越佩服这盆植物。一个月以后，我问同事：「这是什么植物？这么厉害？」同事看了一眼，笑着说：「那是塑料的。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǎng zhíwù yìzhí yǎng bù hǎo. Xiānrénzhǎng bèi wǒ yǎng sǐ le, duōròu yě bèi wǒ yǎng sǐ le. Péngyoumen dōu shuō wǒ shì「zhíwù shāshǒu」. Dànshì yǒu yì tiān, wǒ zài bàngōng zhuō shàng fāxiàn le yì pén lǜsè de xiǎo zhíwù. Bù zhīdào shì shéi fàng de. Wǒ juédìng shì yí shì. Dì-yī gè xīngqī, wǒ wàng le jiāo shuǐ. Tā hái huózhe. Dì-èr gè xīngqī, wǒ jiāo le tài duō shuǐ. Tā hái huózhe. Dì-sān gè xīngqī, wǒ bǎ tā fàng zài le méiyǒu yángguāng de jiǎoluò lǐ. Tā hái huózhe. Wǒ yuè lái yuè pèifú zhè pén zhíwù. Yí gè yuè yǐhòu, wǒ wèn tóngshì:「Zhè shì shénme zhíwù? Zhème lìhai?」Tóngshì kàn le yì yǎn, xiàozhe shuō:「Nà shì sùliào de.」
text_en: I've always been terrible at keeping plants alive. I killed a cactus, killed a succulent. Friends all call me the 'plant killer.' But one day I found a small green plant on my desk. No idea who put it there. I decided to try. Week one, I forgot to water it. Still alive. Week two, I watered too much. Still alive. Week three, I put it in a sunless corner. Still alive. I grew more and more impressed by this plant. A month later, I asked a colleague: 'What kind of plant is this? It's amazing!' The colleague glanced at it and laughed: 'That's plastic.'

questions.q_zh: 朋友们叫「我」什么？
questions.q_en: What do friends call 'me'?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「植物杀手」, indicating plant killer.

questions.q_zh: 为什么那盆植物一直不死？
questions.q_en: Why didn't the plant die?

options.text_en: colleague helped take care of it

questions.explanation: 因为 (because) directly states the reason: because it's plastic.

questions.q_zh: 「我」之前养死了什么植物？
questions.q_en: What plants did 'I' kill before?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「仙人掌和多肉」, indicating cactus and succulent.

title: The Soup Grandmother Taught Me
text_zh: 我小时候身体不太好，经常感冒。每次感冒的时候，奶奶都会给我煮一碗姜汤。她的做法比较特别：先把姜切成很薄的片，然后放一点红糖，最后加两个大枣。她说：「这个汤喝完身体会暖暖的，感冒好得比较快。」长大以后我自己也学会了做这个汤。每次觉得不舒服或者天气冷的时候，我就做一碗。但是不知道为什么，味道总是跟奶奶做的不太一样。后来我想明白了——不是味道不一样，是喝的时候旁边没有奶奶坐着。有些东西的味道，不在锅里，在人心里。
text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎo shíhou shēntǐ bú tài hǎo, jīngcháng gǎnmào. Měi cì gǎnmào de shíhou, nǎinai dōu huì gěi wǒ zhǔ yì wǎn jiāng tāng. Tā de zuòfǎ bǐjiào tèbié: xiān bǎ jiāng qiē chéng hěn báo de piàn, ránhòu fàng yìdiǎn hóngtáng, zuìhòu jiā liǎng gè dà zǎo. Tā shuō:「Zhège tāng hē wán shēntǐ huì nuǎnnuǎn de, gǎnmào hǎo de bǐjiào kuài.」Zhǎng dà yǐhòu wǒ zìjǐ yě xuéhuì le zuò zhège tāng. Měi cì juéde bù shūfu huòzhě tiānqì lěng de shíhou, wǒ jiù zuò yì wǎn. Dànshì bù zhīdào wèishénme, wèidào zǒng shì gēn nǎinai zuò de bú tài yíyàng. Hòulái wǒ xiǎng míngbai le——búshì wèidào bù yíyàng, shì hē de shíhou pángbiān méiyǒu nǎinai zuòzhe. Yǒuxiē dōngxi de wèidào, bú zài guō lǐ, zài rén xīnlǐ.
text_en: When I was little my health wasn't great — I caught colds often. Every time I was sick, Grandmother would make me ginger soup. Her method was rather special: first slice ginger thin, add some brown sugar, then two red dates. She'd say: 'After drinking this your body gets warm, and the cold gets better faster.' As an adult I learned to make it myself. Whenever I feel unwell or the weather's cold, I make a bowl. But somehow it never tastes quite like Grandmother's. Later I figured it out — it's not that the taste is different. It's that when I drink it, Grandmother isn't sitting beside me. Some things get their taste not from the pot, but from the heart.

questions.q_zh: 奶奶做的姜汤里放了什么？
questions.q_en: What did Grandmother put in the ginger soup?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「姜、红糖、大枣」, indicating ginger, brown sugar, red dates.

questions.q_zh: 「我」自己做的汤味道怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does the soup 'I' make taste?

options.pinyin: bǐ nǎinai zuò de hǎo hē

options.text: 总是跟奶奶做的不太一样
options.pinyin: zǒng shì gēn nǎinai zuò de bú tài yíyàng
options.text_en: always a bit different from Grandmother's

questions.explanation: 后来我想明白了——不是味道不一样 reveals the narrator's realization: the difference isn't really in taste but in the irreplaceable context of Grandmother making it.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得味道不一样的原因是什么？
questions.q_en: Why does 'I' think it tastes different?

options.text: 喝的时候旁边没有奶奶坐着
options.pinyin: hē de shíhou pángbiān méiyǒu nǎinai zuòzhe
options.text_en: Grandmother isn't sitting beside when drinking

questions.explanation: The key phrase 「喝的时候旁边没有奶奶」 gives the reason — Grandmother isn't sitting beside when drinking.

title: The Dog Walker's Route
text_zh: 每天傍晚六点半，我都会在小区里遛狗。走了三年同一条路以后，我发现了一个有趣的事情——我遇到的人和事每天都不一样，但是路线从来没变过。比如，星期一我总是在花园的转角遇到刘奶奶和她的小白狗。星期三的傍晚，快递员的车会停在三号楼前面。星期五晚上，一楼的灯总是最先亮。有一天我决定换一条路走。结果什么都不一样了——遇到的人不认识了，花也不是我看惯了的那些。我走了十分钟就回来了。我走了十分钟就回来了。还是这条路好。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān bàngwǎn liù diǎn bàn, wǒ dōu huì zài xiǎoqū lǐ liú gǒu. Zǒu le sān nián tóng yì tiáo lù yǐhòu, wǒ fāxiàn le yí gè yǒuqù de shìqing——wǒ yù dào de rén hé shì měi tiān dōu bù yíyàng, dànshì lùxiàn cónglái méi biàn guò. Bǐrú, xīngqī yī wǒ zǒng shì zài huāyuán de zhuǎnjiǎo yù dào Liú nǎinai hé tā de xiǎo bái gǒu. Xīngqī sān de bàngwǎn, kuàidìyuán de chē huì tíng zài sān hào lóu qiánmiàn. Xīngqī wǔ wǎnshang, yī lóu de dēng zǒng shì zuì xiān liàng. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ juédìng huàn yì tiáo lù zǒu. Jiéguǒ shénme dōu bù yíyàng le——yù dào de rén bú rènshi le, huā yě búshì wǒ kàn guàn le de nàxiē. Wǒ zǒu le shí fēnzhōng jiù huílái le. Wǒ hūrán míngbai, xíguàn búshì yīnwèi lǎn, ér shì yīnwèi nà tiáo lù shàng yǒu wǒ zàihū de dōngxi.
text_en: Every evening at six-thirty I walk my dog around the complex. After three years on the same route, I noticed something interesting — the people and events I encounter differ daily, but the route never changes. For example, on Mondays I always meet Grandma Liu and her little white dog at the garden corner. Wednesday evenings, the delivery truck parks in front of Building 3. Friday nights, the first-floor lights are always first to turn on. One day I decided to take a different route. Everything changed — didn't recognize anyone, the flowers weren't the ones I was used to. I came back after ten minutes. I came back after ten minutes. This road is better.

questions.q_zh: 「我」走了多久同一条路？
questions.q_en: How long has 'I' walked the same route?
questions.explanation: 同一条路走了三年 states the timeframe directly — three years of walking the same evening route with the dog.

questions.q_zh: 换了路以后怎么样？
questions.q_en: What happened after changing routes?

options.pinyin: fāxiàn le gèng hǎo de lùxiàn

options.text: 什么都不一样了，十分钟就回来了
options.pinyin: shénme dōu bù yíyàng le, shí fēnzhōng jiù huílái le
options.text_en: everything was different, came back in ten minutes

options.pinyin: yù dào le gèng duō yǒuqù de rén

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「什么都不一样了」, indicating everything was different, came back in ten minutes.

questions.q_en: What does the repeated behavior imply?

others require them to do it

title: The Language I Dream In
text_zh: 我在国外住了五年。刚来的时候，我做梦都是用中文。后来慢慢地，梦里开始出现英文了。有一天我做了一个梦，梦里我跟一个人说英文，但是说到一半忽然不知道一个词怎么说了，就换成了中文。那个人听不懂，我就一边比划一边说。醒来以后，我觉得比较有意思。我跟朋友说了这件事，朋友问我：「那你现在想事情用什么语言？」我想了想，发现我想简单的事情用英文，但是想到家人和小时候的事情，还是会自动用中文。也许语言不只是工具——它还是我们放感情的地方。越深的感情，越用最早学会的语言来装。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài guówài zhù le wǔ nián. Gāng lái de shíhou, wǒ zuòmèng dōu shì yòng Zhōngwén. Hòulái mànman de, mèng lǐ kāishǐ chūxiàn Yīngwén le. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ zuò le yí gè mèng, mèng lǐ wǒ gēn yí gè rén shuō Yīngwén, dànshì shuō dào yíbàn hūrán bù zhīdào yí gè cí zěnme shuō le, jiù huàn chéng le Zhōngwén. Nàge rén tīng bù dǒng, wǒ jiù yìbiān bǐhua yìbiān shuō. Xǐng lái yǐhòu, wǒ juéde bǐjiào yǒuyìsi. Wǒ gēn péngyou shuō le zhè jiàn shì, péngyou wèn wǒ:「Nà nǐ xiànzài xiǎng shìqing yòng shénme yǔyán?」Wǒ xiǎng le xiǎng, fāxiàn wǒ xiǎng jiǎndān de shìqing yòng Yīngwén, dànshì xiǎng dào jiārén hé xiǎo shíhou de shìqing, háishi huì zìdòng yòng Zhōngwén. Yěxǔ yǔyán bù zhǐshì gōngjù——tā háishi wǒmen fàng gǎnqíng de dìfang. Yuè shēn de gǎnqíng, yuè yòng zuì zǎo xuéhuì de yǔyán lái zhuāng.
text_en: I've lived abroad for five years. When I first arrived, I dreamed only in Chinese. Gradually, English started appearing in my dreams. One day I dreamed I was speaking English to someone, but halfway through I couldn't find a word and switched to Chinese. The person didn't understand, so I was gesturing while speaking. After waking up, I found it rather interesting. I told a friend, who asked: 'So what language do you think in now?' I thought about it and realized I think simple things in English, but when I think of family and childhood, Chinese comes automatically. Maybe language isn't just a tool — it's also where we keep our feelings. The deeper the emotion, the more it uses the language we learned first.

questions.q_zh: 刚到国外的时候，做梦用什么语言？
questions.q_en: What language did 'I' dream in when first abroad?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「中文」, meaning Chinese — even abroad, dreams came in the native language at first.

questions.q_zh: 在梦里说英文说到一半怎么了？
questions.q_en: What happened mid-conversation in the dream?

options.text: 忘了一个词，换成了中文
options.pinyin: wàng le yí gè cí, huàn chéng le Zhōngwén
options.text_en: forgot a word, switched to Chinese

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「换成了中文」, indicating forgot a word, switched to Chinese.

questions.q_zh: 这些人之间最重要的是什么？
questions.q_en: What matters most between these people?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「互相理解和支持」, indicating mutual understanding and support.

title: The Street Lamp Repairman
text_zh: 我下班回家的路上有一段路很黑，因为路灯坏了。我走了好几天，都是摸黑走。有一天晚上，我看到一个人站在路灯下面，开着一辆小车，车上有梯子。他正在修路灯。我站在旁边看了一会儿。他爬上梯子，把灯泡换了，灯就亮了。整条路忽然变得比较亮了。他从梯子上下来，看了看灯，满意地点了点头。我跟他说了一声谢谢。他笑着说：「不用谢我，这是我的工作。每天晚上城市里有很多灯坏了，我就开着车一盏一盏地修。」我看着他开着车去修下一盏灯，忽然觉得，这座城市的夜晚，是被这样的人一盏一盏地照亮的。
text_pinyin: Wǒ xiàbān huí jiā de lù shàng yǒu yí duàn lù hěn hēi, yīnwèi lùdēng huài le. Wǒ zǒu le hǎo jǐ tiān, dōu shì mō hēi zǒu. Yǒu yì tiān wǎnshang, wǒ kàn dào yí gè rén zhàn zài lùdēng xiàmiàn, kāizhe yì liàng xiǎo chē, chē shàng yǒu tīzi. Tā zhèngzài xiū lùdēng. Wǒ zhàn zài pángbiān kàn le yíhuìr. Tā pá shàng tīzi, bǎ dēngpào huàn le, dēng jiù liàng le. Zhěng tiáo lù hūrán biàn de bǐjiào liàng le. Tā cóng tīzi shàng xiàlái, kàn le kàn dēng, mǎnyì de diǎn le diǎn tóu. Wǒ gēn tā shuō le yì shēng xièxie. Tā xiàozhe shuō:「Bú yòng xiè wǒ, zhè shì wǒ de gōngzuò. Měi tiān wǎnshang chéngshì lǐ yǒu hěn duō dēng huài le, wǒ jiù kāizhe chē yì zhǎn yì zhǎn de xiū.」Wǒ kànzhe tā kāizhe chē qù xiū xià yì zhǎn dēng, hūrán juéde, zhè zuò chéngshì de yèwǎn, shì bèi zhèyàng de rén yì zhǎn yì zhǎn de zhào liàng de.
text_en: On my way home there's a stretch of dark road — the street light was broken. One evening I saw a man with a small truck and ladder fixing it. He climbed up, changed the bulb, and the light came on. The whole road got bright. He climbed down, looked at the light, and nodded. I thanked him. He smiled: 'No need — it's my job. Every night there are many broken lights in the city. I just drive around fixing them one by one.' I watched him drive off and felt that this city's nights are lit up by people like him, one lamp at a time.

questions.q_zh: 那段路为什么很黑？
questions.q_en: Why was that stretch dark?
questions.explanation: 因为 (because) directly states the reason: street light was broken.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以推断人物是什么样的人？
questions.q_en: What kind of person can we infer the character is?
questions.explanation: The detail 「细心而有耐心」 reveals the underlying meaning — careful and patient.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得城市的夜晚是被谁照亮的？
questions.q_en: Who does 'I' think lights up the city's nights?

options.text: 像修路灯的人这样的人
options.pinyin: xiàng xiū lùdēng de rén zhèyàng de rén
options.text_en: people like the street light repairman

options.pinyin: zhù zài chéngshì lǐ de rén

questions.explanation: The passage identifies this by saying on my way home there's a stretch of dark road — the street light was broken.

title: The Mailbox Nobody Uses
text_zh: 我们小区的一楼有一排信箱，但是大部分都生锈了。现在谁还寄信呢？但是有一个信箱比较干净——三零二室的。我注意到每个星期三下午，有一个年轻人会来开那个信箱，拿出一封信。有一天我忍不住问他：「还有人给你寄信吗？」他笑着说：「是我自己给自己寄的。我每个星期写一封信，写下这个星期发生的事情和我的想法，然后寄给自己。一个星期以后收到的时候，我就可以用另一种角度看自己了。」我觉得这比较有意思——给未来的自己写信，就像跟一个星期以后的自己聊天。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xiǎoqū de yī lóu yǒu yì pái xìnxiāng, dànshì dàbùfen dōu shēng xiù le. Xiànzài shéi hái jì xìn ne? Dànshì yǒu yí gè xìnxiāng bǐjiào gānjìng——sān líng èr shì de. Wǒ zhùyì dào měi gè xīngqī sān xiàwǔ, yǒu yí gè niánqīng rén huì lái kāi nàge xìnxiāng, ná chū yì fēng xìn. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ rěn bú zhù wèn tā:「Hái yǒu rén gěi nǐ jì xìn ma?」Tā xiàozhe shuō:「Shì wǒ zìjǐ gěi zìjǐ jì de. Wǒ měi gè xīngqī xiě yì fēng xìn, xiě xià zhège xīngqī fāshēng de shìqing hé wǒ de xiǎngfǎ, ránhòu jì gěi zìjǐ. Yí gè xīngqī yǐhòu shōu dào de shíhou, wǒ jiù kěyǐ yòng lìng yì zhǒng jiǎodù kàn zìjǐ le.」Wǒ juéde zhè bǐjiào yǒuyìsi——gěi wèilái de zìjǐ xiě xìn, jiù xiàng gēn yí gè xīngqī yǐhòu de zìjǐ liáotiān.
text_en: On the first floor of our complex there's a row of mailboxes, but most are rusty. Who sends letters anymore? Yet one mailbox is rather clean — unit 302's. I noticed every Wednesday afternoon, a young man opens it and takes out a letter. One day I asked: 'Does someone still write to you?' He smiled: 'I write to myself. Every week I write a letter about what happened and my thoughts, then mail it to myself. When I receive it a week later, I can see myself from a different angle.' I found this rather interesting — writing to your future self is like chatting with yourself a week from now.

questions.q_zh: 大部分信箱怎么样了？
questions.q_en: What happened to most mailboxes?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「生锈了」, meaning rusted — most mailboxes have deteriorated from disuse.

questions.q_zh: 那个年轻人的信是谁寄的？
questions.q_en: Who sends the young man's letters?

options.pinyin: tā zìjǐ gěi zìjǐ jì de

questions.explanation: The passage identifies this by saying every week i write a letter about what happened and my thoughts, then mail it to myself.

questions.q_zh: 他为什么这样做？
questions.q_en: Why does he do this?

options.text: 想用另一种角度看自己
options.pinyin: xiǎng yòng lìng yì zhǒng jiǎodù kàn zìjǐ
options.text_en: wants to see himself from a different angle

questions.explanation: The key phrase 「想用另一种角度看自己」 gives the reason — wants to see himself from a different angle.

title: The Bicycle Lane
text_zh: 我们城市去年新修了一条自行车道，从城东一直到城西。刚修好的时候，很少有人骑。大家还是习惯开车或者坐地铁。但是慢慢地，越来越多的人开始骑自行车了。早上七八点的时候，自行车道上全是骑车上班的人。有一个同事以前每天开车上班要四十分钟，现在骑自行车只要三十分钟，而且不用找停车位。他说：「骑车的时候能感觉到风，还能看到路边的花。开车的时候什么都看不到，只能看到前面的车。」自行车道旁边种了很多树，春天的时候骑在上面，比较舒服。有时候慢一点，反而能看到更多。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen chéngshì qùnián xīn xiū le yì tiáo zìxíngchē dào, cóng chéng dōng yìzhí dào chéng xī. Gāng xiū hǎo de shíhou, hěn shǎo yǒu rén qí. Dàjiā háishi xíguàn kāi chē huòzhě zuò dìtiě. Dànshì mànman de, yuè lái yuè duō de rén kāishǐ qí zìxíngchē le. Zǎoshang qī bā diǎn de shíhou, zìxíngchē dào shàng quán shì qí chē shàngbān de rén. Yǒu yí gè tóngshì yǐqián měi tiān kāi chē shàngbān yào sìshí fēnzhōng, xiànzài qí zìxíngchē zhǐ yào sānshí fēnzhōng, érqiě bú yòng zhǎo tíng chē wèi. Tā shuō:「Qí chē de shíhou néng gǎnjué dào fēng, hái néng kàn dào lù biān de huā. Kāi chē de shíhou shénme dōu kàn bú dào, zhǐ néng kàn dào qiánmiàn de chē.」Zìxíngchē dào pángbiān zhòng le hěn duō shù, chūntiān de shíhou qí zài shàngmiàn, bǐjiào shūfu. Yǒu shíhou màn yìdiǎn, fǎn'ér néng kàn dào gèng duō.
text_en: Our city built a new bike lane last year, running from east to west. When first completed, few people rode. Everyone was still used to driving or taking the subway. But gradually more and more people started cycling. At seven or eight in the morning, the bike lane is full of commuters. One colleague used to drive forty minutes to work — now she bikes in thirty, and doesn't need to find parking. She says: 'When you bike you feel the wind, you see flowers along the road. When you drive you see nothing except the car ahead.' The bike lane has many trees planted alongside it — riding in spring is rather comfortable. Sometimes going slower, you actually see more.

questions.q_zh: 根据这段话，人物接下来最可能做什么？
questions.q_en: Based on this passage, what would the character most likely do next?

options.pinyin: kāishǐ zuò wánquán bùtóng de shì

questions.explanation: The narrator's colleague switched to cycling, the neighbor started riding with her daughter — people are gradually choosing the bike lane and enjoying it, suggesting the character would continue savoring this trend.

questions.q_zh: 同事为什么改骑自行车？
questions.q_en: Why did the colleague switch to cycling?

options.text: 骑车比开车快，还不用找停车位
options.pinyin: qí chē bǐ kāi chē kuài, hái bú yòng zhǎo tíng chē wèi
options.text_en: faster than driving, no need for parking

questions.explanation: The colleague explains 现在她骑车三十分钟就到，也不用找停车位 — cycling takes thirty minutes and eliminates the need to find parking, making it faster and simpler than driving.

questions.q_zh: 如果另一个人来到同样的场景，会有同样的感受吗？
questions.q_en: Would another person in the same setting have the same feelings?

options.text: 不一定，取决于个人经历
options.pinyin: bù yídìng, qǔjué yú gèrén jīnglì
options.text_en: not necessarily, it depends on personal experience

questions.explanation: Their behavior — she says: 'when you bike you feel the wind, you see flowers along the road — shows that attitude.

title: The Birthday Surprise
text_zh: 今天是同事小王的生日。我们几个人决定给他一个惊喜。计划很简单：中午的时候，我们假装去开会，把他一个人留在办公室。然后带着蛋糕回来，唱生日歌。但是出了两个问题。第一，蛋糕上的名字被写错了——写成了「小黄」，不是「小王」。第二，我们回到办公室的时候，小王已经出去吃午饭了。我们只好带着蛋糕去餐厅找他。他正在吃面。我们端着蛋糕进去的时候，整个餐厅的人都在看我们。小王看了一眼蛋糕，嘴里还含着面条。蛋糕上写的是「小黄」。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān shì tóngshì Xiǎo Wáng de shēngrì. Wǒmen jǐ gè rén juédìng gěi tā yí gè jīngxǐ. Jìhuà hěn jiǎndān: zhōngwǔ de shíhou, wǒmen jiǎzhuāng qù kāi huì, bǎ tā yí gè rén liú zài bàngōngshì. Ránhòu dàizhe dàngāo huílái, chàng shēngrì gē. Dànshì chū le liǎng gè wèntí. Dì-yī, dàngāo shàng de míngzi bèi xiě cuò le——xiě chéng le「Xiǎo Huáng」, búshì「Xiǎo Wáng」. Dì-èr, wǒmen huí dào bàngōngshì de shíhou, Xiǎo Wáng yǐjīng chūqù chī wǔfàn le. Wǒmen zhǐhǎo dàizhe dàngāo qù cāntīng zhǎo tā. Tā zhèngzài chī miàn. Wǒmen duānzhe dàngāo jìnqù de shíhou, zhěng gè cāntīng de rén dōu zài kàn wǒmen. Xiǎo Wáng xiān shì lèng le yíxià, ránhòu kàn dào dàngāo shàng de míngzi, xiào de chàdiǎn bǎ miàn pēn chūlái. Tā shuō:「Zhè shì wǒ shōu dào de zuì hǎoxiào de jīngxǐ.」
text_en: Today is colleague Xiao Wang's birthday. A few of us decided to surprise him. Simple plan: at noon, we'd pretend to go to a meeting, leaving him alone. Then come back with cake and sing. But two things went wrong. First, the name on the cake was wrong — it said 'Xiao Huang' instead of 'Xiao Wang.' Second, when we returned to the office, Xiao Wang had already gone out for lunch. We had to take the cake to the restaurant to find him. He was eating noodles. When we walked in carrying the cake, the entire restaurant stared. Xiao Wang looked at the cake, noodles still hanging from his mouth. The cake said 'Xiao Huang.'

questions.q_zh: 蛋糕上的名字写的是什么？
questions.q_en: What name was on the cake?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「小黄」, indicating Xiao Huang.

questions.q_zh: 他们回到办公室发现了什么？
questions.q_en: What did they find when they returned to the office?

options.text: 小王已经出去吃午饭了
options.pinyin: Xiǎo Wáng yǐjīng chūqù chī wǔfàn le
options.text_en: Xiao Wang had gone to lunch
Xiao Wang was in a meeting

questions.explanation: 「小王已经出去吃午饭了」 gives the time — Xiao Wang had gone to lunch.

questions.q_zh: 小王觉得这个惊喜怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did Xiao Wang feel about the surprise?
questions.explanation: The passage describes a few of us decided to surprise him, which reveals that feeling.

title: The Old Man and His Camera
text_zh: 公园里有一个老人，每天都带着一台很旧的相机来拍照。他不拍风景，只拍人。他拍在长椅上看书的人，拍在草地上玩的孩子，拍跳广场舞的阿姨们。有人问他：「为什么不拍风景？树和花比较好看啊。」他说：「花明年还会开，但是今天这个在椅子上睡着的老人，明年不一定还在这里。」他把拍的照片打印出来，送给被拍的人。有一次他给一个带孩子的妈妈送了一张照片。照片里她正在给孩子擦嘴，笑得很温柔。她看了以后红了眼眶，说：「平时都是我给别人拍照，从来没有人帮我拍过这样的照片。」
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yí gè lǎorén, měi tiān dōu dàizhe yì tái hěn jiù de xiàngjī lái pāi zhào. Tā bù pāi fēngjǐng, zhǐ pāi rén. Tā pāi zài cháng yǐ shàng kàn shū de rén, pāi zài cǎodì shàng wán de háizi, pāi tiào guǎngchǎng wǔ de āyímen. Yǒu rén wèn tā:「Wèishénme bù pāi fēngjǐng? Shù hé huā bǐjiào hǎokàn a.」Tā shuō:「Huā míngnián hái huì kāi, dànshì jīntiān zhège zài yǐzi shàng shuìzháo de lǎorén, míngnián bù yídìng hái zài zhèlǐ.」Tā bǎ pāi de zhàopiàn dǎyìn chūlái, sòng gěi bèi pāi de rén. Yǒu yí cì tā gěi yí gè dài háizi de māma sòng le yì zhāng zhàopiàn. Zhàopiàn lǐ tā zhèngzài gěi háizi cā zuǐ, xiào de hěn wēnróu. Tā kàn le yǐhòu hóng le yǎnkuàng, shuō:「Píngshí dōu shì wǒ gěi biéren pāi zhào, cónglái méiyǒu rén bāng wǒ pāi guò zhèyàng de zhàopiàn.」
text_en: An old man comes to the park every day with a old camera to take photos. He doesn't photograph scenery — only people. He photographs people reading on benches, children playing on grass, aunties doing square dancing. Someone asked: 'Why not photograph scenery? Trees and flowers are prettier.' He said: 'Flowers will bloom again next year, but this old man sleeping on the bench may not be here next year.' He prints his photos and gives them to his subjects. Once he gave a photo to a mother with her child. In it she was wiping her child's mouth, smiling tenderly. She teared up and said: 'Usually I'm the one taking photos of others. Nobody has ever taken a photo like this of me.'

questions.q_zh: 这种感情最可能来自什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most likely source of this feeling?
questions.explanation: The passage describes an old man comes to the park every day with a old camera to take photos, which reveals that feeling.

questions.q_zh: 老人为什么不拍风景？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the old man photograph scenery?

options.text: 花明年还会开，但人不一定还在
options.pinyin: huā míngnián hái huì kāi, dàn rén bù yídìng hái zài

options.text_en: nobody had ever taken a photo like this of her

title: The Rice Grandmother Made
text_zh: 我外婆做的米饭跟别人的不一样。她不用电饭锅，用的是一个很旧的铁锅。她先把米洗三遍，然后放水——水刚好到她手指第一个关节的位置。她说这是她妈妈教她的。用铁锅做的米饭底下会有一层锅巴，又脆又香。小时候我最喜欢吃锅巴。外婆每次都把锅巴留给我，自己吃上面比较软的饭。我长大以后在城市里工作，用电饭锅做饭，味道总是比较一般。有一次我回家，外婆又给我做了铁锅饭。我吃了一口锅巴，忽然觉得眼睛比较酸。不是因为好吃，是因为那个味道让我想起了小时候的厨房，和站在厨房里的外婆。
text_pinyin: Wǒ wàipó zuò de mǐfàn gēn biéren de bù yíyàng. Tā bú yòng diàn fàn guō, yòng de shì yí gè hěn jiù de tiě guō. Tā xiān bǎ mǐ xǐ sān biàn, ránhòu fàng shuǐ——shuǐ gānghǎo dào tā shǒuzhǐ dì-yī gè guānjié de wèizhì. Tā shuō zhè shì tā māma jiāo tā de. Yòng tiě guō zuò de mǐfàn dǐxia huì yǒu yì céng guōba, yòu cuì yòu xiāng. Xiǎo shíhou wǒ zuì xǐhuan chī guōba. Wàipó měi cì dōu bǎ guōba liú gěi wǒ, zìjǐ chī shàngmiàn bǐjiào ruǎn de fàn. Wǒ zhǎng dà yǐhòu zài chéngshì lǐ gōngzuò, yòng diàn fàn guō zuò fàn, wèidào zǒng shì bǐjiào yìbān. Yǒu yí cì wǒ huí jiā, wàipó yòu gěi wǒ zuò le tiě guō fàn. Wǒ chī le yì kǒu guōba, hūrán juéde yǎnjīng bǐjiào suān. Búshì yīnwèi hǎochī, shì yīnwèi nàge wèidào ràng wǒ xiǎng qǐ le xiǎo shíhou de chúfáng, hé zhàn zài chúfáng lǐ de wàipó.
text_en: My grandmother makes rice differently from everyone else. She doesn't use a rice cooker — she uses an old iron pot. She washes the rice three times, adds water to the first knuckle of her finger. Her mother taught her this. Iron-pot rice has a crispy bottom layer — crunchy and fragrant. As a child, that crust was my favorite. Grandmother always saved it for me. After growing up, I cook with a rice cooker — the taste is ordinary. Once I went home and Grandmother made iron-pot rice again. I took a bite of the crust and my eyes stung. That taste brought back the childhood kitchen, and Grandmother standing in it.

questions.q_zh: 这种坚持说明这个人怎么样？
questions.q_en: What does this persistence tell us about the person?

options.text_en: has no ideas of their own

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「有毅力和决心」, indicating determined and persistent.

questions.q_zh: 如果打破了这个习惯，可能会怎样？
questions.q_en: What might happen if this routine were broken?

options.pinyin: huì zhǎodào gèng hǎo de xíguàn

questions.explanation: Grandmother's rice is made with an iron pot, precise water measurement, and decades of instinct — disrupting that ritual would remove the sensory anchor the family relies on.

questions.q_zh: 「我」回家吃锅巴的时候为什么眼睛酸？
questions.q_en: Why did 'my' eyes sting when eating the crust at home?

options.text: 那个味道让「我」想起了小时候和外婆
options.pinyin: nàge wèidào ràng wǒ xiǎng qǐ le xiǎo shíhou hé wàipó
options.text_en: the taste reminded me of childhood and Grandmother

questions.explanation: 那个味道把小时候的厨房和站在里面的奶奶都带回来了 — the taste of the rice crust transported the narrator back to childhood, to Grandmother's kitchen, triggering the emotional sting.

title: The 24-Hour Laundromat
text_zh: 我家旁边有一家二十四小时的自助洗衣店。白天来的人比较多，大多是学生和上班的年轻人。但是深夜来的人不一样——有刚下夜班的护士，有跑了一天的外卖员，有抱着一堆衣服的单身爸爸。洗衣机转的时候，大家坐在塑料椅子上等。那三十分钟是一段比较奇怪的时间——太短了不值得回家，太长了又有点无聊。我看到那个单身爸爸一边等洗衣服一边给孩子打电话：「爸爸很快就回来了，你先睡。」洗衣机停了，他把衣服叠好，走了。那家店比较旧了，灯也不太亮。但是对很多人来说，它是深夜最后一个开着门的地方。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā pángbiān yǒu yì jiā èrshísì xiǎoshí de zìzhù xǐyī diàn. Báitiān lái de rén bǐjiào duō, dàduō shì xuéshēng hé shàngbān de niánqīng rén. Dànshì shēnyè lái de rén bù yíyàng——yǒu gāng xià yèbān de hùshi, yǒu pǎo le yì tiān de wàimàiyuán, yǒu bàozhe yì duī yīfu de dānshēn bàba. Xǐyījī zhuǎn de shíhou, dàjiā zuò zài sùliào yǐzi shàng děng. Nà sānshí fēnzhōng shì yí duàn bǐjiào qíguài de shíjiān——tài duǎn le bù zhíde huí jiā, tài cháng le yòu yǒudiǎn wúliáo. Wǒ kàn dào nàge dānshēn bàba yìbiān děng xǐ yīfu yìbiān gěi háizi dǎ diànhuà:「Bàba hěn kuài jiù huílái le, nǐ xiān shuì.」Xǐyījī tíng le, tā bǎ yīfu dié hǎo, zǒu le. Nà jiā diàn bǐjiào jiù le, dēng yě bú tài liàng. Dànshì duì hěn duō rén lái shuō, tā shì shēnyè zuìhòu yí gè kāizhe mén de dìfang.
text_en: Next to my place there's a 24-hour self-service laundromat. Daytime customers are mostly students and young workers. But late-night visitors are different — nurses just off night shift, delivery workers who've been running all day, single fathers carrying piles of clothes. While the machines spin, everyone sits on plastic chairs waiting. Those thirty minutes are a rather strange stretch — too short to go home, too long to not be bored. I saw the single father call his child while waiting for laundry: 'Dad will be home soon, go to sleep first.' The machine stopped, he folded the clothes, and left. The shop is rather old, the lights not very bright. But for many people, it's the last place with an open door late at night.

questions.q_zh: 深夜来洗衣店的都是什么人？
questions.q_en: Who comes to the laundromat late at night?

options.text: 下夜班的护士、外卖员、单身爸爸
options.pinyin: xià yèbān de hùshi, wàimàiyuán, dānshēn bàba
options.text_en: off-shift nurses, delivery workers, single fathers

questions.q_zh: 单身爸爸等洗衣服的时候在做什么？
questions.q_en: What was the single father doing while waiting?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「给孩子打电话」, indicating calling his child.

questions.q_zh: 关系中的小细节说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What do the small details in the relationship suggest?

options.text_en: they don't really like each other

questions.explanation: 对...来说 (for someone) frames the perspective — they know each other well.

title: The Sound of Morning
text_zh: 如果你五点半醒来，城市还在睡觉。但是如果你仔细听，你会发现早晨有很多声音。五点半的时候，环卫工人的扫地声是第一个声音——沙沙沙的，很有节奏。五点四十五分左右，第一班公交车开过去了，引擎的声音低低的。六点钟的时候，早餐摊开始了，油锅的声音滋滋响。六点半，遛狗的人出来了，狗的指甲在地上哒哒哒。七点，小区里的灯一盏一盏地亮了。我有一段时间每天五点半醒来，不是因为闹钟，而是因为窗外那些声音叫醒了我。我后来才明白，城市从来不是被阳光叫醒的——它是被人叫醒的。
text_pinyin: Rúguǒ nǐ wǔ diǎn bàn xǐng lái, chéngshì hái zài shuì jiào. Dànshì rúguǒ nǐ zǐxì tīng, nǐ huì fāxiàn zǎochén yǒu hěn duō shēngyīn. Wǔ diǎn bàn de shíhou, huánwèi gōngrén de sǎo dì shēng shì dì-yī gè shēngyīn——shāshāshā de, hěn yǒu jiézòu. Wǔ diǎn sìshíwǔ fēn zuǒyòu, dì-yī bān gōngjiāo chē kāi guòqu le, yǐnqíng de shēngyīn dī dī de. Liù diǎn zhōng de shíhou, zǎocān tān kāishǐ le, yóu guō de shēngyīn zīzī xiǎng. Liù diǎn bàn, liú gǒu de rén chūlái le, gǒu de zhǐjia zài dì shàng dādādā. Qī diǎn, xiǎoqū lǐ de dēng yì zhǎn yì zhǎn de liàng le. Wǒ yǒu yí duàn shíjiān měi tiān wǔ diǎn bàn xǐng lái, búshì yīnwèi nàozhōng, ér shì yīnwèi chuāng wài nàxiē shēngyīn jiào xǐng le wǒ. Wǒ hòulái cái míngbai, chéngshì cónglái búshì bèi yángguāng jiào xǐng de——tā shì bèi rén jiào xǐng de.
text_en: If you wake at five-thirty, the city is still sleeping. But if you listen carefully, you'll find the morning has many sounds. At five-thirty, the sweeping of sanitation workers is the first sound — swish swish swish, very rhythmic. Around five-forty-five, the first bus passes, its engine humming low. At six, breakfast stalls start up, oil sizzling in the pan. At six-thirty, dog walkers come out, dog nails clicking on the ground. At seven, the lights in the complex turn on one by one. For a while I woke at five-thirty every day — not because of an alarm, but because those sounds outside the window woke me. I later understood: the city is never woken by sunlight — it's woken by people.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的'安静'不只是物理上的安静，还代表什么？
questions.q_en: The 'quiet' in this passage is not just physical silence — what else does it represent?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「内心的平和」, indicating inner peace.

questions.q_zh: 六点钟的时候能听到什么？
questions.q_en: What can you hear at six o'clock?

options.pinyin: zǎocān tān yóu guō de shēngyīn

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「早餐摊油锅的声音」, indicating breakfast stall oil sizzling.

questions.q_zh: 天气在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the weather play in this passage?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「反映人物的心情」, indicating reflects the character's mood.

title: The Forgotten Garden
text_zh: 城市东边有一块空地，以前是一个工厂。工厂搬走以后，那块地就空了。但是慢慢地，不知道谁开始在那里种花。先是几朵向日葵，然后是一排月季。等我发现的时候，那里已经变成了一个小花园。附近的老人经常来浇水、拔草。没有人组织，也没有人管理。大家只是觉得空地太难看了，就自己动手种点东西。有一天我问一个正在浇花的阿姨：「这些花是你种的吗？」她说：「我只种了那边几棵。但是你看，每个人种一点，慢慢地就变成了一个花园。」那个花园比较小也比较乱，但是每一棵花都是有人在乎的。
text_pinyin: Chéngshì dōngbiān yǒu yí kuài kōngdì, yǐqián shì yí gè gōngchǎng. Gōngchǎng bān zǒu yǐhòu, nà kuài dì jiù kōng le. Dànshì mànman de, bù zhīdào shéi kāishǐ zài nàlǐ zhòng huā. Xiān shì jǐ duǒ xiàngrìkuí, ránhòu shì yì pái yuèjì. Děng wǒ fāxiàn de shíhou, nàlǐ yǐjīng biàn chéng le yí gè xiǎo huāyuán. Fùjìn de lǎorén jīngcháng lái jiāo shuǐ, bá cǎo. Méiyǒu rén zǔzhī, yě méiyǒu rén guǎnlǐ. Dàjiā zhǐshì juéde kōngdì tài nánkàn le, jiù zìjǐ dòngshǒu zhòng diǎn dōngxi. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ wèn yí gè zhèngzài jiāo huā de āyí:「Zhèxiē huā shì nǐ zhòng de ma?」Tā shuō:「Wǒ zhǐ zhòng le nàbiān jǐ kē. Dànshì nǐ kàn, měi gè rén zhòng yìdiǎn, mànman de jiù biàn chéng le yí gè huāyuán.」Nàge huāyuán bǐjiào xiǎo yě bǐjiào luàn, dànshì měi yì kē huā dōu shì yǒu rén zàihū de.
text_en: On the city's east side there's an empty lot that used to be a factory. After the factory moved, the land sat empty. But gradually, someone started planting flowers there. First a few sunflowers, then a row of roses. By the time I noticed, it had become a small garden. Elderly neighbors come to water and pull weeds. Nobody organized it, nobody manages it. People just felt the empty lot was too ugly and started planting things themselves. One day I asked an auntie who was watering: 'Did you plant these flowers?' She said: 'I only planted a few over there. But look — everyone plants a little, and slowly it becomes a garden.' That garden is rather small and rather messy, but every flower is cared for by someone.

questions.q_zh: 那块空地以前是什么？
questions.q_en: What was the empty lot before?
questions.explanation: 城市东边有一块空地，以前是一个工厂 directly identifies the lot's history — it was formerly a factory.

questions.q_zh: 花园是怎么形成的？
questions.q_en: How was the garden formed?

options.text: 每个人自己种一点，慢慢变成的
options.pinyin: měi gè rén zìjǐ zhòng yìdiǎn, mànman biàn chéng de
options.text_en: everyone planted a little, gradually formed

questions.explanation: The passage describes but look — everyone plants a little, and slowly it becomes a garden, showing how.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得这个花园怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does 'I' describe the garden?

options.text: 比较小也比较乱，但每棵花都有人在乎
options.pinyin: bǐjiào xiǎo yě bǐjiào luàn, dàn měi kē huā dōu yǒu rén zàihū
options.text_en: small and messy, but every flower is cared for

questions.explanation: The passage describes ' that garden is rather small and rather messy, but every flower is cared for by someone, showing how.

title: The Footprints in the Snow
text_zh: 下雪那天早上，我发现阳台上有一排小脚印。脚印从栏杆那里开始，走到花盆旁边，又走回去了。我住在四楼——谁会爬上来呢？我觉得比较奇怪，就去问了邻居。楼下的王叔叔说他也看到了，但是在三楼的阳台上。我们两个一起去看了一楼的阳台——也有！而且一楼的阳台上，花盆旁边还有一个小洞。我蹲下来看了看——洞里面有几颗花生。原来是松鼠！小区旁边有一棵很大的松树，松鼠从树上跳到阳台上来，在花盆的土里藏花生过冬。这个小谜题比较简单，但是看到答案的时候，我笑了很久。
text_pinyin: Xià xuě nà tiān zǎoshang, wǒ fāxiàn yángtái shàng yǒu yì pái xiǎo jiǎoyìn. Jiǎoyìn cóng lángān nàlǐ kāishǐ, zǒu dào huāpén pángbiān, yòu zǒu huíqu le. Wǒ zhù zài sì lóu——shéi huì pá shànglái ne? Wǒ juéde bǐjiào qíguài, jiù qù wèn le línjū. Lóu xià de Wáng shūshu shuō tā yě kàn dào le, dànshì zài sān lóu de yángtái shàng. Wǒmen liǎng gè yìqǐ qù kàn le yī lóu de yángtái——yě yǒu! Érqiě yī lóu de yángtái shàng, huāpén pángbiān hái yǒu yí gè xiǎo dòng. Wǒ dūn xiàlái kàn le kàn——dòng lǐmiàn yǒu jǐ kē huāshēng. Yuánlái shì sōngshǔ! Xiǎoqū pángbiān yǒu yì kē hěn dà de sōngshù, sōngshǔ cóng shù shàng tiào dào yángtái shàng lái, zài huāpén de tǔ lǐ cáng huāshēng guò dōng. Zhège xiǎo mítí bǐjiào jiǎndān, dànshì kàn dào dá'àn de shíhou, wǒ xiào le hěn jiǔ.
text_en: The morning it snowed, I found small footprints on my balcony. They went from the railing to the flower pot and back. I live on the fourth floor — who would climb up here? Uncle Wang downstairs said he'd seen them too, on the third-floor balcony. We checked the first floor — there too. And next to the flower pot, a small hole with peanuts inside. A squirrel. There's a big pine tree near the complex. The squirrel jumps from the tree to the balconies, burying peanuts in the soil for winter. This little mystery was simple, but when I saw the answer, I laughed.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么不直接说出结论？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the author state the conclusion directly?

options.text_en: to let the reader experience it themselves
the author doesn't know the answer

questions.explanation: Instead of explaining the footprints directly, the author presents the clues — small prints on a fourth-floor balcony, bird seed in the pot — and lets the reader piece together that a bird made them.

questions.q_zh: 花盆旁边的洞里有什么？
questions.q_en: What was in the hole by the flower pot?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「花生」, meaning peanuts — the squirrel had been hiding them by the flower pot.

questions.q_zh: 脚印是谁留下的？
questions.q_en: Who left the footprints?
questions.explanation: 原来 (originally/turns out) reveals a realization — the answer is a squirrel.

title: The Work Persona
text_zh: 上班的时候，我是一个比较安静、说话很少的人。同事们觉得我很严肃，不太好接近。但是下班以后，我跟朋友在一起的时候完全不一样——我会讲笑话，会唱歌，会大声笑。有一次，一个同事在商场里碰到了我和朋友。他看到我在跟朋友开玩笑，非常惊讶，说：「你上班的时候从来不笑的！」我不知道怎么回答。后来我想了很久：上班的我和下班的我，哪个才是真的我？也许都是。我们在不同的地方，需要不同的样子。这不是假装，而是因为每个地方需要的「我」不一样。但是不管哪个我，回到家以后，我只想做最轻松的那一个。
text_pinyin: Shàngbān de shíhou, wǒ shì yí gè bǐjiào ānjìng, shuō huà hěn shǎo de rén. Tóngshìmen juéde wǒ hěn yánsù, bú tài hǎo jiējìn. Dànshì xiàbān yǐhòu, wǒ gēn péngyou zài yìqǐ de shíhou wánquán bù yíyàng——wǒ huì jiǎng xiàohua, huì chàng gē, huì dà shēng xiào. Yǒu yí cì, yí gè tóngshì zài shāngchǎng lǐ pèng dào le wǒ hé péngyou. Tā kàn dào wǒ zài gēn péngyou kāi wánxiào, fēicháng jīngyà, shuō:「Nǐ shàngbān de shíhou cónglái bú xiào de!」Wǒ bù zhīdào zěnme huídá. Hòulái wǒ xiǎng le hěn jiǔ: shàngbān de wǒ hé xiàbān de wǒ, nǎge cái shì zhēn de wǒ? Yěxǔ dōu shì. Wǒmen zài bùtóng de dìfang, xūyào bùtóng de yàngzi. Zhè búshì jiǎzhuāng, ér shì yīnwèi měi gè dìfang xūyào de「wǒ」bù yíyàng. Dànshì bùguǎn nǎge wǒ, huí dào jiā yǐhòu, wǒ zhǐ xiǎng zuò zuì qīngsōng de nà yí gè.
text_en: At work, I'm a rather quiet person who rarely talks. Colleagues think I'm serious and hard to approach. But after work, when I'm with friends, I'm completely different — I tell jokes, sing, laugh out loud. Once a colleague ran into me and my friends at the mall. He saw me joking around and was astonished: 'You never smile at work!' I didn't know how to respond. Later I thought about it a long time: the work me and the off-work me — which is the real me? Maybe both. In different places we need different selves. This isn't pretending — it's because each place needs a different 'me.' But regardless of which me, when I get home, I just want to be the most relaxed one.

questions.q_en: How do colleagues see 'me' at work?

title: The Paper Airplane
text_zh: 今天下午我在阳台上晒太阳的时候，一架纸飞机飞到了我的阳台上。飞机上面写着：「如果你捡到了这架纸飞机，请在上面写一句话，然后从阳台上扔出去。」我笑了。我想了一会儿，在飞机上写了：「今天的天气很好。」然后把它扔了出去。我看着它飞了一会儿，落在了楼下的花园里。五分钟以后，我看到一个小朋友捡起了它，看了看上面的字，又加了一句话，然后用力扔了出去。我不知道那架纸飞机最后去了哪里。但是我觉得它带着很多人的一句话，飞得比较远了。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān xiàwǔ wǒ zài yángtái shàng shài tàiyáng de shíhou, yí jià zhǐ fēijī fēi dào le wǒ de yángtái shàng. Fēijī shàngmiàn xiězhe:「Rúguǒ nǐ jiǎn dào le zhè jià zhǐ fēijī, qǐng zài shàngmiàn xiě yí jù huà, ránhòu cóng yángtái shàng rēng chūqù.」Wǒ xiào le. Wǒ xiǎng le yíhuìr, zài fēijī shàng xiě le:「Jīntiān de tiānqì hěn hǎo.」Ránhòu bǎ tā rēng le chūqù. Wǒ kànzhe tā fēi le yíhuìr, luò zài le lóu xià de huāyuán lǐ. Wǔ fēnzhōng yǐhòu, wǒ kàn dào yí gè xiǎopéngyou jiǎn qǐ le tā, kàn le kàn shàngmiàn de zì, yòu jiā le yí jù huà, ránhòu yòng lì rēng le chūqù. Wǒ bù zhīdào nà jià zhǐ fēijī zuìhòu qù le nǎlǐ. Dànshì wǒ juéde tā dàizhe hěn duō rén de yí jù huà, fēi de bǐjiào yuǎn le. Yěxǔ zhè jiùshì rén hé rén zhījiān zuì jiǎndān de liánjiē——bù xūyào jiànmiàn, yí jù huà jiù gòu le.
text_en: This afternoon while sunbathing on my balcony, a paper airplane landed on it. It said: 'If you find this airplane, write a sentence on it and throw it from your balcony.' I smiled. I thought for a moment, wrote: 'Today's weather is nice,' and threw it out. I watched it fly a while before landing in the garden below. Five minutes later, I saw a child pick it up, read the writing, add a sentence, and throw it hard. I don't know where that paper airplane ended up. But I feel it carried many people's words and flew rather far.

title: The GPS Voice
text_zh: 我爸爸最近学会了用手机导航。但是他有一个问题——他总是跟导航吵架。导航说「请右转」，他说「右边是墙你让我怎么转！」导航说「前方一百米掉头」，他说「你早点说不行吗！」最好笑的一次，导航说「您已偏离路线，正在重新规划」。爸爸生气地说：「不是我偏离路线，是你的路线有问题！」妈妈在旁边笑得不行，说：「你跟一个机器吵架，它又听不到。」爸爸说：「我知道它听不到，但是说出来我比较舒服。」从那以后，每次坐爸爸的车，我都一边听导航一边听爸爸的评论，觉得这比听相声还有意思。
text_pinyin: Wǒ bàba zuìjìn xuéhuì le yòng shǒujī dǎoháng. Dànshì tā yǒu yí gè wèntí——tā zǒng shì gēn dǎoháng chǎo jià. Dǎoháng shuō「qǐng yòu zhuǎn」, tā shuō「yòubiān shì qiáng nǐ ràng wǒ zěnme zhuǎn!」Dǎoháng shuō「qiánfāng yì bǎi mǐ diào tóu」, tā shuō「nǐ zǎo diǎn shuō bù xíng ma!」Zuì hǎoxiào de yí cì, dǎoháng shuō「nín yǐ piānlí lùxiàn, zhèngzài chóngxīn guīhuà」. Bàba shēngqì de shuō:「Búshì wǒ piānlí lùxiàn, shì nǐ de lùxiàn yǒu wèntí!」Māma zài pángbiān xiào de bùxíng, shuō:「Nǐ gēn yí gè jīqì chǎo jià, tā yòu tīng bú dào.」Bàba shuō:「Wǒ zhīdào tā tīng bú dào, dànshì shuō chūlái wǒ bǐjiào shūfu.」Cóng nà yǐhòu, měi cì zuò bàba de chē, wǒ dōu yìbiān tīng dǎoháng yìbiān tīng bàba de pínglùn, juéde zhè bǐ tīng xiàngsheng hái yǒuyìsi.
text_en: My dad recently learned phone navigation. But he always argues with the GPS. It says 'turn right,' he says 'there's a wall on the right!' It says 'make a U-turn,' he says 'couldn't you have said that earlier!' The funniest time, the GPS said 'you have deviated from the route.' Dad said angrily: 'I didn't deviate — your route has a problem!' Mom was laughing: 'You're arguing with a machine that can't hear you.' Dad said: 'I know, but saying it makes me feel better.' Every ride in Dad's car, I hear the GPS and Dad's commentary at once — more entertaining than comedy shows.

questions.q_en: What feeling does the author want the reader to experience?
questions.explanation: The passage describes ' dad said: 'i know, but saying it makes me feel better, which reveals that feeling.

title: The Last Leaf
text_zh: 秋天快要结束的时候，窗外那棵树只剩下最后一片叶子了。我每天都看着它，想：它什么时候会掉下来？刮风的时候，它动了一下，但是没有掉。下雨的时候，它又动了一下，还是没有掉。我开始佩服这片叶子了。一个星期过去了，它还在那里。我跟妈妈说了这件事。妈妈说：「你不要盯着它看，等你不注意的时候，它自然就掉了。」我觉得妈妈说得比较对。很多事情都是这样——你越在意，它越不走；你不看了，它就自己走了。后来有一天早上，我一看窗外，树上什么都没有了。那片叶子悄悄地走了，没有跟我说再见。
text_pinyin: Qiūtiān kuài yào jiéshù de shíhou, chuāng wài nà kē shù zhǐ shèng xià zuìhòu yí piàn yèzi le. Wǒ měi tiān dōu kànzhe tā, xiǎng: tā shénme shíhou huì diào xiàlái? Guā fēng de shíhou, tā dòng le yíxià, dànshì méiyǒu diào. Xià yǔ de shíhou, tā yòu dòng le yíxià, háishi méiyǒu diào. Wǒ kāishǐ pèifú zhè piàn yèzi le. Yí gè xīngqī guòqu le, tā hái zài nàlǐ. Wǒ gēn māma shuō le zhè jiàn shì. Māma shuō:「Nǐ bú yào dīngzhe tā kàn, děng nǐ bú zhùyì de shíhou, tā zìrán jiù diào le.」Wǒ juéde māma shuō de bǐjiào duì. Hěn duō shìqing dōu shì zhèyàng——nǐ yuè zàiyì, tā yuè bù zǒu; nǐ bú kàn le, tā jiù zìjǐ zǒu le. Hòulái yǒu yì tiān zǎoshang, wǒ yí kàn chuāng wài, shù shàng shénme dōu méiyǒu le. Nà piàn yèzi qiāoqiāo de zǒu le, méiyǒu gēn wǒ shuō zàijiàn.
text_en: Near the end of autumn, the tree outside my window had only one leaf left. I watched it every day, wondering: when will it fall? When the wind blew, it trembled but didn't fall. When it rained, it trembled again but still held on. I began to admire this leaf. A week passed and it was still there. I told Mom about it. She said: 'Stop staring at it. When you're not paying attention, it'll fall on its own.' I think Mom was rather right. Many things work this way — the more you watch, the longer they stay; look away, and they leave on their own. Then one morning I looked out the window and the tree was bare. That leaf had quietly left, without saying goodbye.

title: The Flower Shop at Midnight
text_zh: 我家附近有一家花店，关门比较晚——通常到晚上十一点。老板说：「买花的人不一定是白天来的。有时候晚上才想起来明天是某个重要的日子。」我有一次晚上十点半去买花，是因为第二天是妈妈的生日。花店里只有我一个客人。老板帮我挑了几朵百合花，说：「给妈妈的？」我说是的。她笑着说：「你妈妈一定会开心的。不是因为花，是因为你记得。」她把花包得很漂亮。我走出花店的时候，街上比较安静。手里拿着花走在夜里的感觉比较特别——好像全世界只有我一个人在做一件温暖的事情。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā fùjìn yǒu yì jiā huā diàn, guān mén bǐjiào wǎn——tōngcháng dào wǎnshang shíyī diǎn. Lǎobǎn shuō:「Mǎi huā de rén bù yídìng shì báitiān lái de. Yǒu shíhou wǎnshang cái xiǎng qǐlái míngtiān shì mǒu gè zhòngyào de rìzi.」Wǒ yǒu yí cì wǎnshang shí diǎn bàn qù mǎi huā, shì yīnwèi dì-èr tiān shì māma de shēngrì. Huā diàn lǐ zhǐyǒu wǒ yí gè kèrén. Lǎobǎn bāng wǒ tiāo le jǐ duǒ bǎihé huā, shuō:「Gěi māma de?」Wǒ shuō shì de. Tā xiàozhe shuō:「Nǐ māma yídìng huì kāixīn de. Búshì yīnwèi huā, shì yīnwèi nǐ jìde.」Tā bǎ huā bāo de hěn piàoliang. Wǒ zǒu chū huā diàn de shíhou, jiē shàng bǐjiào ānjìng. Shǒu lǐ názhe huā zǒu zài yè lǐ de gǎnjué bǐjiào tèbié——hǎoxiàng quán shìjiè zhǐyǒu wǒ yí gè rén zài zuò yí jiàn wēnnuǎn de shìqing.
text_en: Near my home there's a flower shop that closes rather late — usually around eleven. The owner says: 'People who buy flowers don't always come during the day. Sometimes at night they remember tomorrow is an important day.' One night at ten-thirty I went to buy flowers because the next day was Mom's birthday. I was the only customer. The owner helped me pick a few lilies and asked: 'For your mom?' I said yes. She smiled: 'Your mom will be happy. Not because of the flowers, but because you remembered.' She wrapped them beautifully. Walking out of the shop, the street was rather quiet. Carrying flowers at night felt rather special — as if I were the only person in the world doing something warm.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的整体情感是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the overall emotional tone of this passage?
questions.explanation: The passage describes as if i were the only person in the world doing something warm, which reveals that feeling.

questions.q_en: Why did the owner say Mom would be happy?

options.text_en: as if only I was doing something warm in the world

questions.explanation: The passage describes as if i were the only person in the world doing something warm, which reveals that feeling.

title: The Skyline from the Roof
text_zh: 搬到新的城市以后，我一直觉得少了什么东西。直到有一天同事带我去了一栋旧楼的天台。从那里可以看到整个城市的天际线——远处的高楼、近处的老房子、中间的河。同事说：「我每次觉得不开心的时候，就来这里坐一坐。看着那么大的城市，就觉得自己的烦恼比较小了。」我站在那里看了很久。风从远处吹过来，比较凉但是比较舒服。我也想找一个这样的地方。
text_pinyin: Bān dào xīn de chéngshì yǐhòu, wǒ yìzhí juéde shǎo le shénme dōngxi. Zhídào yǒu yì tiān tóngshì dài wǒ qù le yí dòng jiù lóu de tiāntái. Cóng nàlǐ kěyǐ kàn dào zhěng gè chéngshì de tiānjìxiàn——yuǎnchù de gāo lóu, jìnchù de lǎo fángzi, zhōngjiān de hé. Tóngshì shuō:「Wǒ měi cì juéde bù kāixīn de shíhou, jiù lái zhèlǐ zuò yí zuò. Kànzhe nàme dà de chéngshì, jiù juéde zìjǐ de fánnǎo bǐjiào xiǎo le.」Wǒ zhàn zài nàlǐ kàn le hěn jiǔ. Fēng cóng yuǎnchù chuī guòlái, bǐjiào liáng dànshì bǐjiào shūfu. Wǒ hūrán míngbai le zìjǐ shǎo de shì shénme——zài zhège chéngshì lǐ, wǒ hái méiyǒu zhǎo dào yí gè shǔyú zìjǐ de gāochù. Měi gè rén dōu xūyào yí gè kěyǐ kàn dào yuǎnfāng de dìfang, búshì wèile kàn dào shénme, ér shì wèile ràng zìjǐ de xīn biàn dà yìdiǎn.
text_en: After moving to a new city, I always felt something was missing. Until one day a colleague took me to the rooftop of an old building. From there you could see the entire city skyline — distant skyscrapers, nearby old houses, a river in between. The colleague said: 'Whenever I'm unhappy, I come sit here. Looking at such a big city, my own troubles feel rather small.' I stood there watching for a long time. The wind came from far away, rather cool but rather comfortable. I want to find a place like that too.

questions.q_zh: 从天台能看到什么？
questions.q_en: What can you see from the rooftop?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「整个城市的天际线」, indicating the entire city skyline.

questions.q_zh: 同事为什么来天台？
questions.q_en: Why does the colleague come to the rooftop?
