options.text_en: when unhappy, sits here and troubles feel smaller

questions.explanation: The colleague says 每次不开心的时候就来这里坐一坐，看着那些灯，觉得烦恼小了很多 — sitting on the rooftop watching city lights makes troubles feel smaller by comparison.

questions.q_zh: 这个选择反映了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this choice reflect?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「个人的喜好和价值观」, indicating personal preferences and values.

title: The Tofu Man
text_zh: 每天早上六点，一个骑三轮车的老人会在我们小区门口停下来，卖豆腐。他的豆腐是自己做的，用的是传统的方法。他说他做了四十年的豆腐。我问他为什么不去超市卖。他说：「超市要做包装，要加防腐剂。我的豆腐今天做，今天吃，不需要那些东西。」他的豆腐比超市的贵一点，但是味道完全不一样。他每天只做两百块豆腐，卖完就回家。有人说他应该多做一点。他摇摇头说：「做得多了，质量就下来了。我宁愿少卖一点，也不想让味道变差。」我觉得比较佩服他——在一个什么都越来越快的世界里，他还在用最慢的方法做豆腐。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn, yí gè qí sānlún chē de lǎorén huì zài wǒmen xiǎoqū ménkǒu tíng xiàlái, mài dòufu. Tā de dòufu shì zìjǐ zuò de, yòng de shì chuántǒng de fāngfǎ. Tā shuō tā zuò le sìshí nián de dòufu. Wǒ wèn tā wèishénme bú qù chāoshì mài. Tā shuō:「Chāoshì yào zuò bāozhuāng, yào jiā fángfǔ jì. Wǒ de dòufu jīntiān zuò, jīntiān chī, bù xūyào nàxiē dōngxi.」Tā de dòufu bǐ chāoshì de guì yìdiǎn, dànshì wèidào wánquán bù yíyàng. Tā měi tiān zhǐ zuò liǎng bǎi kuài dòufu, mài wán jiù huí jiā. Yǒu rén shuō tā yīnggāi duō zuò yìdiǎn. Tā yáoyao tóu shuō:「Zuò de duō le, zhìliàng jiù xiàlái le. Wǒ nìngyuàn shǎo mài yìdiǎn, yě bù xiǎng ràng wèidào biàn chà.」Wǒ juéde bǐjiào pèifú tā——zài yí gè shénme dōu yuè lái yuè kuài de shìjiè lǐ, tā hái zài yòng zuì màn de fāngfǎ zuò dòufu.
text_en: Every morning at six, an old man on a tricycle stops at our complex gate to sell tofu. Handmade, traditional methods. He's been making tofu for forty years. I asked why he doesn't sell in supermarkets. He said: 'Supermarkets need packaging and preservatives. My tofu is made today, eaten today.' His tofu costs more than supermarket tofu, but the taste is completely different. He makes only two hundred pieces daily — sells out and goes home. Someone said he should make more. He shook his head: 'Make more, quality goes down. I'd rather sell less than let the taste get worse.'

questions.q_zh: 老人做了多少年的豆腐？
questions.q_en: How long has the old man made tofu?
questions.explanation: The number 「四十年」 answers directly — forty years of making tofu, a lifetime of practice.

questions.q_zh: 他为什么不去超市卖？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't he sell at supermarkets?

options.pinyin: chāoshì bú yào tā de dòufu

options.text: 超市要做包装和加防腐剂
options.pinyin: chāoshì yào zuò bāozhuāng hé jiā fángfǔ jì

questions.explanation: 他说：'超市要包装，要加防腐剂' — supermarkets require packaging and preservatives, which contradicts his handmade, traditional approach.

questions.q_zh: 他为什么不多做一点？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't he make more?

options.pinyin: zuò duō le zhìliàng huì xiàlái

questions.explanation: 他摇摇头：'做多了，质量就下去了' — making more would reduce quality, so he deliberately limits output to maintain standards.

title: The Park Bench Dedication
text_zh: 我们城市的公园里有一些长椅，上面刻着人的名字和一句话。我以前从来没有注意过。有一天我停下来看了一张——上面写着：「给我的妻子，王美兰。她最喜欢在这里看日落。1942-2019。」旁边还有一张：「给所有在这里休息过的陌生人，希望你们都能找到回家的路。」我走了一圈，发现每张椅子上都有不同的故事。有人是为了纪念家人，有人是为了感谢这个地方。公园管理员说：「这些椅子都是捐赠的。每个人花一点钱，就可以在上面写一句话。」我觉得比较感动。这些椅子上的名字和话，让一个普通的公园变成了一个充满故事的地方。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen chéngshì de gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yìxiē cháng yǐ, shàngmiàn kè zhe rén de míngzi hé yí jù huà. Wǒ yǐqián cónglái méiyǒu zhùyì guò. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ tíng xiàlái kàn le yì zhāng——shàngmiàn xiězhe:「Gěi wǒ de qīzi, Wáng Měilán. Tā zuì xǐhuan zài zhèlǐ kàn rìluò. 1942-2019.」Pángbiān hái yǒu yì zhāng:「Gěi suǒyǒu zài zhèlǐ xiūxi guò de mòshēng rén, xīwàng nǐmen dōu néng zhǎo dào huí jiā de lù.」Wǒ zǒu le yì quān, fāxiàn měi zhāng yǐzi shàng dōu yǒu bùtóng de gùshi. Yǒu rén shì wèile jìniàn jiārén, yǒu rén shì wèile gǎnxiè zhège dìfang. Gōngyuán guǎnlǐyuán shuō:「Zhèxiē yǐzi dōu shì juānzèng de. Měi gè rén huā yìdiǎn qián, jiù kěyǐ zài shàngmiàn xiě yí jù huà.」Wǒ juéde bǐjiào gǎndòng. Zhèxiē yǐzi shàng de míngzi hé huà, ràng yí gè pǔtōng de gōngyuán biàn chéng le yí gè chōngmǎn gùshi de dìfang.
text_en: In our city's park there are benches with names and inscriptions carved on them. I'd never noticed before. One day I stopped to read one: 'For my wife, Wang Meilan. She loved watching sunsets here. 1942-2019.' Beside it another: 'For all the strangers who rested here — may you all find your way home.' I walked around and found each bench had a different story. Some commemorated family, some thanked the place. The park manager said: 'These benches are all donated. For a small contribution, you can write a sentence.' I felt rather moved. The names and words on these benches turned an ordinary park into a place full of stories.

questions.q_zh: 长椅上刻着什么？
questions.q_en: What's carved on the benches?

options.pinyin: rén de míngzi hé yí jù huà

questions.explanation: 公园里有一些椅子，上面刻着人名和一句话 — the benches are carved with people's names and a personal inscription.

questions.q_zh: 王美兰最喜欢在公园里做什么？
questions.q_en: What did Wang Meilan like to do in the park?
questions.explanation: The inscription reads 她最喜欢在这里看日落 — 'she loved watching sunsets here,' directly stating Wang Meilan's favorite park activity.

questions.q_zh: 这些椅子是怎么来的？
questions.q_en: How did the benches get there?
questions.explanation: The passage describes the park manager said: 'these benches are all donated, showing how.

title: The Sleeping City
text_zh: 有一次我加班到凌晨三点才回家。走在路上的时候，城市好像睡着了。商店都关了，路灯还亮着。马路上一辆车都没有。我的脚步声在空空的街上比较响。我忽然发现了一些白天看不到的东西：一楼花店的窗户里面，花在灯光下面安安静静地开着。路边的自动售货机发出嗡嗡的声音，好像在自己说话。远处有一只猫慢慢地走过马路，完全不怕车。我觉得凌晨三点的城市和白天的完全不一样——白天它属于所有人，凌晨三点它只属于还没有回家的人。那天晚上我走得比较慢，因为我想多看一看这个只有少数人见过的城市。
text_pinyin: Yǒu yí cì wǒ jiābān dào língchén sān diǎn cái huí jiā. Zǒu zài lù shàng de shíhou, chéngshì hǎoxiàng shuìzháo le. Shāngdiàn dōu guān le, lùdēng hái liàngzhe. Mǎlù shàng yí liàng chē dōu méiyǒu. Wǒ de jiǎobù shēng zài kōngkōng de jiē shàng bǐjiào xiǎng. Wǒ hūrán fāxiàn le yìxiē báitiān kàn bú dào de dōngxi: yī lóu huā diàn de chuānghu lǐmiàn, huā zài dēngguāng xiàmiàn ān'ānjìngjìng de kāizhe. Lù biān de zìdòng shòuhuò jī fā chū wēngwēng de shēngyīn, hǎoxiàng zài zìjǐ shuō huà. Yuǎnchù yǒu yì zhī māo mànman de zǒu guò mǎlù, wánquán bú pà chē. Wǒ juéde língchén sān diǎn de chéngshì hé báitiān de wánquán bù yíyàng——báitiān tā shǔyú suǒyǒu rén, língchén sān diǎn tā zhǐ shǔyú hái méiyǒu huí jiā de rén. Nà tiān wǎnshang wǒ zǒu de bǐjiào màn, yīnwèi wǒ xiǎng duō kàn yí kàn zhège zhǐyǒu shǎoshù rén jiàn guò de chéngshì.
text_en: Once I worked overtime until three AM. Walking home, the city seemed asleep. Shops were closed, streetlights still on. Not a single car on the road. My footsteps sounded rather loud on the empty streets. I suddenly noticed things invisible by day: inside the ground-floor flower shop window, flowers bloomed quietly under the lights. A roadside vending machine hummed as if talking to itself. In the distance, a cat crossed the road slowly, completely unafraid of cars. I felt the three AM city was completely different from daytime — by day it belongs to everyone; at three AM it belongs only to those who haven't gone home yet. That night I walked rather slowly, wanting to see more of this city that only a few have witnessed.

questions.q_zh: 凌晨三点的马路上有什么？
questions.q_en: What was on the road at 3 AM?
questions.explanation: 路上一辆车都没有 states the scene at 3 AM plainly — not a single car on the road.

questions.q_zh: 「我」发现了什么白天看不到的东西？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' notice that's invisible by day?

options.text: 花在灯光下面安静地开、猫慢慢过马路
options.pinyin: huā zài dēngguāng xiàmiàn ānjìng de kāi, māo mànman guò mǎlù
options.text_en: flowers blooming under lights, cat slowly crossing

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「花在灯光下面安」, indicating flowers blooming under lights, cat slowly crossing.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得凌晨三点的城市属于谁？
questions.q_en: Who does 'I' think the 3 AM city belongs to?

options.pinyin: hái méiyǒu huí jiā de rén

questions.explanation: 「还没有回家的人」 identifies who — those who haven't gone home.

title: The Wrong Seat
text_zh: 今天我去电影院看电影。找到位子坐下以后，旁边已经有一个人了。电影开始了，我发现我的爆米花不见了——我记得我把它放在了右边的扶手上。我往右边一看，旁边那个人正在吃爆米花。我正要说「那是我的」，他先开口了：「你好，这好像是我的位子。」我低头看了看票——没错，我坐错了。我的位子在隔壁那排。我比较不好意思地站起来，拿回我的爆米花（他已经吃了一半了），走到正确的位子上。最好笑的是，他在我走的时候小声说了一句：「你的爆米花味道还不错。」整场电影我都在想这件事。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān wǒ qù diànyǐngyuàn kàn diànyǐng. Zhǎo dào wèizi zuò xià yǐhòu, pángbiān yǐjīng yǒu yí gè rén le. Diànyǐng kāishǐ le, wǒ fāxiàn wǒ de bàomǐhuā bújiàn le——wǒ jìde wǒ bǎ tā fàng zài le yòubiān de fúshǒu shàng. Wǒ wǎng yòubiān yí kàn, pángbiān nàge rén zhèngzài chī bàomǐhuā. Wǒ zhèng yào shuō「nà shì wǒ de」, tā xiān kāi kǒu le:「Nǐ hǎo, zhè hǎoxiàng shì wǒ de wèizi.」Wǒ dī tóu kàn le kàn piào——méi cuò, wǒ zuò cuò le. Wǒ de wèizi zài gébì nà pái. Wǒ bǐjiào bù hǎoyìsi de zhàn qǐlái, ná huí wǒ de bàomǐhuā (tā yǐjīng chī le yíbàn le), zǒu dào zhèngquè de wèizi shàng. Zuì hǎoxiào de shì, tā zài wǒ zǒu de shíhou xiǎo shēng shuō le yí jù:「Nǐ de bàomǐhuā wèidào hái búcuò.」Zhěng chǎng diànyǐng wǒ dōu zài xiǎng zhè jiàn shì.
text_en: Today I went to the movies. After finding my seat and sitting down, someone was already next to me. The movie started and I noticed my popcorn was gone — I remembered putting it on the right armrest. I looked right, and the person next to me was eating popcorn. I was about to say 'that's mine' when he spoke first: 'Hi, this seems to be my seat.' I looked at my ticket — sure enough, I was in the wrong seat. My seat was in the next row. I stood up rather embarrassed, took back my popcorn (he'd already eaten half), and moved to the right seat. The funniest part: as I left, he whispered: 'Your popcorn tastes pretty good.' I thought about this the entire movie.

questions.q_zh: 「我」的爆米花怎么了？
questions.q_en: What happened to 'my' popcorn?

options.pinyin: bèi pángbiān de rén chī le yíbàn
options.text_en: the person next to me ate half

options.pinyin: bèi gōngzuò rényuán ná zǒu le

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「被旁边的人吃了一半」, indicating the person next to me ate half.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么坐错了位子？
questions.q_en: Why was 'I' in the wrong seat?

options.pinyin: piào shàng méiyǒu xiě wèizi hào

options.text: 没有看清楚票上的位子
options.pinyin: méiyǒu kàn qīngchu piào shàng de wèizi
bèi biéren zhǐ le cuò de wèizi
options.text_en: pointed to wrong seat by someone

options.pinyin: zuòwèi hào tài xiǎo kàn bù qīng
options.text_en: seat numbers too small to read

questions.explanation: The key phrase 「没有看清楚票上的位子」 gives the reason — didn't read the ticket carefully.

questions.q_zh: 那个人走的时候说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the person say as 'I' left?

options.text: 你的爆米花味道还不错
options.pinyin: nǐ de bàomǐhuā wèidào hái búcuò

questions.explanation: 走的时候他小声说：'你的爆米花还挺好吃的' — as the narrator left, the stranger whispered that the popcorn (which he'd been eating by mistake) tasted pretty good.

title: Neighborhood Sounds
text_zh: 每天早上六点半，楼下的老人开始打太极拳。他们不说话，只有音乐的声音。七点左右，卖早饭的人来了，他大声说「包子，豆浆！」我听着这些声音，觉得很安心。每个早上都一样，但是我一点也不觉得无聊。
text_pinyin: Měitiān zǎoshang liù diǎn bàn, lóuxià de lǎorén kāishǐ dǎ tàijí quán. Tāmen bù shuōhuà, zhǐyǒu yīnyuè de shēngyīn. Qī diǎn zuǒyòu, mài zǎofàn de rén lái le, tā dàshēng shuō「bāozi, dòujiāng!」Wǒ tīngzhe zhèxiē shēngyīn, juéde hěn ānxīn. Měi ge zǎoshang dōu yíyàng, dànshì wǒ yìdiǎn yě bù juéde wúliáo.
text_en: Every morning at six thirty, the elderly people downstairs begin practicing tai chi. They don't speak — there is only the sound of music. Around seven o'clock, the breakfast seller arrives and calls out loudly, 'Baozi, soy milk!' Listening to these sounds, I feel at ease. Every morning is the same, but I don't find it boring at all.

questions.q_zh: 楼下的老人早上做什么？
questions.q_en: What do the elderly people downstairs do in the morning?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「打太极拳」, indicating Practice tai chi.

questions.q_zh: 作者觉得每天早上的声音怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does the author feel about the morning sounds every day?
questions.explanation: Their behavior — ' listening to these sounds, i feel at ease — shows that attitude.

title: Weekend Morning
text_zh: 周末不用上班，我起得很晚。阳光从窗户照进来，房间变得很暖和。我慢慢地做了一杯咖啡，坐在窗户旁边看外面的树。风吹过来，树叶轻轻地动。没有什么特别的事情，但是这样的早上让我觉得很幸福。
text_pinyin: Zhōumò búyòng shàngbān, wǒ qǐ de hěn wǎn. Yángguāng cóng chuānghù zhào jìnlái, fángjiān biàn de hěn nuǎnhuo. Wǒ mànmàn de zuò le yì bēi kāfēi, zuò zài chuānghù pángbiān kàn wàimiàn de shù. Fēng chuī guòlái, shùyè qīngqīng de dòng. Méiyǒu shénme tèbié de shìqíng, dànshì zhèyàng de zǎoshang ràng wǒ juéde hěn xìngfú.
text_en: On weekends I don't need to go to work, so I get up very late. Sunlight comes in through the window and the room becomes warm. I slowly make a cup of coffee and sit by the window looking at the trees outside. The wind blows over and the leaves move gently. Nothing special is happening, but a morning like this makes me feel happy.

questions.q_zh: 周末的早上，作者做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the author do on the weekend morning?
questions.explanation: 我慢慢地泡了一杯咖啡，坐在窗边看外面的树 — making coffee and sitting by the window looking at trees is exactly what the author did that weekend morning.

questions.q_zh: 那天早上天气怎么样？
questions.q_en: What was the weather like that morning?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「有阳光，暖和」, indicating Sunny and warm.

title: The Tea Shop
text_zh: 我家附近有一个小茶馆，已经开了很多年了。里面的桌子和椅子都是旧的，但是很干净。老板是一个安静的老人，他泡茶泡得很好。每次我去，他都会说「来了啊」，然后给我倒一杯茶。我喜欢坐在角落里，一边喝茶一边看书。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā fùjìn yǒu yí ge xiǎo cháguǎn, yǐjīng kāi le hěn duō nián le. Lǐmiàn de zhuōzi hé yǐzi dōu shì jiù de, dànshì hěn gānjìng. Lǎobǎn shì yí ge ānjìng de lǎorén, tā pào chá pào de hěn hǎo. Měi cì wǒ qù, tā dōu huì shuō「lái le a」, ránhòu gěi wǒ dào yì bēi chá. Wǒ xǐhuān zuò zài jiǎoluò lǐ, yìbiān hē chá yìbiān kàn shū.
text_en: Near my home there is a small tea shop that has been open for many years. The tables and chairs inside are old, but clean. The owner is a quiet old man who makes tea very well. Every time I go, he says 'You're here,' and then pours me a cup of tea. I like to sit in the corner, drinking tea and reading at the same time.

questions.q_zh: 这个选择反映了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this choice reflect?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「个人的喜好和价值观」, indicating personal preferences and values.

questions.q_zh: 为什么作者注意到这些细节？
questions.q_en: Why does the author notice these details?

options.text: 这些细节揭示了更深的意义
options.pinyin: zhèxiē xìjié jiēshì le gèng shēn de yìyì

questions.explanation: The narrator pays attention to the old man's tea-making, his few quiet words, and the worn but clean furniture — these small sensory details reveal a deeper appreciation for places that endure through simplicity.

title: A Handwritten Letter
text_zh: 今天我收到了奶奶寄来的信。她不会用手机，所以每个月都给我写一封信。她的字写得很慢，但是很好看。信里她问我吃得好不好，工作忙不忙。最后她写「天冷了，多穿衣服」。看完信，我的眼睛有一点红了。我决定今天晚上也给她写一封信。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān wǒ shōudào le nǎinai jì lái de xìn. Tā búhuì yòng shǒujī, suǒyǐ měi ge yuè dōu gěi wǒ xiě yì fēng xìn. Tā de zì xiě de hěn màn, dànshì hěn hǎokàn. Xìn lǐ tā wèn wǒ chī de hǎo bù hǎo, gōngzuò máng bù máng. Zuìhòu tā xiě「tiān lěng le, duō chuān yīfu」. Kàn wán xìn, wǒ de yǎnjīng yǒu yìdiǎn hóng le. Wǒ juédìng jīntiān wǎnshang yě gěi tā xiě yì fēng xìn.
text_en: Today I received a letter from my grandmother. She doesn't know how to use a phone, so every month she writes me a letter. Her characters are written slowly, but they are beautiful. In the letter she asks if I'm eating well and if work is busy. At the end she wrote, 'It's gotten cold, wear more clothes.' After reading the letter, my eyes got a little red. I decided to write her a letter tonight too.

questions.q_zh: 奶奶为什么写信？
questions.q_en: Why does grandmother write letters?
questions.explanation: 她不会用手机，所以每个月给我写一封信 — Grandmother cannot use a phone, so monthly letters are her only way to communicate.

questions.q_zh: 看完信以后，作者想做什么？
questions.q_en: After reading the letter, what does the author want to do?

options.pinyin: gěi nǎinai xiě yì fēng xìn

questions.explanation: After reading about Grandmother's hand-written characters, her care in each letter, and her wish to see the narrator, the natural impulse is to write back in the same personal, tangible way.

title: The Old Bookstore
text_zh: 这条街上有一家很小的书店。门口放着几个旧书架，上面的书都很便宜。我常常在这里看很长时间的书，老板从来不说什么。有时候我会买一本旧书回家。书里面有别人写的笔记，我觉得很有意思。好像通过这些笔记，我认识了一个不知道名字的朋友。
text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo jiē shàng yǒu yì jiā hěn xiǎo de shūdiàn. Ménkǒu fàngzhe jǐ ge jiù shūjià, shàngmiàn de shū dōu hěn piányi. Wǒ chángcháng zài zhèlǐ kàn hěn cháng shíjiān de shū, lǎobǎn cónglái bù shuō shénme. Yǒu shíhòu wǒ huì mǎi yì běn jiù shū huí jiā. Shū lǐmiàn yǒu biérén xiě de bǐjì, wǒ juéde hěn yǒu yìsi. Hǎoxiàng tōngguò zhèxiē bǐjì, wǒ rènshi le yí ge bù zhīdào míngzì de péngyǒu.
text_en: On this street there is a small bookstore. At the entrance there are a few old bookshelves, and the books on them are all very cheap. I often spend a long time reading here, and the owner never says anything. Sometimes I buy an old book to take home. Inside the books there are notes written by other people, which I find interesting. It's as if through these notes, I've gotten to know a friend whose name I don't know.

questions.q_zh: 这种坚持说明这个人怎么样？
questions.q_en: What does this persistence tell us about the person?

options.text_en: has no ideas of their own

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「有毅力和决心」, indicating determined and persistent.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么觉得旧书有意思？
questions.q_en: Why does the author find old books interesting?

options.text_en: There are other people's notes inside

questions.explanation: 书里面有别人写的笔记，我觉得很有意思 — other people's handwritten notes inside the old books create a sense of connection and discovery that makes them more interesting than new copies.

title: A Bicycle Ride
text_zh: 下班以后，我没有坐地铁，而是骑自行车回家。路上经过一条河，河边的花开了，很好看。我停下来，站在桥上看了一会儿。风从河上吹过来，很凉快。有几个小孩在河边跑来跑去。我觉得骑车回家虽然慢，但是能看到很多平时看不到的东西。
text_pinyin: Xiàbān yǐhòu, wǒ méiyǒu zuò dìtiě, érshì qí zìxíngchē huí jiā. Lùshang jīngguò yì tiáo hé, hé biān de huā kāi le, hěn hǎokàn. Wǒ tíng xiàlái, zhàn zài qiáo shàng kàn le yíhuìr. Fēng cóng hé shàng chuī guòlái, hěn liángkuài. Yǒu jǐ ge xiǎohái zài hé biān pǎo lái pǎo qù. Wǒ juéde qí chē huí jiā suīrán màn, dànshì néng kàndào hěn duō píngshí kàn búdào de dōngxi.
text_en: After work, I didn't take the subway but rode a bicycle home instead. On the way I passed a river, and the flowers along the riverbank were blooming beautifully. I stopped and stood on the bridge looking for a while. The wind blew over from the river, very cool. A few children were running back and forth by the river. I think cycling home is slower, but you can see many things you normally wouldn't notice.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么在桥上停下来？
questions.q_en: Why did the author stop on the bridge?

options.text_en: To look at the flowers by the river

questions.explanation: The key phrase 「看河边的花」 gives the reason — To look at the flowers by the river.

questions.q_zh: 自然环境对故事有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the natural setting affect the story?
questions.explanation: The passage shows this through: after work, i didn't take the subway but rode a bicycle home instead.

title: Rain on the Window
text_zh: 今天下了一整天的雨。我坐在家里，看雨水从窗户上慢慢地流下来。外面的声音变得很小，只能听到雨的声音。我泡了一壶茶，拿了一条毯子，坐在沙发上。什么也不做，就这样听着雨声。有时候，什么都不做也是一种休息。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān xià le yì zhěng tiān de yǔ. Wǒ zuò zài jiā lǐ, kàn yǔshuǐ cóng chuānghù shàng mànmàn de liú xiàlái. Wàimiàn de shēngyīn biàn de hěn xiǎo, zhǐ néng tīngdào yǔ de shēngyīn. Wǒ pào le yì hú chá, ná le yì tiáo tǎnzi, zuò zài shāfā shàng. Shénme yě bú zuò, jiù zhèyàng tīngzhe yǔshēng. Yǒu shíhòu, shénme dōu bú zuò yě shì yì zhǒng xiūxi.
text_en: Today it rained all day. I sat at home watching the rainwater slowly flow down the window. The sounds outside became quiet — you could only hear the sound of rain. I made a pot of tea, grabbed a blanket, and sat on the sofa. I did nothing at all, just listened to the rain like that. Sometimes, doing nothing at all is also a kind of rest.

questions.q_zh: 今天的天气怎么样？
questions.q_en: What was the weather like today?

options.pinyin: xià le yì zhěng tiān de yǔ

options.text_en: It rained for a little while

questions.explanation: 今天下了一整天的雨 states the weather directly — it rained the entire day.

questions.q_zh: 自然环境对故事有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the natural setting affect the story?
questions.explanation: The passage describes today it rained all day, showing how.

title: A Shared Meal
text_zh: 今天晚上，我请邻居来家里吃饭。我做了三个菜：一个西红柿炒鸡蛋，一个青菜，一个鱼汤。菜不多，但是都是自己做的。邻居带了一些水果来。我们一边吃一边聊天，说了很多有意思的事情。吃完饭以后，我们一起洗碗。虽然是很简单的晚饭，但是大家都很开心。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān wǎnshang, wǒ qǐng línjū lái jiā lǐ chīfàn. Wǒ zuò le sān ge cài: yí ge xīhóngshì chǎo jīdàn, yí ge qīngcài, yí ge yú tāng. Cài bù duō, dànshì dōu shì zìjǐ zuò de. Línjū dài le yìxiē shuǐguǒ lái. Wǒmen yìbiān chī yìbiān liáotiān, shuō le hěn duō yǒu yìsi de shìqíng. Chī wán fàn yǐhòu, wǒmen yìqǐ xǐ wǎn. Suīrán shì hěn jiǎndān de wǎnfàn, dànshì dàjiā dōu hěn kāixīn.
text_en: Tonight I invited my neighbor to eat at my home. I made three dishes: scrambled eggs with tomatoes, green vegetables, and fish soup. Not many dishes, but all homemade. My neighbor brought some fruit. We chatted while eating, talking about many interesting things. After dinner, we washed the dishes together. Although it was a simple dinner, everyone was happy.

questions.q_zh: 作者想让读者感受到什么？
questions.q_en: What feeling does the author want the reader to experience?
questions.explanation: Their behavior — tonight i invited my neighbor to eat at my home — shows that attitude.

questions.q_zh: 邻居带了什么来？
questions.q_en: What did the neighbor bring?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「一些水果」, indicating Some fruit.

title: An Evening Walk
text_zh: 吃完晚饭，我和妈妈出去散步。我们走在小区旁边的路上，路灯已经亮了。空气里有花的香味。妈妈走得不快，我也放慢了脚步。她跟我说了一些小时候的事情，都是我没听过的。走了半个小时，我们慢慢走回家。我觉得和家人一起散步的时间，比看手机有意思多了。
text_pinyin: Chī wán wǎnfàn, wǒ hé māma chūqù sànbù. Wǒmen zǒu zài xiǎoqū pángbiān de lù shàng, lùdēng yǐjīng liàng le. Kōngqì lǐ yǒu huā de xiāngwèi. Māma zǒu de bú kuài, wǒ yě fàngmàn le jiǎobù. Tā gēn wǒ shuō le yìxiē xiǎoshíhòu de shìqíng, dōu shì wǒ méi tīng guò de. Zǒu le bàn ge xiǎoshí, wǒmen mànmàn zǒu huí jiā. Wǒ juéde hé jiārén yìqǐ sànbù de shíjiān, bǐ kàn shǒujī yǒu yìsi duō le.
text_en: After dinner, Mom and I went out for a walk. We walked on the road next to the neighborhood, and the streetlights were already on. There was the fragrance of flowers in the air. Mom didn't walk fast, so I also slowed my steps. She told me some stories from when she was young — things I'd never heard before. After walking for half an hour, we slowly walked home. I think the time spent walking with family is much more interesting than looking at a phone.

questions.q_zh: 妈妈跟作者说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did Mom talk about with the author?
questions.explanation: 她给我讲了一些她年轻时候的故事——是我以前从来没有听过的 — Mom shared stories from her youth that the narrator had never heard before.

questions.q_zh: 这些人之间最重要的是什么？
questions.q_en: What matters most between these people?
questions.explanation: Mom shares childhood stories, walks slowly so they can talk, and the narrator realizes 这样的时间很少 — their unhurried connection and willingness to share show mutual understanding and support.

title: Garden Moments
text_zh: 我的阳台上有几盆花。每天早上，我都会给它们浇水。有一盆花上个星期还没有开，今天早上我发现它开了一朵小花，是黄色的。我高兴得拍了一张照片发给朋友看。朋友说「你现在像一个老人一样了」。我笑了，觉得这样也挺好的。慢慢地看着一朵花开，是一件很快乐的事。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de yángtái shàng yǒu jǐ pén huā. Měitiān zǎoshang, wǒ dōu huì gěi tāmen jiāo shuǐ. Yǒu yì pén huā shàng ge xīngqī hái méiyǒu kāi, jīntiān zǎoshang wǒ fāxiàn tā kāi le yì duǒ xiǎo huā, shì huángsè de. Wǒ gāoxìng de pāi le yì zhāng zhàopiàn fā gěi péngyǒu kàn. Péngyǒu shuō「nǐ xiànzài xiàng yí ge lǎorén yíyàng le」. Wǒ xiào le, juéde zhèyàng yě tǐng hǎo de. Mànmàn de kànzhe yì duǒ huā kāi, shì yí jiàn hěn kuàilè de shì.
text_en: I have a few pots of flowers on my balcony. Every morning, I water them. One pot hadn't bloomed last week, but this morning I noticed it had opened a small flower — it was yellow. I was so happy that I took a photo and sent it to a friend. My friend said, 'You're like an old person now.' I laughed and thought that's actually pretty nice. Slowly watching a flower bloom is a very joyful thing.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么因为一朵小花那么高兴？
questions.q_en: Why is the narrator so happy about one small flower?

options.text: 因为他享受慢慢等待、看到变化的过程
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā xiǎngshòu mànmàn děngdài, kàn dào biànhuà de guòchéng
options.text_en: because he enjoys the slow process of waiting and seeing change

options.text: 因为这种花很贵，很难买到
options.pinyin: yīnwèi zhè zhǒng huā hěn guì, hěn nán mǎi dào
options.text_en: because this type of flower is expensive and hard to buy

options.text: 因为他想把照片发到网上变有名
options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā xiǎng bǎ zhàopiàn fā dào wǎng shàng biàn yǒumíng
options.text_en: because he wants to post the photo online and become famous

options.text: 因为朋友说花好看，他感到骄傲
options.pinyin: yīnwèi péngyǒu shuō huā hǎokàn, tā gǎndào jiāo'ào
options.text_en: because his friend said the flower was pretty and he felt proud

questions.explanation: 慢慢地看着一朵花开，是一件很快乐的事 — the joy comes from the slow process of watching something grow and change, not from the flower's size or beauty.

questions.q_zh: 朋友说了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the friend say?

options.pinyin: nǐ xiàng yí ge lǎorén yíyàng le
options.text_en: You're like an old person now
I also want to grow flowers

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「你像一个老人一样了」, indicating You're like an old person now.

title: A Small Kindness
text_zh: 今天下雨，我在公共汽车站等车。旁边站着一个小女孩，她没有带伞，衣服已经湿了。我把伞给了她，说「你用吧，我快到家了」。其实我家还很远。小女孩笑着说「谢谢姐姐」。车来了，我跑上车，虽然身上湿了，但是心里很暖。有时候，帮一个人不需要做很大的事。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān xiàyǔ, wǒ zài gōnggòng qìchē zhàn děng chē. Pángbiān zhànzhe yí ge xiǎo nǚhái, tā méiyǒu dài sǎn, yīfu yǐjīng shī le. Wǒ bǎ sǎn gěi le tā, shuō「nǐ yòng ba, wǒ kuài dào jiā le」. Qíshí wǒ jiā hái hěn yuǎn. Xiǎo nǚhái xiàozhe shuō「xièxie jiějie」. Chē lái le, wǒ pǎo shàng chē, suīrán shēnshang shī le, dànshì xīnlǐ hěn nuǎn. Yǒu shíhòu, bāng yí ge rén bù xūyào zuò hěn dà de shì.
text_en: Today it was raining and I was waiting for the bus at the stop. A little girl was standing next to me — she didn't have an umbrella and her clothes were already wet. I gave her my umbrella and said, 'You use it, I'm almost home.' Actually my home was still very far. The little girl smiled and said, 'Thank you, big sister.' The bus came and I ran on. Although my clothes were wet, my heart felt warm. Sometimes helping someone doesn't require doing anything big.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么把伞给小女孩？
questions.q_en: Why did the author give her umbrella to the little girl?

options.text: 小女孩没有伞，衣服湿了
options.pinyin: xiǎo nǚhái méiyǒu sǎn, yīfu shī le
options.text_en: The girl had no umbrella and was wet

questions.explanation: 她没有伞，衣服已经湿了 — the girl had no umbrella and was already soaked, prompting the narrator to give hers away.

questions.q_zh: 作者说「我快到家了」是真的吗？
questions.q_en: Was the author telling the truth when she said 'I'm almost home'?

options.pinyin: búshì, tā jiā hái hěn yuǎn
options.text_en: No, her home was still far away

questions.explanation: 其实我家还很远 reveals the lie — her home was actually still far away, meaning she gave up her umbrella knowing she'd get wet too.

title: Seasonal Changes
text_zh: 我办公室窗外有一棵大树。春天的时候，树上长出了绿色的小叶子。夏天，叶子变得又大又多，像一把大伞。秋天来了，叶子慢慢变成红色和黄色，特别好看。冬天的时候，叶子都掉了，只有树枝。同事说这棵树不好看了，但是我觉得每个季节的树都有不一样的美。
text_pinyin: Wǒ bàngōngshì chuāngwài yǒu yì kē dà shù. Chūntiān de shíhòu, shù shàng zhǎng chū le lǜsè de xiǎo yèzi. Xiàtiān, yèzi biàn de yòu dà yòu duō, xiàng yì bǎ dà sǎn. Qiūtiān lái le, yèzi mànmàn biàn chéng hóngsè hé huángsè, tèbié hǎokàn. Dōngtiān de shíhòu, yèzi dōu diào le, zhǐyǒu shùzhī. Tóngshì shuō zhè kē shù bù hǎokàn le, dànshì wǒ juéde měi ge jìjié de shù dōu yǒu bù yíyàng de měi.
text_en: Outside my office window there is a big tree. In spring, small green leaves grow on it. In summer, the leaves become big and plentiful, like a large umbrella. When autumn comes, the leaves slowly turn red and yellow — especially beautiful. In winter, all the leaves have fallen, leaving only branches. My colleague says the tree doesn't look nice anymore, but I think the tree has a different kind of beauty in each season.

questions.q_zh: 天气在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the weather play in this passage?
questions.explanation: The tree mirrors the seasons — green in spring, full in summer, golden in autumn, bare in winter — and the narrator maps their own emotional states onto these changes, making the weather a reflection of mood.

questions.q_zh: 天气在文中起什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the weather play in this passage?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「反映人物的心情」, indicating reflects the character's mood.

title: A Familiar Route
text_zh: 我每天走同一条路去上班。路过早饭店的时候，老板会对我笑一下。经过学校门口，能听到孩子们读书的声音。到了公园旁边，总是能看到几个老人在锻炼。这条路我走了三年了，路上的人和店我都很熟悉。有人问我为什么不换一条路走，我说这条路上的每一个小变化，都让我觉得生活在慢慢变好。
text_pinyin: Wǒ měitiān zǒu tóng yì tiáo lù qù shàngbān. Lùguò zǎofàndiàn de shíhòu, lǎobǎn huì duì wǒ xiào yíxià. Jīngguò xuéxiào ménkǒu, néng tīngdào háizimen dúshū de shēngyīn. Dào le gōngyuán pángbiān, zǒngshì néng kàndào jǐ ge lǎorén zài duànliàn. Zhè tiáo lù wǒ zǒu le sān nián le, lùshang de rén hé diàn wǒ dōu hěn shúxī. Yǒu rén wèn wǒ wèishénme bú huàn yì tiáo lù zǒu, wǒ shuō zhè tiáo lù shàng de měi yí ge xiǎo biànhuà, dōu ràng wǒ juéde shēnghuó zài mànmàn biàn hǎo.
text_en: Every day I walk the same road to work. When I pass the breakfast shop, the owner smiles at me. Passing the school entrance, I can hear the sound of children reading aloud. By the park, I can always see a few elderly people exercising. I've walked this road for three years, and I'm familiar with all the people and shops along the way. Someone asked me why I don't take a different route. I said that every small change on this road makes me feel that life is slowly getting better.

questions.q_zh: 作者走这条路多长时间了？
questions.q_en: How long has the author been walking this route?
questions.explanation: 我走了三年这条路，路上的人和店我都很熟悉了 states the duration — three years of walking the same route to work.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么不换一条路走？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the author take a different route?

options.text_en: Because there are no other routes

options.pinyin: yīnwèi zhè tiáo lù zuì duǎn

options.text: 因为路上的小变化让他觉得生活变好
options.pinyin: yīnwèi lùshang de xiǎo biànhuà ràng tā juéde shēnghuó biàn hǎo
options.text_en: Because the small changes make him feel life is getting better

questions.explanation: 这条路上的每一个小变化，都让我觉得生活在慢慢变好 — each small change along the route (new flowers, a repainted shop) makes the narrator feel life is gradually improving, so a different route would lack that continuity.

title: The Bench at the Bus Stop
text_zh: 我家附近的公交车站有一把旧长椅。每天早上，一个老爷爷坐在那里看报纸。他不是在等公交车，因为车来了他也不上去。有一次下雨，我在旁边等车，他把伞给了我一半。我问他为什么每天来这里坐。他说：「退休以后，在家里太安静了。坐在这里，看人来人往，觉得自己还在生活里面。」我听了觉得有点难过，但是他笑着说：「你别担心。我很喜欢这样。每天看到不同的人，觉得这个城市还是热闹的。」后来我每天路过的时候，都会跟他打招呼。他也总是对我笑一笑。一把长椅、一份报纸，就是他跟这个世界保持联系的方式。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā fùjìn de gōngjiāo chē zhàn yǒu yì bǎ jiù cháng yǐ. Měi tiān zǎoshang, yí gè lǎo yéye zuò zài nàlǐ kàn bàozhǐ. Tā búshì zài děng gōngjiāo chē, yīnwèi chē lái le tā yě bú shàng qù. Yǒu yí cì xià yǔ, wǒ zài pángbiān děng chē, tā bǎ sǎn gěi le wǒ yí bàn. Wǒ wèn tā wèishénme měi tiān lái zhèlǐ zuò. Tā shuō:「Tuìxiū yǐhòu, zài jiā lǐ tài ānjìng le. Zuò zài zhèlǐ, kàn rén lái rén wǎng, juéde zìjǐ hái zài shēnghuó lǐmiàn.」Wǒ tīng le juéde yǒudiǎn nánguò, dànshì tā xiàozhe shuō:「Nǐ bié dānxīn. Wǒ hěn xǐhuan zhèyàng. Měi tiān kàn dào bùtóng de rén, juéde zhège chéngshì háishi rènao de.」Hòulái wǒ měi tiān lù guò de shíhou, dōu huì gēn tā dǎ zhāohu. Tā yě zǒngshì duì wǒ xiào yí xiào. Yì bǎ cháng yǐ, yí fèn bàozhǐ, jiùshì tā gēn zhège shìjiè bǎochí liánxì de fāngshì.
text_en: There's an old bench at the bus stop near my home. Every morning, an elderly man sits there reading the newspaper. He's not waiting for the bus — when one comes, he doesn't get on. Once when it rained, I was waiting nearby and he shared his umbrella with me. I asked why he comes here every day. He said: 'After retiring, home is too quiet. Sitting here, watching people come and go, I feel I'm still part of life.' I felt a little sad, but he smiled: 'Don't worry. I enjoy this. Seeing different people every day, the city still feels lively.' After that, I'd greet him whenever I passed by. He'd always smile back. One bench, one newspaper — that's how he stays connected to the world.

questions.q_zh: 老爷爷为什么每天坐在公交车站？
questions.q_en: Why does the old man sit at the bus stop every day?

options.text: 看人来人往，觉得自己还在生活里面
options.pinyin: kàn rén lái rén wǎng, juéde zìjǐ hái zài shēnghuó lǐmiàn
options.text_en: watching people come and go, feeling part of life

questions.explanation: 坐在这里，看着人来人往，觉得自己还是生活的一部分 — watching people come and go makes him feel he's still part of life, which is why he sits there daily without ever boarding a bus.

questions.q_zh: 下雨的时候老爷爷做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the old man do when it rained?

options.pinyin: bǎ sǎn gěi le wǒ yí bàn

questions.explanation: 「把伞给了「我」一半」 gives the time — shared his umbrella.

questions.q_zh: 老爷爷用什么方式跟世界保持联系？
questions.q_en: How does the old man stay connected to the world?

options.pinyin: yì bǎ cháng yǐ, yí fèn bàozhǐ

questions.explanation: The passage describes one bench, one newspaper — that's how he stays connected to the world, showing how.

title: The Name I Chose
text_zh: 我的中文名字是老师帮我取的，叫「文静」。但是我一点也不文静——我说话很大声，笑的时候比谁都响。有一段时间，我觉得这个名字不适合我，想换一个。我跟中国朋友说，他们问我想叫什么。我想了很久，发现自己想不出更好的名字。后来有一天，一个中国同事跟我说：「你知道吗？我觉得你越来越像你的名字了。不是安静那种文静，是你自己的那种——有力量的安静。你不说话的时候，眼睛里有很多想法。」我听了以后，突然觉得这个名字比我以前理解的更好。名字不一定要跟你一样，它也可以是你慢慢变成的样子。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de Zhōngwén míngzi shì lǎoshī bāng wǒ qǔ de, jiào「Wénjìng」. Dànshì wǒ yìdiǎn yě bú wénjìng——wǒ shuō huà hěn dà shēng, xiào de shíhou bǐ shéi dōu xiǎng. Yǒu yí duàn shíjiān, wǒ juéde zhège míngzi bú shìhé wǒ, xiǎng huàn yí gè. Wǒ gēn Zhōngguó péngyou shuō, tāmen wèn wǒ xiǎng jiào shénme. Wǒ xiǎng le hěn jiǔ, fāxiàn zìjǐ xiǎng bù chū gèng hǎo de míngzi. Hòulái yǒu yì tiān, yí gè Zhōngguó tóngshì gēn wǒ shuō:「Nǐ zhīdào ma? Wǒ juéde nǐ yuè lái yuè xiàng nǐ de míngzi le. Búshì ānjìng nà zhǒng wénjìng, shì nǐ zìjǐ de nà zhǒng——yǒu lìliang de ānjìng. Nǐ bù shuō huà de shíhou, yǎnjing lǐ yǒu hěn duō xiǎngfǎ.」Wǒ tīng le yǐhòu, tūrán juéde zhège míngzi bǐ wǒ yǐqián lǐjiě de gèng hǎo. Míngzi bù yídìng yào gēn nǐ yíyàng, tā yě kěyǐ shì nǐ mànman biàn chéng de yàngzi.
text_en: My Chinese name was chosen by my teacher: 'Wenjing' — quiet and gentle. But I'm not quiet at all — I speak loudly, and my laugh is louder than anyone's. For a while I felt the name didn't suit me and wanted to change it. After thinking a long time, I couldn't come up with anything better. Then a Chinese colleague said: 'You're becoming more like your name. Not the quiet type of wenjing, but your own kind — a quiet with strength. When you're not speaking, your eyes are full of thoughts.' A name doesn't have to match who you are — it can be who you're slowly becoming.

questions.q_zh: 「我」为什么想换名字？
questions.q_en: Why did 'I' want to change the name?
questions.explanation: 有一段时间我觉得这个名字不适合我，想改 — the narrator felt 'Wenjing' (quiet and gentle) was a mismatch with her loud, outgoing personality.

questions.q_zh: 这个选择反映了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this choice reflect?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「个人的喜好和价值观」, indicating personal preferences and values.

questions.q_zh: 最后「我」怎么理解自己的名字？
questions.q_en: How did 'I' finally understand the name?

options.text: 名字可以是你慢慢变成的样子
options.pinyin: míngzi kěyǐ shì nǐ mànman biàn chéng de yàngzi
options.text_en: a name can be who you slowly become

options.pinyin: lǎoshī qǔ de míngzi dōu bù hǎo

questions.explanation: The passage describes ' a name doesn't have to match who you are — it can be who you're slowly becoming, showing how.

title: The Slow Train
text_zh: 坐高铁从上海到南京只要一个多小时，但是我这次选了慢车——四个小时。朋友觉得我很奇怪，问我为什么不坐高铁。我说：「我不赶时间。坐慢车可以看到更多的风景。」慢车经过很多小站，每个站都停两三分钟。我看到了水田、小村庄、在田里工作的农民。有一个站叫什么名字我忘了，但是站台上有一棵很大的树，树下面坐着几个老人在聊天。那个画面让我觉得时间变慢了。高铁太快了，快到你看不清窗外的东西。但是慢车让你一边坐车一边慢慢地看这个世界。到了南京以后，朋友说：「你迟到了两个小时。」我说：「但是我多看了两个小时的中国。」
text_pinyin: Zuò gāotiě cóng Shànghǎi dào Nánjīng zhǐ yào yí gè duō xiǎoshí, dànshì wǒ zhè cì xuǎn le màn chē——sì gè xiǎoshí. Péngyou juéde wǒ hěn qíguài, wèn wǒ wèishénme bú zuò gāotiě. Wǒ shuō:「Wǒ bù gǎn shíjiān. Zuò màn chē kěyǐ kàn dào gèng duō de fēngjǐng.」Màn chē jīngguò hěn duō xiǎo zhàn, měi gè zhàn dōu tíng liǎng sān fēnzhōng. Wǒ kàn dào le shuǐtián, xiǎo cūnzhuāng, zài tián lǐ gōngzuò de nóngmín. Yǒu yí gè zhàn jiào shénme míngzi wǒ wàng le, dànshì zhàntái shàng yǒu yì kē hěn dà de shù, shù xiàmiàn zuòzhe jǐ gè lǎorén zài liáo tiān. Nàge huàmiàn ràng wǒ juéde shíjiān biàn màn le. Gāotiě tài kuài le, kuài dào nǐ kàn bù qīng chuāng wài de dōngxi. Dànshì màn chē ràng nǐ yìbiān zuò chē yìbiān mànman de kàn zhège shìjiè. Dào le Nánjīng yǐhòu, péngyou shuō:「Nǐ chídào le liǎng gè xiǎoshí.」Wǒ shuō:「Dànshì wǒ duō kàn le liǎng gè xiǎoshí de Zhōngguó.」
text_en: The bullet train from Shanghai to Nanjing takes just over an hour, but I chose the slow train — four hours. My friend thought I was strange. I said: 'I'm not in a hurry. The slow train lets you see more.' It stops at many small stations. I saw rice paddies, small villages, farmers working in fields. One station had a big tree on the platform with elderly people chatting beneath it. That image made time feel slower. The bullet train is too fast to see what's outside clearly. The slow train lets you watch the world. My friend said: 'You're two hours late.' I said: 'But I saw two more hours of China.'

questions.q_zh: 慢车从上海到南京要多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long does the slow train take from Shanghai to Nanjing?
questions.explanation: The number 「四个小时」 answers directly — four hours on the slow train from Shanghai to Nanjing.

questions.q_zh: 「我」在慢车上看到了什么？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' see from the slow train?
questions.explanation: 我看到了稻田、小村庄、在地里干活的农民 — from the slow train window, the narrator saw rice paddies, small villages, and farmers working in the fields.

questions.q_zh: 「我」对迟到两个小时怎么说的？
questions.q_en: What did 'I' say about being two hours late?

options.text_en: will take bullet train next time

options.text: 多看了两个小时的中国
options.pinyin: duō kàn le liǎng gè xiǎoshí de Zhōngguó
options.text_en: saw two more hours of China

questions.explanation: 我说：'但是我多看了两个小时的中国' — rather than lamenting the delay, the narrator reframed it as two extra hours of seeing China's landscape.

title: The Hostel Kitchen
text_zh: 在成都旅行的时候，我住在一家青年旅馆。房间很小，但是厨房很大，是大家一起用的。每天晚上，厨房里都很热闹。有人做饭，有人洗碗，有人坐在旁边喝啤酒。我刚到的那天晚上，一个韩国女孩正在做泡菜汤。她看我在旁边看，就问我要不要尝一口。我说谢谢。那个汤非常好喝——又辣又暖和。后来我们一边吃一边聊天。她告诉我，她已经一个人旅行了三个月。我问她不觉得孤单吗。她说：「刚开始的时候觉得。但是每到一个地方，厨房里总能遇到新朋友。你看，今天就遇到你了。」那天晚上，厨房里有五个国家的人，用不太标准的中文聊天。那是我在成都最开心的一个晚上。
text_pinyin: Zài Chéngdū lǚxíng de shíhou, wǒ zhù zài yì jiā qīngnián lǚguǎn. Fángjiān hěn xiǎo, dànshì chúfáng hěn dà, shì dàjiā yìqǐ yòng de. Měi tiān wǎnshang, chúfáng lǐ dōu hěn rènao. Yǒu rén zuò fàn, yǒu rén xǐ wǎn, yǒu rén zuò zài pángbiān hē píjiǔ. Wǒ gāng dào de nà tiān wǎnshang, yí gè Hánguó nǚhái zhèngzài zuò pàocài tāng. Tā kàn wǒ zài pángbiān kàn, jiù wèn wǒ yào bu yào cháng yì kǒu. Wǒ shuō xièxie. Nàge tāng fēicháng hǎo hē——yòu là yòu nuǎnhuo. Hòulái wǒmen yìbiān chī yìbiān liáo tiān. Tā gàosu wǒ, tā yǐjīng yí gè rén lǚxíng le sān gè yuè. Wǒ wèn tā bù juéde gūdān ma. Tā shuō:「Gāng kāishǐ de shíhou juéde. Dànshì měi dào yí gè dìfang, chúfáng lǐ zǒng néng yù dào xīn péngyou. Nǐ kàn, jīntiān jiù yù dào nǐ le.」Nà tiān wǎnshang, chúfáng lǐ yǒu wǔ gè guójiā de rén, yòng bú tài biāozhǔn de Zhōngwén liáo tiān. Nà shì wǒ zài Chéngdū zuì kāixīn de yí gè wǎnshang.
text_en: In Chengdu, I stayed at a youth hostel. The room was tiny, but the shared kitchen was big. Every evening it was lively — people cooking, washing dishes, drinking beer. The night I arrived, a Korean girl was making kimchi soup. She noticed me watching and offered a taste. The soup was wonderful — spicy and warm. We ended up eating together. She'd been traveling alone for three months. I asked if she felt lonely. She said: 'At first, yes. But every place I go, I meet new friends in the kitchen. Today I met you.' That night, people from five countries chatted in the kitchen. My happiest evening in Chengdu.

questions.q_zh: 青年旅馆的厨房有什么特点？
questions.q_en: What was special about the hostel kitchen?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「大家一起用，很热闹」, indicating shared and lively.

questions.q_zh: 韩国女孩旅行了多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long had the Korean girl been traveling?
questions.explanation: 她一个人已经旅行了三个月 states the duration — she had been traveling solo for three months.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么情感？
questions.q_en: What emotion does this passage hint at?
questions.explanation: The passage describes in chengdu, i stayed at a youth hostel, which reveals that feeling.

title: The Grandmother's Dumplings
text_zh: 我的中国朋友请我去她奶奶家吃饺子。奶奶住在老城区的一个小房子里，客厅的桌子上已经放好了面粉和肉馅。奶奶一边包饺子一边跟我说话。她的普通话不太标准，但是我能听懂大部分。她问我从哪里来，家里有几个人，喜不喜欢吃辣的。她包饺子特别快——我包一个的时间，她已经包了五个。她笑着说：「包了四十年了，当然快。」吃饭的时候，奶奶一直往我碗里夹饺子。我说够了够了，她说：「年轻人要多吃。」那天的饺子是我吃过的最好吃的——不是因为味道比餐厅的好，而是因为每一个饺子里面都有奶奶的心意。回去的路上，朋友说：「奶奶说你像她以前的邻居家的孩子。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ de Zhōngguó péngyou qǐng wǒ qù tā nǎinai jiā chī jiǎozi. Nǎinai zhù zài lǎo chéngqū de yí gè xiǎo fángzi lǐ, kètīng de zhuōzi shàng yǐjīng fàng hǎo le miànfěn hé ròu xiàn. Nǎinai yìbiān bāo jiǎozi yìbiān gēn wǒ shuō huà. Tā de pǔtōnghuà bú tài biāozhǔn, dànshì wǒ néng tīng dǒng dàbùfen. Tā wèn wǒ cóng nǎlǐ lái, jiā lǐ yǒu jǐ gè rén, xǐ bù xǐhuan chī là de. Tā bāo jiǎozi tèbié kuài——wǒ bāo yí gè de shíjiān, tā yǐjīng bāo le wǔ gè. Tā xiàozhe shuō:「Bāo le sìshí nián le, dāngrán kuài.」Chī fàn de shíhou, nǎinai yìzhí wǎng wǒ wǎn lǐ jiā jiǎozi. Wǒ shuō gòu le gòu le, tā shuō:「Niánqīng rén yào duō chī.」Nà tiān de jiǎozi shì wǒ chī guò de zuì hǎochī de——búshì yīnwèi wèidào bǐ cāntīng de hǎo, ér shì yīnwèi měi yí gè jiǎozi lǐmiàn dōu yǒu nǎinai de xīnyì. Huí qù de lù shàng, péngyou shuō:「Nǎinai shuō nǐ xiàng tā yǐqián de línjū jiā de háizi.」
text_en: My Chinese friend invited me to her grandmother's home for dumplings. Grandma lives in a small house in the old city. The table was set with flour and filling. She wrapped dumplings while chatting with me. She asked where I'm from, how many people in my family, whether I like spicy food. She wrapped incredibly fast — in the time I made one, she'd made five. She laughed: 'Forty years of practice.' During the meal, she kept putting dumplings in my bowl. I said enough. She said: 'Young people should eat more.' Those were the best dumplings I've had — every one filled with grandma's care.

questions.q_zh: 奶奶为什么包饺子特别快？
questions.q_en: Why is grandma so fast at wrapping dumplings?
questions.explanation: 她笑了：'四十年了' — Grandma laughed and said forty years of practice, explaining her speed as the product of decades of repetition.

questions.q_zh: 「我」觉得饺子好吃的原因是什么？
questions.q_en: Why did 'I' think the dumplings were delicious?

options.text: 每个饺子里有奶奶的心意
options.pinyin: měi gè jiǎozi lǐ yǒu nǎinai de xīnyì

questions.explanation: 每一个都包着奶奶的关心 — each dumpling was 'filled with Grandma's care,' meaning the love behind the cooking made them taste special.

questions.q_zh: 吃饭的时候奶奶做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did grandma do during the meal?

options.pinyin: yìzhí wǎng wǒ wǎn lǐ jiā jiǎozi
options.text_en: kept putting dumplings in my bowl

questions.explanation: 吃饭的时候，她一直往我的碗里夹饺子 — Grandma kept placing dumplings into the narrator's bowl throughout the meal, a classic Chinese expression of hospitality.

title: The Library Note
text_zh: 我经常去学校图书馆看书。有一天，我在一本旧书里发现了一张纸条。上面用铅笔写着：「如果你也喜欢这本书，你可以在最后一页写一个字。」我翻到最后一页，上面已经有四个字：静、暖、光、风。每个字的笔迹都不一样，看起来是不同的人在不同的时间写的。我想了想，拿起笔写了一个「缘」字——因为这么多人看了同一本书，本身就是一种缘分。过了两个星期，我又去看了那本书。最后一页又多了一个字：「善」。我不知道是谁写的，但是觉得那个人一定很温柔。一本书把不认识的人连在了一起，每个人留下一个字，就像在安静地对话。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jīngcháng qù xuéxiào túshūguǎn kàn shū. Yǒu yì tiān, wǒ zài yì běn jiù shū lǐ fāxiàn le yì zhāng zhǐtiáo. Shàngmiàn yòng qiānbǐ xiězhe:「Rúguǒ nǐ yě xǐhuan zhè běn shū, nǐ kěyǐ zài zuìhòu yí yè xiě yí gè zì.」Wǒ fān dào zuìhòu yí yè, shàngmiàn yǐjīng yǒu sì gè zì: jìng, nuǎn, guāng, fēng. Měi gè zì de bǐjì dōu bù yíyàng, kàn qǐlái shì bùtóng de rén zài bùtóng de shíjiān xiě de. Wǒ xiǎng le xiǎng, ná qǐ bǐ xiě le yí gè「yuán」zì——yīnwèi zhème duō rén kàn le tóng yì běn shū, běnshēn jiùshì yì zhǒng yuánfèn. Guò le liǎng gè xīngqī, wǒ yòu qù kàn le nà běn shū. Zuìhòu yí yè yòu duō le yí gè zì:「shàn」. Wǒ bù zhīdào shì shéi xiě de, dànshì juéde nàge rén yídìng hěn wēnróu. Yì běn shū bǎ bú rènshi de rén lián zài le yìqǐ, měi gè rén liú xià yí gè zì, jiù xiàng zài ānjìng de duìhuà.
text_en: I often go to the school library. One day I found a note in an old book: 'If you also like this book, write one character on the last page.' I turned to the last page — four characters: quiet, warm, light, wind. Each in different handwriting. I thought, then wrote 'yuan' — fate — because so many people reading the same book is itself destiny. Two weeks later, the last page had one more character: 'kind.' I don't know who wrote it, but they must be gentle. One book connecting strangers, each leaving a single character — like a quiet conversation.

questions.q_zh: 纸条上写了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the note say?

options.text: 喜欢这本书就在最后一页写一个字
options.pinyin: xǐhuan zhè běn shū jiù zài zuìhòu yí yè xiě yí gè zì
options.text_en: write a character on the last page if you like the book

options.pinyin: qǐng bǎ shū huán gěi túshūguǎn

options.text: 我在这本书里藏了东西
options.pinyin: wǒ zài zhè běn shū lǐ cáng le dōngxi
options.text_en: I hid something in this book

questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「在最后一页写一个字」, indicating write a character on the last page if you like the book.

questions.q_zh: 自然环境对故事有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the natural setting affect the story?
questions.explanation: The library, the old book, and the hidden notes create a quiet, enclosed atmosphere — each reader's single character builds a sense of intimate, anonymous connection that shapes the story's mood.

questions.q_zh: 两个星期以后，最后一页多了什么字？
questions.q_en: What character appeared two weeks later?
questions.explanation: The key phrase is 「善」(kindness/goodness) — this single character appeared as a reply two weeks later.

title: The Night Shift Convenience Store
text_zh: 我家楼下有一家二十四小时的便利店。白天的时候，里面很忙，客人来来去去。但是晚上十一点以后，便利店变得很安静。夜班的店员是一个年轻的男生，总是戴着耳机。有一次半夜，我去买水，只有我一个客人。他帮我结账的时候，我看到他面前放着一本打开的书。我问他在看什么书。他说：「准备考试的书。白天上课，晚上在这里上班。客人少的时候就看书。」我说那一定很辛苦。他笑着说：「还好。半夜的便利店特别安静，比图书馆还安静。有时候一个小时都没有客人来，可以看很多页。」从那以后，我每次去便利店，都会跟他聊几句。他的考试后来通过了。他跟我说：「谢谢你每次来的时候跟我说话。夜班最难的不是累，是一个人太久了。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā lóu xià yǒu yì jiā èrshísì xiǎoshí de biànlì diàn. Báitiān de shíhou, lǐmiàn hěn máng, kèrén lái lái qù qù. Dànshì wǎnshang shíyī diǎn yǐhòu, biànlì diàn biàn de hěn ānjìng. Yèbān de diànyuán shì yí gè niánqīng de nánshēng, zǒngshì dàizhe ěrjī. Yǒu yí cì bànyè, wǒ qù mǎi shuǐ, zhǐyǒu wǒ yí gè kèrén. Tā bāng wǒ jiézhàng de shíhou, wǒ kàn dào tā miànqián fàngzhe yì běn dǎkāi de shū. Wǒ wèn tā zài kàn shénme shū. Tā shuō:「Zhǔnbèi kǎoshì de shū. Báitiān shàng kè, wǎnshang zài zhèlǐ shàng bān. Kèrén shǎo de shíhou jiù kàn shū.」Wǒ shuō nà yídìng hěn xīnkǔ. Tā xiàozhe shuō:「Hái hǎo. Bànyè de biànlì diàn tèbié ānjìng, bǐ túshūguǎn hái ānjìng. Yǒu shíhou yí gè xiǎoshí dōu méiyǒu kèrén lái, kěyǐ kàn hěn duō yè.」Cóng nà yǐhòu, wǒ měi cì qù biànlì diàn, dōu huì gēn tā liáo jǐ jù. Tā de kǎoshì hòulái tōngguò le. Tā gēn wǒ shuō:「Xièxie nǐ měi cì lái de shíhou gēn wǒ shuō huà. Yèbān zuì nán de búshì lèi, shì yí gè rén tài jiǔ le.」
text_en: There's a 24-hour convenience store below my building. After eleven at night, it goes quiet. The night clerk is a young man who always wears earphones. Once at midnight I went to buy water. While he rang me up, I noticed an open book. He said: 'Exam prep. I go to class during the day and work here at night. When there are few customers, I study.' I said that must be tough. He smiled: 'The midnight store is quieter than the library. Sometimes no customer comes for a whole hour.' He later passed his exam and told me: 'Thanks for talking to me. The hardest thing about night shifts isn't being tired — it's being alone too long.'

questions.explanation: The passage describes there's a 24-hour convenience store below my building, which reveals that feeling.

questions.q_zh: 他觉得半夜的便利店怎么样？
questions.q_en: How does he feel about the midnight convenience store?

options.text_en: quieter than the library, can study

questions.explanation: The words and actions (he smiled: 'the midnight store is quieter than the library) point to that.

questions.q_zh: 他说夜班最难的是什么？
questions.q_en: What did he say was hardest about night shifts?

options.pinyin: yí gè rén tài jiǔ le
